title: 2025管理类联考英语二真题试卷

questions.article:
There are many understandable reasons why you might find it difficult to ask for help when you need it. Psychologists have been interested in this
1
for decades, not least because people's widespread
2
to ask for help has led to some high-profile failures.
Asking for help takes
3
. It involves communicating a need on your part-there's something you can't do.
4
, you're broadcasting your own weaknesses which can be
5
. You might worry about coming across as incompetent. You might have
6
about losing control of whatever it is you're asking for help with.
7
someone starts to help, perhaps they'll take over, or get credit for your earlier efforts. Yet another
8
that might be worried about is being a nuisance or
9
the person you go to for help. If you struggle with low self-esteem, you might find it especially
difficult to
10
for help because you have the added worry of the other person
11
your request. You might see such refusals as implying something
12
about the status of your relationship with them. To
13
these difficulties, try to remind yourself that everyone needs help sometimes. Nobody knows everything and can do everything all by themselves. And while you might
14
coming across as incompetent, there's actually research that shows that advice-seekers are
15
as more competent, not less. Perhaps most encouraging of all is a paper from 2022 by researchers at Stanford University that involved a mix of contrived help-seeking interactions and asking people to
16
times they'd sought help in the past. The findings showed that help-seeker generally underestimate how
17
other people will be to help and how good it'll make the help-giver feel (for most people, having the chance to help someone is highly
18
). So, bear all this in mind the next time you need to ask for help
19
, take care over who you ask and when you ask them. And if someone can't help right now, avoid talking it personally. They might just be too
20
, or they might not feel confident about their ability to help.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. illusion 错觉；幻想	B. discussion 讨论C. tradition 传统	D. question 问题；课题【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文指出人们在需要帮助时感到难以开口的现象（you might find it difficult to ask for help when you need it），此处提到心理学家对此现象的长期兴趣。空格处需要一个名词总结心理学家研究的主题或问题，question（问题；课题）能够准确表达心理学家研究的对象，最符合语境。因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】心理学家几十年来一直对这个问题感兴趣。

【选项释义】A. reluctance 勉强；不情愿	B. ambition 雄心；抱负C. tendency 倾向；趋势	D. enthusiasm 热情；积极性【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到人们很难开口寻求帮助（difficult to ask for help）。根据语境，空格处需要一个能够表达“人们不愿意寻求帮助”的词汇，reluctance（勉强；不情愿）符合此含义，准确传达了人们对寻求帮助的消极态度。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】几十年来，心理学家一直对这个问题感兴趣，尤其是因为人们普遍不愿寻求帮助，导致了一些引人注目的失败。

【选项释义】A. attention 注意；关心	B. talent 天赋；才能C. courage 勇气；胆量	D. patience 耐心；耐性【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】下文提到它意味着你需要表达自己的需求——有些事情你不能做（communicating a need on your part—there’s something you can’t do），此处讲述了寻求帮助需要的心理素质，特别是需要克服某些内心障碍。空格处需要填入表达“请求帮助时所需的心理特质”的词汇，courage（勇气；胆量）最为合适，请求帮助通常意味着面对自己无法解决的问题，这需要勇气。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】请求帮助需要勇气。

【选项释义】A. At any time 随时	B. In other words 换句话说C. By all means 当然；一定	D. On the contrary 相反【考查点】词组辨析和上下文逻辑。【解题思路】上文提到请求帮助需要勇气（takes courage），并且涉及表达自己的需求（communicating a need on your part）。接下来的部分强调通过寻求帮助，你实际上是在展示自己的弱点（broadcasting your own weakness）。空格处用来解释和总结前面提到的内容。In other words（换句话说）用于引出对前述句子的进一步解释或重述，强调请求帮助所涉及的“弱点”这一方面。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合此处的逻辑关系。【句意】换句话说，你是在展示自己的弱点。

【选项释义】A. unrealistic 不切实际的；不现实的	B. deceptive 欺骗性的；迷惑性的C. tiresome 令人厌烦的；乏味的	D. uncomfortable 令人不安的；不舒服的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述的是寻求帮助时“暴露自己的弱点（broadcasting your own weakness）”可能带来的感受。这样的行为通常会使人感到不安或尴尬，因此空格处需要一个能够反映这种消极感受的词汇。uncomfortable（令人不安的；不舒服的）最符合语境。因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】换句话说，你是在展示自己的弱点，这可能会令人感到不安。

【选项释义】A. doubts 怀疑；疑虑 B. concerns 担忧；顾虑C. suggestions 建议 D. secrets 秘密【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句表达了在请求帮助时，人们可能会担心失去对所求助事情的掌控。此处需要填入一个能够表达“担忧”情绪的词，concerns（担忧；顾虑）最符合语境，能准确表达出人们在请求帮助时的顾虑。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】你可能会担心对你寻求帮助的事情失去控制。

【选项释义】A. Once 一旦；一经	B. Unless 除非C. Although 尽管；虽然	D. Before 在……之前【考查点】连词辨析和句内逻辑。【解题思路】本句讨论的是请求帮助可能导致的担忧，尤其是担心他人接管或获得功劳。此处需要一个连词引出假设条件，强调“某人一旦开始帮助你”可能发生的后果。Once（一旦）最符合语境，能够准确引出后续可能的情形。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合此处的逻辑关系。【句意】一旦有人开始帮忙，也许他们会接手，或者把你之前的努力归功于自己。

【选项释义】A. theory 理论；假设	B. choice 选择C. factor 因素；原因	D. context 上下文；背景【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到人们可能会担心失去控制（have concerns about losing control）。此处继续探讨人们在请求帮助时可能担心的问题，提到“成为麻烦（being a nuisance）”也是一个担忧的原因。空格处需要一个能够表示“原因或影响要素”的词，factor（因素）最符合语境，用于描述这种担忧的具体方面。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】另一个可能让你担忧的因素是自己成为一种麻烦。

【选项释义】A. overpraising 过分赞扬	B. outperforming 表现得比……更好C. reassessing 重新评估	D. inconveniencing 给……带来不便【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了请求帮助时可能的担忧，提到“成为麻烦（being a nuisance）”后进一步补充另一个相关的担忧。空格处需要一个动词表达“给他人带来不便”的意思。inconveniencing（给……带来不便）与“being a nuisance（成为麻烦）”语义相符。因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】另一个可能让你担忧的因素是自己成为一种麻烦，或者给你寻求帮助的人带来不便。

【选项释义】A. reach out 伸出手；寻求帮助	B. settle down 安定下来；平静下来C. turn over 翻转；移交	D. look back 回顾【考查点】词组辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述的是低自尊的人在寻求帮助时面临的困难。空格处需要一个短语表达“寻求帮助”的意思。reach out（寻求帮助）符合语境。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】如果你有低自尊的问题，你可能会发现寻求帮助特别困难。

【选项释义】A. declining 拒绝	B. considering 考虑C. criticizing 批评	D. evaluating 评估【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句讨论了低自尊的人在寻求帮助时的额外担忧。低自尊者往往会害怕自己的请求被拒绝，空格处需要一个表示“拒绝”的动词。declining（拒绝）最符合语境。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】如果你有低自尊的问题，你可能会发现寻求帮助特别困难，因为你还有额外的担忧，害怕对方会拒绝你的请求。

【选项释义】A. unnecessary 不必要的	B. negative 消极的；负面的C. strange 奇怪的；陌生的	D. impractical 不切实际的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】上文讨论了因低自尊导致的对请求帮助的担忧，尤其是对拒绝的敏感（worry of the other person declining your request）。由此可知，此处表达的是低自尊者可能会将拒绝视为对他们与他人关系的“负面暗示”。negative（消极的；负面的）最符合语境，准确传达了这种情绪反应。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】你可能会将这样的拒绝视为对你们关系状态的某种负面暗示。

【选项释义】A. explain 解释	B. identify 识别；确定C. predict 预测	D. overcome 克服【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句提供了解决低自尊导致的寻求帮助困难的方法，强调“试着提醒自己每个人有时都需要帮助（remind yourself that everyone needs help sometimes）”。此处需要一个能够表达“克服困难”的动词，overcome（克服）最符合语义，准确反映解决问题的目的。因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】为了克服这些困难，试着提醒自己每个人有时都需要帮助。

【选项释义】A. deny 否认	B. forget 忘记C. miss 错过；想念	D. fear 害怕【考查点】动词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到没有人什么都懂，什么都能独自完成（Nobody knows everything and can do everything all by themselves.），此处表示人们在请求帮助时害怕看起来无能。空格处需要一个动词表达“害怕表现得无能”的情绪。fear（害怕）最符合语境，准确反映出人们的心理障碍。因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】尽管你可能害怕表现得像个无能的人。

【选项释义】A. disguised 伪装	B. perceived 认为；感知C. followed 跟随	D. introduced 介绍【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句强调研究发现“寻求建议的人被视为更有能力，而不是相反（as more competent, not less）”。空格处需要一个能够表达“被认为”的动词，perceived（认为；感知）最符合语境。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】尽管你可能害怕表现得像个无能的人，但实际上有研究表明，寻求建议的人被认为更有能力，而不是相反。

【选项释义】A. recall 回忆	B. classify 分类C. analyze 分析	D. compare 比较【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了研究人员让参与者回忆他们“过去”寻求帮助的经历（times they’d sought help in the past）。空格处需要一个能够表达“回忆”动作的词。recall（回忆）最符合语境，准确表达了参与者被要求回忆过往经历的含义。因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】也许最令人鼓舞的是2022年斯坦福大学的研究人员发表的一篇论文，其中包括人为设计的寻求帮助的互动场景，以及让人们回忆他们过去寻求帮助的经历。

【选项释义】A. brave 勇敢的	B. disapproving 不赞成的C. willing 愿意的	D. hesitant 犹豫的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句提到“这会让帮助者感到多么好（how good it’ll make the help-giver feel）”，表明提供帮助是积极的、令人愉快的行为。空格处需要一个形容词来描述人们“愿意提供帮助”的情感。willing（愿意的）最符合语境，传达了帮助者愿意提供帮助的积极态度。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】研究结果表明，寻求帮助的人通常低估了其他人愿意帮助的程度，以及这将让帮助者感到多么愉快。

【选项释义】A. relaxing 轻松的	B. surprising 令人惊讶的C. rewarding 有益的，值得的	D. demanding 费力的，要求高的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句提到“为他人提供帮助让帮助者感到愉快（how good it’ll make the help-giver feel）”，空格处需要一个形容词来描述“有机会帮助别人（having the chance to help someone）”对大多数人来说是一件积极的事情。rewarding（有益的，值得的）表示提供帮助能带来回报和满足感。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】研究结果表明，寻求帮助的人通常低估了他人愿意提供帮助的程度，以及这将让帮助者感到多么愉快（对大多数人来说，有机会帮助别人是非常值得的）。

【选项释义】A. Thus 因此	B. Also 也C. Finally 最后	D. Instead 代替【考查点】副词辨析和上下文逻辑。【解题思路】上文提到“下次你需要寻求帮助时，请记住这些（bear all this in mind the next time you need to ask for help）”，接下来的“小心选择向谁求助以及何时向他们求助（take care over who you ask and when you ask them）”是对之前建议的补充。Also（也）能够自然地连接前后内容，表示“此外”或“还要”注意这一点。因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合此处的逻辑关系。【句意】此外，要小心选择向谁求助以及何时向他们求助。

【选项释义】A. polite 有礼貌的	B. proud 自豪的C. busy 忙碌的	D. lazy 懒惰的【考查点】形容词辨析和上文语义。【解题思路】上文提到如果某人现在无法提供帮助，不要把它当作个人的拒绝。接着列举了两种可能的原因，描述无法提供帮助的合理解释是busy（忙碌的）。因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】他们可能只是太忙了，或者他们可能对自己的能力没有信心。

questions.article:
Customers historically tipped people they assumed were earning most of their income via tips, such as restaurant servers earning less than the minimum wage. In the early 2010s, a wide range of businesses started processing purchase with ipads and other digital payment systems. These systems often prompted customers to tip for services that were not previously tipped.
Today’s tip request are not connected to the salary and service norms that used to determine when and how people tip. Customers in the past nearly always paid tips after receiving a service, such as at the conclusion of a restaurant meal, after getting a haircut once your pizza was delivered. That timing could reward high-quality service and give workers an incentive to provide it.
It’s becoming more common for tips to be requested beforehand. And new tipping technology may even automatically add tips.
The prevalence of digital payment device has made it easier to ask customers for a tip. That helps explain why tip request are creeping into new kinds of services. Customers now mountain see menus of suggested default options often well above 20% of what they owe. The amounts have risen from 10 or less in 1980 to is around the up or 2000 to 20 or higher today. This insurance is sometimes called application--the expection of ever-higher tip amounts.
Tipping has always been a certain source of income for worker in history tipped services, like restaurants, where the tipped minimum wage can be as low as 2.03. Tip creep and tipflation are now further supplmenting the income of many low-wage services workers.
Notably, tipping primarily benefits some of these workers, such as waiters, but not others, such as cooks and dishwashers. To ensure that all employees were paid fair wages, some restaurants banned tipping and increased prices, but this movement toward no-tipping services has largely fizzled out.
So, to increase employee wages without raising prices, more employers are succumbing to the temptations of tip creep and tipflation. However, many customers are frustrated because they feel they are being asked for too high of a tip, too often. And, as our research emphasizes, tipping now seems to be more coercive, less generous and often completely dissociated from service quality.
21.According to Paragraph 1, the practice of tipping _____.
22.Compared with tips in the past, today’s tips _____.
23.Tip request are creeping into new services as a result of _____.
24.The movement toward no-tipping service intend to _____.
25.It can be learned from the last paragraph that tipping _____.

options.A: was regarded as a sign of generosity

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 1, the practice of tipping in the U.S. ________.	根据第一段，在美国给小费________。A. was regarded as a sign of generosity	A. 被认为是慷慨的表现B. was considered essential for waiters	B. 被认为对服务员是必要的C. was a way of rewarding diligence	C. 是奖励勤奋的一种方式D. was optional in most businesses	D. 在大多数企业中是可有可无的【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the practice of tipping in the U.S.可以定位到文章第一段第一句U.S. customers historically tipped people they assumed were earning most of their income via tips, such as restaurant servers earning less than the minimum wage.（美国顾客过去通常会给那些他们认为收入大部分来自小费的人打赏，比如餐厅服务员，他们的工资低于最低工资。），说明过去给小费的做法是针对那些收入主要依赖小费的人，如餐厅服务员，小费是服务员收入的一个重要部分。因此B选项“被认为对服务员是必要的”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“被认为是慷慨的表现”，C选项“是奖励勤奋的一种方式”和D选项“在大多数企业中是可有可无的”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.C: have less to do with service quality

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Compared with tips in the past, today’s tips ________.	与过去的小费相比，现在的小费________。A. are paid much less frequently	A. 支付的频率要低得多B. are less often requested in advance	B. 较少提前要求C. have less to do with service quality	C. 与服务质量关系不大D. contribute less to workers’ income	D. 对员工收入的贡献较小【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词today’s tips可以定位到文章第二段第一句Today’s tip requests are often not connected to the salary and service norms that used to determine when and how people tip.（今天的小费要求通常与工资和服务标准无关，而这些标准过去是决定人们何时以及如何给小费的。），说明当今的小费与服务质量之间的关系较小。因此C选项“与服务质量关系不大”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“支付的频率要低得多”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B选项“较少提前要求”，文章提到现在越来越多的小费要求是在服务前提出，属于反向干扰；D选项“对员工收入的贡献较小”，文章提到“小费通胀”进一步补充了低薪服务行业工作人员的收入，属于反向干扰。

【选项释义】Tip requests are creeping into new kinds of services as a result of ________.	由于________，小费要求正逐渐成为新型服务的一部分。A. the advancement of technology	A. 技术进步B. the desire for income increase	B. 增加收入的愿望C. the diversification of business	C. 业务多样化D. the emergence of tipflation	D. 小费通胀的出现【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词new kinds of services可以定位到文章第四段第一、二句The prevalence of digital payment devices has made it easier to ask customers for a tip. That helps explain why tip requests are creeping into new kinds of services.（数字支付设备的普及使得向顾客要小费变得更加容易。这有助于解释为什么小费要求正逐渐进入新的服务种类。），说明小费要求进入新服务类型是由于数字支付设备的普及，也就是技术的进步。因此A选项“技术进步”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“增加收入的愿望”和C选项“业务多样化”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“小费通胀的出现”，文章提到“小费通胀”是指小费金额的不断提高，但它并没有直接与小费要求进入新服务类型相关联，属于出处错位。

【选项释义】The movement toward no-tipping services was intended to ________.	无小费服务运动的目的是________。A. promote consumption	A. 促进消费B. enrich income sources	B. 丰富收入来源C. maintain reasonable prices	C. 保持合理价格D. guarantee income fairness	D. 保证收入公平【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词movement toward no-tipping services可以定位到文章第六段第二句To ensure that all employees were paid fair wages, some restaurants banned tipping and increased prices, but this movement toward no-tipping services has largely fizzled out.（为了确保所有员工都能得到公平的工资，一些餐馆禁止给小费，并提高了价格，但这种无小费服务的运动基本上已经失败了。），说明一些餐厅实行无小费运动是为了确保所有员工都能获得公平工资，即保证收入公平。因此D选项“保证收入公平”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“促进消费”，B选项“丰富收入来源”和C选项“保持合理价格”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: is becoming a burden for customers
options.D: to the need to reduce price

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned from the last paragraph that tipping ________.	从最后一段可以看出，小费________。A. is becoming a burden for customers	A. 正在成为顾客的负担B. helps encourage quality service	B. 有助于鼓励优质服务C. is vital to business development	C. 对企业发展至关重要D. reflects the need to reduce prices	D. 反映了降低价格的需要【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the last paragraph可以定位到文章第七段第二、三句However, many customers are frustrated because they feel they are being asked for too high of a tip, too often. And, as our research emphasizes, tipping now seems to be more coercive, less generous and often completely dissociated from service quality.（然而，许多顾客感到沮丧，因为他们觉得他们被要求的小费太高，太频繁了。而且，正如我们的研究所强调的那样，现在给小费似乎更加强制性，不那么慷慨，而且往往与服务质量完全脱节。），说明现在的小费已不再与服务质量挂钩，且顾客感到小费要求有些强制性，常常被要求支付过高的小费，这意味着小费正在成为顾客的负担。因此A选项“正在成为顾客的负担”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“有助于鼓励优质服务”，文中明确提到现在的小费往往与服务质量完全脱节，属于反向干扰；C选项“对企业发展至关重要”和D选项“反映了降低价格的需要”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

questions.article:
When it was established, the National Health Service was visionary: offering high-quality, timely care to meet the dominant needs of the population it served. Nearly 75 years on, with the country facing very different health challenges, it is clear that model is out of date.
From life expectancy to cancer and infant mortality rates, we are lagging behind many of our peers. With more than 6.8 million on waitlists, healthcare is becoming increasingly inaccessible for those who cannot opt to pay for private treatment; and the cost of providing healthcare is increasingly squeezing out investment in other public services. The OBR now describes healthcare spending as the “largest – and most likely – source of long-term risk to fiscal sustainability”.
As demand for healthcare continues to grow, pressures on the workforce – which is already near breaking point – will only become more acute.
Many of the answers to the crisis in health and care are well rehearsed. We need to be much better at reducing and diverting demand on health services, rather than simply managing it. Much more needs to be invested in communities and primary care to reduce our reliance on hospitals. And capacity in social care needs to be greater, to support the growing number of people living with long-term conditions.
Yet despite two decades of strategies and a number of major health reforms, we have failed to make meaningful progress on any of these aims.
That is why Reform is launching a new programme of work entitled "Reimagining health", supported by ten former health ministers from across the three main political parties. Together, we are calling for a much more open and honest conversation about the future of health in the UK, and an “urgent rethink” of the hospital-centric model we retain.
This must begin with the question of how we maximise the health of the nation, rather than “fix” the NHS. It is estimated, for example, that healthcare accounts for only about 20% of health outcomes. Much more important are the places we live, work and socialise – yet there is no clear cross-government strategy for improving these social determinants of health. Worse, when policies like the national obesity strategy are scrapped, taxpayers are left with the hefty price tag of treating the illnesses, like diabetes, that result.
Reform wants to ask how power and resources should be distributed in our health system. What health functions should remain at the centre, and what should be devolved to local leaders, often responsible for services that create health, and with a much better understanding of the needs of their populations?
26.According to the first two paragraphs, the NHS
 _____.
27.One answer to the crisis in health and care is to _____.
28."Reimagining health" is aimed to _____.
29.To maximise the nations health,the author suggests _____.
30.It can be inferred that local leaders should _____.

options.C: can longly retain its current employes

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to the first two paragraphs, the NHS ________.	根据前两段，国家医疗服务体系________。A. is troubled by funding deficiencies	A. 受到资金不足的困扰B. can hardly satisfy people’s needs	B. 难以满足人们的需求C. can barely retain its current employees	C. 难以留住现有雇员D. is rivalled by private medical services	D. 与私人医疗服务相抗衡【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词NHS可以定位到文章第一段第二句Nearly 75 years on, with the country facing very different health challenges, it is clear that model is out of date.（近75年过去了，我国面临着截然不同的卫生挑战，显然这种模式已经过时了。），说明NHS的原有模式不再适应当前的健康需求。接着定位到文章第二段第二句With more than 6.8 million on waitlists, healthcare is becoming increasingly inaccessible for those who cannot opt to pay for private treatment; and the cost of providing healthcare is increasingly squeezing out investment in other public services.（等待名单上有超过680万人，对于那些无法选择支付私人治疗费用的人来说，医疗保健变得越来越难以获得；提供医疗保健的成本越来越多地挤占了对其他公共服务的投资。），说明NHS无法满足人们的需求，特别是在服务可获得性和资金方面。由此可知，NHS难以满足公众需求。因此B选项“难以满足人们的需求”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“受到资金不足的困扰”只在第二段中部分提及，在第一段中没有提及，不是文章重点讨论的问题，属于以偏概全；C选项“难以留住现有雇员”和D选项“与私人医疗服务相抗衡”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: lighten the burden on social care

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】One answer to the crisis in health and care is to ________.	解决医疗保健危机的一个办法是________。A. boost the efficiency of hospitals	A. 提高医院的效率B. lighten the burden on social care	B. 减轻社会医疗负担C. increase resources for primary care	C. 增加基础保健的资源D. reduce the pressure on communities	D. 减轻社区压力【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词answer to the crisis可以定位到文章第三段第三句Much more needs to be invested in communities and primary care to reduce our reliance on hospitals.（需要在社区和基础保健方面投入更多资金，以减少我们对医院的依赖。），说明解决医疗保健危机的其中一个办法是增加对基础保健和社区的投资。因此C选项“增加基础保健的资源”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“提高医院的效率”，B选项“减轻社会医疗负担”和D选项“减轻社区压力”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】“Reimagining health” is aimed to ________.	“重塑健康”旨在________。A. reinforce hospital management	A. 加强医院管理B. readjust healthcare regulations	B. 重新调整医疗法规C. restructure the health system	C. 重构医疗体系D. resume suspended health reforms	D. 恢复暂停的医疗改革【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Reimagining health可以定位到文章第四段第二、三句That is why Reform is launching a new programme of work entitled “Reimagining health”, supported by ten former health ministers from across the three main political parties. Together, we are calling for a much more open and honest conversation about the future of health in the UK, and an “urgent rethink” of the hospital-centric model we retain.（这就是为什么改革委员会正在启动一项名为“重塑健康”的新工作计划，该计划得到了来自三个主要政党的十位前卫生部长的支持。我们共同呼吁就英国健康的未来进行更加开放和诚实的对话，并对我们保留的以医院为中心的模式进行“紧急反思”。），说明“重塑健康”的目标是重构现有的健康系统，改变当前的医疗模式，而不是单纯的调整规定或恢复旧的改革。因此C选项“重构医疗体系”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“加强医院管理”，文章强调的是对医院为中心的模式进行反思和改变，而不是单纯地加强医院管理，属于曲解原文；B选项“重新调整医疗法规”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“恢复暂停的医疗改革”，文章指出“重塑健康”不是恢复停滞的改革，而是启动新的计划，属于反向干扰。

options.B: paying due attention to social factors
options.D: enhancing the quality of health care

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】To maximize the nations’ health, the author suggests ________.	为了最大限度地提高国民的健康水平，作者建议________。A. introducing relevant taxation policies	A. 引入相关的税收政策B. paying due attention to social factors	B. 充分重视社会因素C. reevaluating major health outcomes	C. 重新评估主要的健康结果D. enhancing the quality of healthcare	D. 提高医疗质量【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词maximize the nations’ health可以定位到文章第五段第一、二、三句This must begin with the question of how we maximize the health of the nation, rather than “fix” the NHS. It is estimated, for example, that healthcare accounts for only about 20% of health outcomes. Much more important are the places we live, work and socialize—yet there is no clear cross-government strategy for improving these social determinants of health.（这必须从我们如何最大化国民健康的问题开始，而不是“修复”NHS。例如，据估计，医疗保健仅占健康结果的20%左右。更重要的是我们生活、工作和社交的场所，但目前还没有明确的跨政府战略来改善这些健康的社会决定因素。），说明要最大化国家的健康，必须关注社会因素而不仅仅是医疗保健。因此B选项“充分重视社会因素”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“引入相关的税收政策”和C选项“重新评估主要的健康结果”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“提高医疗质量”，文中指出，健康的改善更多依赖于社会因素，而非单纯提高医疗质量，属于以偏概全。

options.B: develop a stronger sense of responibility
options.C: play a bigger role in the health system

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be inferred that local leaders should ________.	可以推断地方领导应该________。A. exercise their power more reasonably	A. 更合理地行使权力B. develop a stronger sense of responsibility	B. 增强责任感C. play a bigger role in the health system	C. 在卫生系统中发挥更大的作用D. understand people’s health needs better	D. 更好地了解人们的健康需求【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词local leaders可以定位到文章第六段最后一句What health functions should remain at the centre, and what should be devolved to local leaders, often responsible for services that create health, and with a much better understanding of the needs of their populations?（哪些保健职能应留在中心，哪些职能应下放给地方领导人，这些领导人往往负责提供创造保健的服务，并对其人口的需求有更好的了解？），说明地方领导应该在卫生系统中发挥更大的作用，特别是在理解和解决本地卫生问题方面。因此C选项“在卫生系统中发挥更大的作用”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“更合理地行使权力”和B选项“增强责任感”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“更好地了解人们的健康需求”，虽然文章提到理解人们的健康需求很重要，但文中强调的重点是地方领导需要在健康系统中发挥更大的作用，而不仅仅是理解需求，属于以偏概全。

questions.article:
These heat action plans, or HAPs, have been proliferating in India in the past few years. In general, an HAP spells out when and how officials should issue heat warnings and alert hospitals and other institutions. Nagpur’s plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside “cold wards” in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days.
But implementation of existing HAPs has been uneven, according to a March report from the Centre for Policy Research (CPR), a prominent Indian think tank. It reviewed 37 plans adopted by cities, states, or administrative districts.
Many lack adequate funding, it found. And their triggering thresholds often are not customized to the local climate, says Dileep Mavalankar, director of the Indian Institute of Public Health, who has been closely involved in Ahmedabad’s HAP. In some areas, high daytime temperatures alone might serve as an adequate trigger for alerts. Ahmedabad, for example, set its threshold for initial alerts at 41°C based on data showing that heat-related deaths began to climb at that point. But in other places, nighttime temperatures or humidity might be as important a gauge of risk as daytime highs.
Mumbai’s April heat stroke deaths highlighted the need for more nuanced and localized warnings, researchers say. That day’s high temperature of roughly 36°C was 1°C shy of the heat wave alert threshold for coastal cities set by national meteorological authorities. But the effects of the heat were amplified by humidity—an often neglected factor in heat alert systems—and the lack of shade at the late-morning outdoor ceremony. Ironically, the state of Maharashtra, which includes Mumbai, had adopted its own HAP just 2 months before the tragedy. It advised shifting outdoor events to early mornings on hot days.
To help improve HAPs, Kotharkar’s team is working on a model plan that outlines best practices and could be adapted to local conditions. Among other things, she says, all cities should create a vulnerability map to help focus responses on the populations most at risk. (The CPR study found that only two of the 37 HAPs it examined identified the most vulnerable populations.)
Such mapping doesn’t need to be complex, Kotharkar says. “A useful map can be created by looking at even a few key parameters.” For example, neighborhoods with a large elderly population or informal dwellings that cope poorly with heat could get special warnings or be bolstered with cooling centers. The Nagpur project has already created a risk and vulnerability map, which enabled Kotharkar to tell officials which neighborhoods to focus on in the event of a heat wave this summer.
HAPs shouldn’t just include short-term emergency responses, researchers say, but also recommend medium- to long-term measures that could make communities cooler. In Nagpur, for example, Kotharkar’s team has been able to advise city officials about where to plant trees to provide shade. HAPs could also guide efforts to retrofit homes or tweak building regulations. “Reducing deaths [in an emergency] is good target to have, but it’s the lowest [target],” Singh says.
31.According to Paragraph 1,Nagpur's plan proposes measures to _____.
32. One problem with existing HAPs is that they _____.
33. Mumbai's case shows that India's heat alert systems need to _____.
34.Kotharkar holds that a vulnerability map can help _____.
35.According to the last paragraph, researchers believe that HAPs should _____.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 1, Nagpur’s plan proposes measures to ________.	根据第一段，那格浦尔计划提出的措施是为了________。A. tackle extreme weather	A. 应对极端天气B. ensure construction quality	B. 确保建筑质量C. monitor emergency warnings	C. 监控紧急警报D. address excessive workloads	D. 解决工作量过大的问题【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Nagpur’s plan可以定位到文章第一段最后一句Nagpur’s plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside “cold wards” in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days.（例如，那格浦尔的计划要求医院在夏季留出“冷却病房”来治疗中暑患者，并建议建筑商在非常炎热的日子里让建筑工人休息一下。），说明那格浦尔计划提出了应对极端天气的措施，如设立冷却病房和为工人提供休息。因此A选项“应对极端天气”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“确保建筑质量”和D选项“解决工作量过大的问题”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“监控紧急警报”，虽然文章提到了预警系统，但重点在于如何帮助应对热浪，而不是单纯讨论监控预警，属于出处错位。

options.A: prove too costly to be implemented
options.C: give delayed responses to heat waves

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】One problem with existing HAPs is that they ________.	现有高温行动计划的一个问题是它们________。A. prove too costly to be implemented	A. 实施成本太高B. lack localized alert-issuing criteria	B. 缺乏本地化的警报发布标准C. give delayed responses to heat waves	C. 对热浪的反应迟缓D. keep hospitals under great pressure	D. 使医院承受巨大压力【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词problem with existing HAPs可以定位到文章第二段第三、四句And their triggering thresholds often are not customized to the local climate. In some areas, high daytime temperatures alone might serve as an adequate trigger for alerts.（而且它们的触发阈值往往不是根据当地气候定制的。在一些地区，仅白天的高温就足以触发警报。），说明现有的高温行动计划存在缺乏本地化警报发布标准的问题，触发阈值没有根据当地气候调整。因此B选项“缺乏本地化的警报发布标准”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“实施成本太高”，C选项“对热浪的反应迟缓”和D选项“使医院承受巨大压力”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: take subtle weather changes into account
options.D: draw further support from local authorities

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Mumbai’s case shows that India’s heat alert systems need to ________.	孟买的案例表明，印度的高温预警系统需要________。A. include other factors besides temperature	A. 包括温度以外的其他因素B. take subtle weather changes into account	B. 考虑到微妙的天气变化C. prioritize potentially disastrous heat waves	C. 优先考虑潜在的灾难性热浪D. draw further support from local authorities	D. 吸引地方当局的进一步支持【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Mumbai’s case可以定位到文章第三段第一句Mumbai’s April heat stroke deaths highlighted the need for more nuanced and localized warnings, researchers say.（研究人员说，孟买4月份的中暑死亡突显出需要更细致和局部的预警。）和第三句But the effects of the heat were amplified by humidity—an often neglected factor in heat alert systems—and the lack of shade at the late-morning outdoor ceremony.（但是高温的影响被湿度放大了——在高温警报系统中，湿度是一个经常被忽视的因素——而且在上午晚些时候的户外活动缺乏遮阳。），说明孟买的案例强调了高温警报系统应当考虑湿度这一通常被忽视的因素，而不仅仅是温度。因此A选项“包括温度以外的其他因素”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“考虑到微妙的天气变化”，C选项“优先考虑潜在的灾难性热浪”和D选项“吸引地方当局的进一步支持”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: prevent the harm of high humidity
options.D: make relief plans for heat-stricken people

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Kotharkar holds that a vulnerability map can help ________.	科塔卡尔认为，脆弱性地图可以帮助________。A. prevent the harm of high humidity	A. 防止高湿度带来的危害B. target areas needing special attention	B. 锁定需要特别关注的地区C. expand the Nagpur projects coverage	C. 扩大那格浦尔项目的覆盖范围D. make relief plans for heat-stricken people	D. 为受高温影响的人们制定救援计划【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词vulnerability map可以定位到文章第四段最后一句all cities should create a vulnerability map to help focus responses on the populations most at risk（所有城市都应该创建一个脆弱性地图，以帮助将应对措施集中在风险最大的人群上）和第五段第三句For example, neighborhoods with a large elderly population or informal dwellings that cope poorly with heat could get special warnings or be bolstered with cooling centers.（例如，老年人口较多的社区或供暖能力较差的非正式住宅可能会得到特别警告，或者配备冷却中心。），说明脆弱性地图的作用是帮助识别需要特殊关注的区域，从而采取相应措施。因此B选项“锁定需要特别关注的地区”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“防止高湿度带来的危害”，文中提到湿度对热浪影响的重要性，但与脆弱性地图无直接关联，属于出处错位；C选项“扩大那格浦尔项目的覆盖范围”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“为受高温影响的人们制定救援计划”，文章指出脆弱性地图的目的是标记高风险区域，而非直接制定救援计划，属于过度推断。

options.D: serve a broader range of purposes

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to the last paragraph, researchers suggest that HAPs should ________.	根据最后一段，研究人员建议高温津贴应该________。A. focus more on heatstroke treatment	A. 更多地关注中暑治疗B. invite wider public participation	B. 邀请更多公众参与C. apply for more government grants	C. 申请更多的政府拨款D. serve a broader range of purposes	D. 服务于更广泛的目的【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词researchers suggest可以定位到文章第六段段第一句HAPs shouldn’t just include short-term emergency responses, researchers say, but also recommend medium- to long-term measures that could make communities cooler.（研究人员说，高温行动计划不应该只包括短期应急响应，还应该建议中长期措施，让社区变得更凉爽。），说明研究人员建议高温行动计划不仅要有短期目标，还要制定中长期措施，使它们具备更广泛的功能和目标。因此D选项“服务于更广泛的目的”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“更多地关注中暑治疗”是短期紧急响应的一部分，不是研究人员重点建议的内容，属于以偏概全；B选项“邀请更多公众参与”和C选项“申请更多的政府拨款”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

questions.article:
Navigating beyond the organised pavements and parks of our urban spaces, desire paths are the unofficial footprints of a community, revealing the unspoken preferences, shared shortcuts and collective choices of humans. Often appearing as trodden dirt tracks through otherwise neat green spaces, these routes of collective disobedience cut corners, bisect lawns and traverse hills, representing the natural capability of people (and animals) to go from point A to point B most effectively.
Urban planners interpret desire paths as more than just convenient shortcuts; they offer valuable insights into the dynamics between planning and behaviour. Ohio State University allowed its students to navigate the Oval, a lawn in the centre of campus, freely, then proceeded to pave the desire paths, creating a web of effective routes students had established.
Yet, reluctance persists among other planners to integrate desire paths into formal plans, citing concerns about safety, environmental impact, or primarily, aesthetics. AReddit webpagedevoted to the phenomenon, boasting nearly 50,000 members, showcases images of local desire paths adorned with signs instructing pedestrians to adhere to designated walkways, underscoring the rebellious nature inherent in these human-made tracks. This clash highlights an ongoing struggle between the organic, user-driven evolution of public spaces and the desire for a visually curated and controlled urban environment.
The Wickquasgeck Trail is an example of a historical desire path, created by Native Americans to traverse the forests of Manhattan and move between settlements quickly. This trail, when Dutch colonists arrived, was widened and made into one of the main trade roads across the island, known at the time as de Heere Straat, or Gentlemen’s Street. Following the British assumption of control in New York, the street was renamed Broadway. Notably, Broadway stands out as one of the few areas in NYC that defies the grid-based system applied to the rest of the city, cutting a diagonal across parts of the city.
In online spaces, desire paths have sparked a fascination that can approach obsession, with the Reddit page serving as a hub. Contributors offer a wide array of stories, from little-known new shortcuts to long-established alternate routes.
Animal desire paths, such as ducks forging trails through frozen ponds or dogs carving direct routes in gardens, highlight the adaptability of these trails in both human and animal experiences. As desire paths criss-cross through both physical and virtual landscapes, they stand as a testament to the collective insistence on forging unconventional routes and embracing the spirit of communal choice.
36.According to Paragraph 1, desire paths are a result of___.
37. It can be inferred that Ohio State University___.
38.The images on the Reddit webpage reflect___.
39.The example of the Wickquasgeck Trail illustrates____.
40. It can be learned from the last paragraph that desire paths___.

options.A: the curiosity to explore surrounding hills
options.B: the necessity to preserve green spaces
options.D: the wish to find comfort in solitude

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 1, desire paths are a result of ________.	根据第一段，“愿望路径”源于________。A. the curiosity to explore surrounding hills	A. 探索周围山丘的好奇心B. the necessity to preserve green spaces	B. 保护绿地的必要性C. the tendency to pursue convenience	C. 追求方便的倾向D. the wish to find comfort in solitude	D. 希望在孤独中找到慰藉【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词desire paths可以定位到文章第一段第一句desire paths are the unofficial footprints of a community, revealing the unspoken preferences, shared shortcuts and collective choices of humans（愿望路径是一个社区的非正式足迹，揭示了人类未说出口的偏好、共享的捷径和集体的选择），说明愿望路径的产生源于人类对便利和高效路径的追求。因此C选项“追求方便的倾向”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“探索周围山丘的好奇心”，文中提到欲望路径可能会翻越小丘，而不是人们因探索小丘的好奇心而开辟这些路径，属于曲解原文；B选项“保护绿地的必要性”和D选项“希望在孤独中找到慰藉”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: intends to improve its desire paths
options.B: leads in the research on desire paths
options.C: guides the creation ofits desire paths
options.D: takes a positive view of desire paths

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be inferred that Ohio State University ________.	可以推断俄亥俄州立大学________。A. intends to improve its desire paths	A. 打算改善其愿望路径B. leads in the research on desire paths	B. 在愿望路径的研究中处于领先地位C. guides the creation of its desire paths	C. 指导愿望路径的创建D. takes a positive view of desire paths	D. 积极看待愿望路径【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Ohio State University可以定位到文章第二段最后一句Ohio State University allowed its students to navigate the Oval, a lawn in the center of campus, freely, then proceeded to pave the desire paths, creating a web of effective routes students had established.（俄亥俄州立大学允许学生在校园中心的椭圆草坪自由穿行，然后开始铺设自己想要的道路，创造出学生们建立的有效路线网络。），说明学校认可并采纳了这些学生实际选择的路径，并将其铺设为正式道路，表现出一种积极接纳愿望路径的态度。因此D选项“积极看待愿望路径”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“打算改善其愿望路径”和B选项“在愿望路径的研究中处于领先地位”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“指导愿望路径的创建”，文中提到学校允许学生自由穿行，说明路径的创建是学生的行为，而非学校引导，属于反向干扰。

options.A: conflicting opinions on the use of desire paths
options.B: the call to upgrade the designing of public spaces
options.C: the demand for proper planning of desire paths
options.D: growing concems over the loss of public spaces

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The images on the Reddit webpage reflect ________.	Reddit网页上的图片反映了________。A. conflicting opinions on the use of desire paths	A. 对愿望路径的使用意见不一B. the call to upgrade the designing of public spaces	B. 提升公共空间设计的呼声C. the demand for proper planning of desire paths	C. 要求对愿望路径进行合理规划D. growing concerns over the loss of public spaces	D. 人们越来越担心公共空间的消失【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Reddit webpage可以定位到文章第三段第二、三句A Reddit webpage devoted to the phenomenon, boasting nearly 50,000 members, showcases images of local desire paths adorned with signs instructing pedestrians to adhere to designated walkways, underscoring the rebellious nature inherent in these human-made tracks. This clash highlights an ongoing struggle between the organic, user-driven evolution of public spaces and the desire for a visually curated and controlled urban environment.（Reddit网站上有一个专门讨论这一现象的网页，拥有近5万名成员，展示了一些当地的愿望路径的图片，上面装饰着指示行人遵守指定人行道的标志，强调了这些人造路径固有的叛逆本质。这种冲突凸显了公共空间有机的、用户驱动的演变与对视觉策划和控制的城市环境的渴望之间的持续斗争。），说明持续斗争反映了关于愿望路径使用的不同意见：一种是用户驱动的自由选择，另一种是对受控环境的追求。因此A选项“对愿望路径的使用意见不一”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“提升公共空间设计的呼声”，C选项“要求对愿望路径进行合理规划”和D选项“人们越来越担心公共空间的消失”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: the growth of New York City
options.B: the Dutch origin of desire paths

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The example of the Wickquasgeck Trail illustrates ________.	Wickquasgeck小路的例子说明了________。A. the growth of New York City	A. 纽约市的发展B. the Dutch origin of desire paths	B. 愿望路径起源于荷兰C. the importance of urban planning	C. 城市规划的重要性D. the recognition of desire paths	D. 对愿望路径的认识【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Wickquasgeck Trail可以定位到文章第四段第一、二、三句The Wickquasgeck Trail is an example of a historical desire path, created by Native Americans to cross the forests of Manhattan and move between settlements quickly. This trail, when Dutch colonists arrived, was widened and made into one of the main trade roads across the island, known at the time as de Heere Straat, or Gentlemen’s Street. Following the British assumption of control in New York, the street was renamed Broadway.（Wickquasgeck小路是一条历史上的愿望路径，由美洲原住民创建，穿过曼哈顿的森林，在定居点之间快速移动。荷兰殖民者到达后，这条小径被拓宽，成为横穿全岛的主要贸易道路之一，当时被称为de Heere Straat，或绅士街。在英国控制纽约之后，这条街被重新命名为百老汇。），说明本段描述了Wickquasgeck小路作为愿望路径的一段演变史，从美洲原住民到荷兰殖民者再到英国的使用，逐渐发展成为今天的百老汇，体现了对愿望路径的历史认可和应用。因此D选项“对愿望路径的认识”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“纽约市的发展”，虽然提到百老汇的历史演变与纽约的发展有关，但文中重点在于描述Wickquasgeck小路如何被逐步认可和利用，而非专注于纽约市整体的发展，属于曲解原文；B选项“愿望路径起源于荷兰”，文中提到Wickquasgeck小路最早是由美洲原住民创建的，而非源于荷兰殖民者，属于曲解原文；C选项“城市规划的重要性”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: are crucial to humans' mental wellbeing
options.C: are a human imitation of animal behaviour
options.D: show a shared trait in humans and animals

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned from the last paragraph that desire paths ________.	从最后一段可以得知，愿望路径________。A. reveal humans’ deep respect for nature	A. 揭示了人类对自然的深深敬意B. are crucial to humans’ mental well-being	B. 对人类的心理健康至关重要C. are a human imitation of animal behavior	C. 是人类对动物行为的模仿D. show a shared trait in humans and animals	D. 显示了人类和动物的共同特征【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the last paragraph可以定位到文章最后一段第一句Animal desire paths, such as ducks forging trails through frozen ponds or dogs carving direct routes in gardens, highlight the adaptability of these trails in both human and animal experiences.（动物的愿望路径，如鸭子在结冰的池塘里开辟小径，狗在花园里开辟直线路线，突出了这些小径在人类和动物体验中的适应性。），说明愿望路径不仅是人类的特性，动物也会通过类似的行为展现适应性。因此D选项“显示了人类和动物的共同特征”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“揭示了人类对自然的深深敬意”和B选项“对人类的心理健康至关重要”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“是人类对动物行为的模仿”，文中提到人类和动物都有愿望路径，但并未说人类是在模仿动物的行为，而是两者展现了共同的特性，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
Everyone wants to be that person--the one who looks at the same information as everyone else, but who sees a fresh, innovative solution. However, it takes more than simply having a good idea. How you share it is as important as the suggestion itself.
Why? Because writing a new script--literally or figuratively-means that other team members will have to adapt to something new. So whether you're suggesting a (seemingly) benign change like streamlining outdated protocol, or a bigger change like adding anhour to each workday so people can leave early on Fridays, you're asking others to reimagine their workflow or schedule. Not to mention, if the process your scrapping is one someone else suggested, there's the possibility ofhurt feelings.
To gain buy-in on an innovative, new idea, follow these five steps:
41._______
Great ideas don't stand alone. In other words, you can't mention your suggestion once andexpect it to be adopted. To see a change, you'll needto champion your plan and sell its merits. In addition, you need to be willing to stand up to scrutiny and criticism and be prepared to explain your innovation in different ways for various audiences.
42.________
Sometimes it makes sense to go to your boss first. But other times, it's useful to build a coalition among your co-workers or other stakeholders. When it works, it works greatbecause you're ready for your stubbom supervisor's pushback with answers like, “Actually, I cormected with a few people in our tech department to discuss how much time these kinds of website updates would take, andthey suggested they have the bandwidth."
43._________
One of the biggest baniers to gaining buy-in occurs when the owner of anidea is viewed as
argumentative, defensive, or close-minded. Because, let's behonest: No one likes a know-it-all. So, if people disagree with you, don't be indignant. Instead, listen to their concems fully, try to understand their perspective, andinclude their concems (and possible remedies) in future discussions.
So. instead of saving.“Martha. our current slogan is confusing and should be updated," you could try, “Martha raises a great point that our current slogan has a long history for our stakeholders, but I wonder if we might able to brainstomm a tagline that could build onthat-and be clearer for new customers."
44._________
New ideas are the grandchildren of old ones. In other words, don't throw old solutions under the bus to make your improvement stand out. Remember that in light of whatever the problem the old system solved--or, maybe, has failed to solve in recent memory--it was a great idea at the time. Appreciating the older contributions as you suggest future innovations helps bolster the credibility of your idea.
45.__________
When pitching a new idea, it 's important use the language of abundance instead ofthe language of deficit. Instead of saying what is wrong, broken, or suboptimal, talk about what is right, fixable, or ideal. For example, try, “I can see lots of applications for this new approach" rather than, “This innovation is the only way.” Be optimistic but realistic, andyou will stand out.

options.G: Be humble

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. Stay positive	A. 保持积极B. Respect the past	B. 尊重过去C. Use channels	C. 利用渠道D. Give it time	D. 给它时间E. Invite resistance	E. 邀请阻力F. Be a salesman	F. 做个销售G. Be humble	G. 保持谦虚【解题思路】本段强调了推广和推销自己想法的重要性。根据you’ll need to champion your plan and sell its merits（你需要捍卫你的计划，并推销它的优点）可以推断出，提出的想法不能仅仅依赖一次陈述，而需要积极展示其优点，接受批评并根据不同的听众调整表达方式。就像销售员需要反复推销商品一样，这与销售员不断推销、应对质疑和调整策略的过程十分相似。因此F选项“做个销售”正确。

options.G: Be humble

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段强调了通过不同的渠道（如同事或其他利益相关者）来推动想法或解决问题的重要性。根据it’s useful to build a coalition among your co-workers or other stakeholders（在你的同事或其他利益相关者之间建立一个联盟是有用的）可以推断出，有时通过与同事或相关方建立联盟，比直接向上级汇报更有效。这也表明，利用不同的沟通渠道可以帮助更好地准备应对上司的反对，增强提案的说服力。因此C选项“利用渠道”正确。

options.G: Be humble

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段强调了在面对反对意见时保持谦虚和开放的重要性。根据if people disagree with you, don’t be indignant. Instead, listen to their concerns fully（如果别人不同意你的观点，不要愤愤不平。相反，充分倾听他们的担忧）可以推断出，当别人不同意时，应该避免自负，而是要耐心倾听对方的担忧，试图理解他们的视角。通过这种谦虚的态度，能够更好地推动讨论，并考虑他人的意见和建议，从而促进合作。因此G选项“保持谦虚”正确。

options.G: Be humble

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段强调了在提出新想法时要尊重和认可旧有解决方案的重要性。根据don’t throw old solutions under the bus to make your improvement stand out（不要为了让你的改进脱颖而出而抛弃旧的解决方案）可以推断出，在推销新想法时，不应贬低旧的方案，而应理解并尊重它们在当时所做出的贡献。通过尊重过去的努力，可以增强自己新提案的可信度，体现对历史的尊重。因此B选项“尊重过去”正确。

options.G: Be humble

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段强调了在提出新想法时保持积极、乐观的态度。根据it’s important to use the language of abundance instead of the language of deficit（重要的是使用富足的语言而不是缺乏的语言）可以推断出，在推销新方案时，应该专注于强调其潜力和积极方面，而不是批评现有问题。通过使用“lots of applications（很多应用）”而非“the only way（唯一的方法）”，可以展现一种更具建设性和乐观的态度，这样能帮助自己在讨论中脱颖而出。因此A选项“保持积极”正确。

questions.article: You know the moment the conversation shows,then there's a pause. It's awkward, and so awkward that some people will panic and say anything. Do we all find such silences so stressful? Researchers analyzed the frequency and impact of gaps greater than 2 seconds during conversations including an overview of previous studies which indicate that the fear of awkward silences can be so extreme that people avoid talking to strangers, even though doing so this likely to be an enjoyable experience.

小作文是要写一封邮件邀请朋友来参加活动。
Suppose you are planning a short play based on a classic Chinese novel.
Write your friend John an email to
1) introduce the play and
2) invite him to take part in it
You should write about 100 words on the ANSWER SHEET.
Do not use your own name. Use "Li Ming” instead.(10 points)

大作文是一个柱状图，某社区老年人的日常生活活动，让描述图表，写自己的评价
Directions:
Write an essay based on the chart below. In your essay, you should
1) describle and interpret the chart, and
2) give your comments.
Write your answer in about 150 words on the ANSWER SHEET.
(15 points)

Your social life is defined as "the activities you do with other people, for pleasure, when you are not working". It's important to have a social life, but what's right for one person won’t be right for another. Some of us feel energised by spending lots of time with others, （ 1 ） some of us may feel drained, even if it’s doing something we enjoy.
This is why finding a （ 2 ） in your social life is key. Spending too much time on your own, not （ 3 ） others, can make you feel lonely and （ 4 ）. Loneliness is known to impact on your mental health and （ 5 ） a low mood. Anyone can feel lonely at any time. This might be especially true if, （ 6 ） you are working from home and you are （ 7 ）on the social conversations that happen in the office. Other life changes also （ 8 ） periods of loneliness too, such as retirement, changing a job or becoming a parent.
It’s important to recognize feelings or loneliness. There are ways to （ 9 ） a social life. But it be overwhelming （ 10 ）. You can then find groups and activities related to those where you will be able to meet （ 11 ） people. There are groups aimed at new parents, at those who want to （ 12 ） a new sport for the first time or networking events for those in the same profession to meet up and （ 13 ） ideas.
On the other hand, it is （ 14 ）possible to have too much of a social life. If you feel like you’re always doing something and there is never any （ 15 ） in your calendar for downtime, you could suffer social burnout or social （ 16 ）. We all have our own social limit and it's important to recognize when you're feeling like it's all too much. Low mood, low energy, irritability and trouble sleeping could all be （ 17 ） of poor social health. Make sure you （ 18 ） some time in your diary when you're （ 19 ） for socialising and use this time to relax, （ 20 ） and recover.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. because因为	B. unless除非C. whereas但是	D. until直到……为止【答案】C【考查点】连词辨析和句内逻辑。【解题思路】本句描述了两种不同的经历，通过与他人在一起而感到充满活力与即使在做自己喜欢的事情时也感到疲惫，此处存在比较或对比的逻辑关系，表明了两者之间的差异。whereas用以比较或对比两个事实，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不能表示对比关系。【句意】我们中的一些人会因为花很多时间和别人在一起而感到精力充沛，然而我们中的另一些人可能会感到筋疲力尽，即使是在做我们喜欢的事情。

【选项释义】A. contrast 对比 B. balance 平衡C. link 联系 D. gap 间隙【答案】B【考查点】名词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到了一些人在与他人相处时感到充满活力，而另一些人可能感到疲惫，所以空格处应表示在社交生活中寻找平衡是关键。balance强调了需要在社交活动和独处时间之间找到一种合适的比例，以满足不同人的需求和偏好，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】这就是为什么在你的社交生活中找到平衡是关键。

【选项释义】A. seeing 见到 B. pleasing 使满意；使愉快C. judging 判断 D. teaching 讲授；教授【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了太长时间不与别人交往见面会让人感到孤单。seeing强调了与他人直接见面和互动的必要性，可以避免孤独感的产生，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】花太多时间独处，不与别人见面，会让你感到孤独。

【选项释义】A. misguided 搞错的 B. surprised 惊奇的；惊讶的C. spoiled 惯坏的；宠坏的 D. disconnected 断开的；无关联的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了太长时间不与别人交往见面会感觉与周围的社会环境和人际关系脱节。disconnected表达了一种与他人断开联系、脱离社交网络的状态，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】花太多时间独处，不与别人见面，会让你感到孤独和疏离。

【选项释义】A. contribute to 促成；导致 B. rely on 依赖C. interfere with 干扰；阻碍 D. go against 反对；对抗【考查点】词组辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了孤独对心理健康的负面影响，会导致心情低落。contribute to表示某个行为、事件或因素对某个结果的产生起到了作用，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】众所周知，孤独会影响你的心理健康，导致情绪低落。

【选项释义】A. in fact 实际上；事实上 B. of course 当然C. for example 例如 D. on average 平均【考查点】词组辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到了任何人都可能在任何时候感到孤独，此处的“如果你在家工作”是对其举例说明。for example用于提供一个具体的情境或例子，以支持前文的陈述，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】例如，如果你在家工作，你可能尤其感到孤独。

【选项释义】A. cutting back 减少；缩减 B. missing out 错过；遗漏C. breaking in 闯入；非法进入 D. looking down 俯视；向下看【考查点】词组辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了在家工作可能导致错过在办公室发生的社交对话，从而增加了孤独感。missing out通常用来表达错过某种体验、活动或机会，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】例如，如果你在家工作，你错过了在办公室发生的社交对话，你可能尤其感到孤独。

【选项释义】A. shorten 变短；缩短 B. trigger 引起；触发C. follow 跟随；跟着 D. interrupt 打扰；打岔【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了生活变化可能是导致孤独感的触发因素。trigger表示引发、触发或激发某种反应或事件，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】其他生活变化也会引发孤独感，比如退休、换工作或为人父母。

【选项释义】A. assess 评估；评定 B. interpret 诠释；说明C. provide 提供；供应 D. regain 重新获得；恢复【考查点】动词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到了有很多因素会导致孤独，此处强调了认识到孤独感之后采取积极的步骤来改善社交状况，有办法重新建立社交生活。regain表示重新获得失去的东西，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】有很多方法可以恢复社交生活。

【选项释义】A. at first 最初；开始阶段 B. in turn 依次；轮流C. on time 按时；准时 D. by chance 碰巧地；偶然地【考查点】词组辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了采取措施恢复社交生活可能会在一开始导致压力重重或不适应。at first表示在某个过程或时间段的开始阶段，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】但一开始会让人不知所措。

【选项释义】A. far-sighted 远见的 B. strong-willed 坚定意志的C. kind-hearted 善良的 D. like-minded 志同道合的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了一种寻找社交机会的方法，即在团体和活动中遇到志同道合的人，这有助于提高在社交圈中建立深层次连接的可能性，因为共同的兴趣可以成为交流和建立关系的良好起点。like-minded表示具有相似或一致的观点、价值观、兴趣等，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】然后你可以找到相关的团体和活动，在那里你可以遇到志同道合的人。

【选项释义】A. try 尝试 B. promote 推动；促进C. watch 观察 D. describe 描述【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句列举了一些尝试新鲜事物的新群体，比如针对新晋父母群体、针对第一次尝试某项体育运动的人的群体等。try表示一种初次尝试或体验，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】有针对新父母的团体，有针对第一次想尝试新运动的人的团体。

【选项释义】A. test 测试 B. share 分享C. accept 接受 D. revise 修订【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了在同行业的专业人士群体中相互交流，分享想法和经验。share表示将信息、经验、观点等与他人共享，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】有针对新父母的团体，有针对那些第一次想尝试一项新运动的人的团体，也有针对同一职业的人的社交活动，让他们见面并分享想法。
【选项释义】A. already 已经 B. thus 因此C. also 也 D. only 只；仅仅【考查点】副词辨析和上下文语义。【解题思路】上文提到过度孤独可能导致不良影响，此处表示同样存在过度社交的问题。also用于引出另一方面的观点，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】另一方面，也有可能有太多的社交生活。

【选项释义】A. visit 访问 B. order 命令C. space 空间 D. boundary 边界【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了感觉总是在忙于各种活动，没有给予自己任何休息时间。space指的是在时间上的空隙，即为自己留出一些放松和休息的时间，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】如果你觉得你总是在忙于各种事情，而且你的日历上从来没有任何休息的空间。

【选项释义】A. fatigue 疲劳 B. criticism 批评C. injustice 不公正 D. dilemma 困境；两难选择【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了总是在忙于各种活动，没有时间放松的感觉，这可能会导致社交疲劳。fatigue表示因为过度活动而导致的身体或精神上的疲劳状态，因此A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】如果你觉得你总是在忙于各种事情，并且在你的日历上没有任何休息的空间，你可能会感受到社交倦怠或社交疲劳。

【选项释义】A. sources 来源 B. standards 标准C. signs 迹象 D. scores 分数【考查点】名词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了情绪低落、精力不足、易怒和睡眠困难的一系列症状，这些症状可能是指示社交健康不佳的迹象。signs表示某事物存在或发生的标志、征兆，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】情绪低落、精力不足、易怒和睡眠困难都可能是社交健康状况不佳的迹象。

【选项释义】A. take over 接管 B. wipe off 擦掉C. add up 相加 D. mark out 标记【考查点】词组辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句讲到，建议确保在日历中为某段时间做出特殊的标记，提醒人们特别注意将这段时间用于休息和放松。mark out表示通过标记、划定或指示来特别标注某物的位置、时间或范围，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】一定要在日记本上标出一些时间。

【选项释义】A. ungrateful 不感激的 B. unavailable 不可用的C. responsible 负责的 D. regretful 后悔的【考查点】形容词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句讲到，建议确保在个人的日历中标注某段时间，以明确表示在这段时间内不用参与社交活动。unavailable表示在某段时间内不用参与社交活动的状态，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境。【句意】确保在你的日记中标出一些你不用参加社交活动的时间，并利用这段时间来放松。

【选项释义】A. react 反应 B. repeat 重复C. return 返回 D. rest 休息【考查点】动词辨析和句内语义。【解题思路】本句描述了在某段时间内应该专注于放松和休息，提醒人们在繁忙的日程中合理安排休息时间，以维护身体和心理的健康。rest表示放松身体和思维，不从事活动，以恢复体力和精神，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合语境。【句意】确保在你的日记中标出一些你不用参加社交活动的时间，利用这段时间来放松、休息和恢复。

questions.article:
In her new book
Cogs and Monsters: What Economics Is, and What It Should Be
, Diane Coyle, an economist at Cambridge University, argues that the digital economy requires new ways of thinking about progress. “Whatever we mean by the economy growing, by things getting better, the gains will have to be more evenly shared than in the recent past,” she writes. “An economy of tech millionaires or billionaires and gig workers, with middle-income jobs undercut by automation, will not be politically sustainable.”
Improving living standards and increasing prosperity for more people will require greater use of digital technologies to boost productivity in various sectors, including health care and construction, says Coyle. But people can’t be expected to embrace the changes if they’re not seeing the benefits—if they’re just seeing good jobs being destroyed.
In a recent interview with MIT Technology Review, Coyle said she fears that tech’s inequality problem could be a roadblock to deploying AI. “We’re talking about disruption,” she says. “These are transformative technologies that change the ways we spend our time every day, that change business models that succeed.” To make such “tremendous changes,” she adds, you need social buy-in.
Instead, says Coyle, resentment is simmering among many as the benefits are perceived to go to elites in a handful of prosperous cities.
According to the Brookings Institution, a short list of eight American cities that included San Francisco, San Jose, Boston, and Seattle had roughly 38% of all tech jobs by 2019. New AI technologies are particularly concentrated: Brookings’s Mark Muro and Sifan Liu estimate that just 15 cities account for two-thirds of the AI assets and capabilities in the United States (San Francisco and San Jose alone account for about one-quarter).
The dominance of a few cities in the invention and commercialization of AI means that geographical disparities in wealth will continue to soar. Not only will this foster political and social unrest, but it could, as Coyle suggests, hold back the sorts of AI technologies needed for regional economies to grow.
Part of the solution could lie in somehow loosening the stranglehold that Big Tech has on defining the AI agenda. That will likely take increased federal funding for research independent of the tech giants. Muro and others have suggested hefty federal funding to help create US regional innovation centers, for example.
A more immediate response is to broaden our digital imaginations to conceive of AI technologies that don’t simply replace jobs but expand opportunities in the sectors that different parts of the country care most about, like health care, education, and manufacturing.
1. Coyle argues that economic growth should ______.
2. According to Paragraph 2, digital technologies should be used to ______.
3. What does Coyle fear about transformative technologies?
4. Several cities are mentioned to ______.
5. With regard to concern,the author suggests ______.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Coyle argues that economic growth should ________.	科伊尔认为，经济增长应________。A. give rise to innovations	A. 带来创新B. diversity career choices	B. 多样化的职业选择C. benefit people equally	C. 使人们平等受益D. be promoted forcedly	D. 被迫推动【答案】C【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Coyle可以定位到文章第一段倒数第二句Whatever we mean by the economy growing, by things getting better, the gains will have to be more evenly shared than in the recent past（无论我们所说的经济增长、情况好转是什么意思，收益都必须比最近更加平均地分配），说明科伊尔认为经济增长应该比过去更加平均分配收益。因此C选项“使人们平等受益”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“带来创新”，B选项“多样化的职业选择”和D选项“被迫推动”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】According to Paragraph 2, digital technologies should be used to ________.	根据第二段，数字技术应该用来________。A. bring about instant prosperity	A. 带来即时繁荣B. reduce people’s workload	B. 减轻人们的工作量C. raise overall work efficiency	C. 提高整体工作效率D. enhance cross-sector cooperation	D. 加强跨部门合作【答案】C【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词digital technologies可以定位到文章第二段第一句Improving living standards and increasing prosperity for more people will require greater use of digital technologies to boost productivity in various sectors, including health care and construction, says Coyle.（科伊尔说，要提高生活水平，让更多人富裕起来，就需要更多地利用数字技术来提高各行各业的生产力，包括医疗保健和建筑业。）说明数字技术应该用来在不同领域中提高生产力。因此C选项“提高整体工作效率”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“带来即时繁荣”，B选项“减轻人们的工作量”和D选项“加强跨部门合作”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: They may be impractical to deploy.
options.D: They may be unwelcome to public.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What does Coyle fear about transformative technologies?	科伊尔对变革性技术的担心是什么？A. They may affect work-life balance.	A. 它们可能会影响工作与生活的平衡。B. They may be impractical to deploy.	B. 部署它们可能不切实际。C. They may incur huge expenditure.	C. 它们可能会产生巨额开支。D. They may be unwelcome to the public.	D. 它们可能不受公众欢迎。【答案】D【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词transformative technologies可以定位到文章第三段第二、三、四句“We’re talking about disruption,” she says. “These are transformative technologies that change the ways we spend our time every day, that change business models that succeed.” To make such “tremendous changes,” she adds, you need social buy-in.（她说：“我们谈论的是颠覆。这些都是变革性的技术，它们改变了我们每天花时间的方式，改变了成功的商业模式。”她补充说：“要想做出这样的‘巨大改变’，就需要社会的支持。”），说明科伊尔担心变革性技术可能引发颠覆，改变人们日常生活方式和成功商业模式，难以受到公众欢迎。因此D选项“它们可能不受公众欢迎。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“它们可能会影响工作与生活的平衡。”，B选项“部署它们可能不切实际。”和C选项“它们可能会产生巨额开支。”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: the uneven distribution of Al technology in US
options.B: disappointing prospect of jobs in US
options.C: fast progress of US regional economics
options.D: increasing significance of US Al assets

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Several American cities are mentioned to show ________.	文中提到几个美国城市，以说明________。A. the uneven distribution of AI technologies in the US	A. 人工智能技术在美国的分布不均衡B. the disappointing prospect of tech jobs in the US	B. 美国科技工作的前景令人失望C. the fast progress of US regional economics	C. 美国地区经济的快速发展D. the increasing significance of US AI assets	D. 美国的人工智能资产越来越重要【答案】A【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词American cities可以定位到文章第五段第一句According to the Brookings Institution, a short list of eight American cities that included San Francisco, San Jose, Boston, and Seattle had roughly 38% of all tech jobs by 2019.（根据布鲁金斯学会的数据，到2019年，包括旧金山、圣何塞、波士顿和西雅图在内的八座美国城市将拥有大约38%的科技工作岗位。），说明在2019年，仅有八个美国城市就占据了大约38%的所有科技岗位，表明了人工智能技术在美国的分布不均。因此A选项“人工智能技术在美国的分布不均衡”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“美国科技工作的前景令人失望”，C选项“美国地区经济的快速发展”和D选项“美国的人工智能资产越来越重要”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: raising funds to start new Al projects
options.D: redefining the role of Al technologies

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】With regard to Coyle’s concern, the author suggests ________.	对于科伊尔的担忧，作者建议________。A. raising funds to start new AI projects	A. 筹集资金启动新的人工智能项目B. encourage collaboration in AI research	B. 鼓励人工智能研究方面的合作C. guarding against side effects of AI	C. 防止人工智能的副作用D. redefining the role of AI technologies	D. 重新定义人工智能技术的作用【答案】D【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the author suggests可以定位到文章第七段第一、二句Part of the solution could lie in somehow loosening the stranglehold that Big Tech has on defining the AI agenda. That will likely take increased federal funding for research independent of the tech giants.（部分解决方案可能在于以某种方式放松大型科技公司对人工智能议程定义的束缚。这可能需要联邦政府增加对独立于科技巨头的研究的资助。），说明作者建议减轻大型科技公司对人工智能定义的控制，并提出可能需要增加对不依赖科技巨头的研究的联邦资助，即重新定义人工智能技术的角色。因此D选项“重新定义人工智能技术的作用”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“筹集资金启动新的人工智能项目”，B选项“鼓励人工智能研究方面的合作”和C选项“防止人工智能的副作用”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。【参考译文】剑桥大学经济学家黛安·科伊尔在她的新书《齿轮与怪物：经济学是什么，以及它应该是什么》中指出，数字经济需要新的进步思维方式。她写道：“无论我们所说的经济增长是什么意思，事情变得更好是什么意思，收益的分享都必须比过去更加平均。”一个由科技百万富翁或亿万富翁和临时工组成的经济，中等收入的工作岗位被自动化削弱，在政治上是不可持续的。科伊尔说，要提高生活水平，让更多人富裕起来，就需要更多地利用数字技术来提高各行各业的生产力，包括医疗保健和建筑业。但是，如果人们看不到这些变化带来的好处——如果他们只是看到好工作被毁掉，就不能指望他们接受这些变化。在最近接受《麻省理工科技评论》采访时，科伊尔说，她担心科技的不平等问题会成为部署人工智能的障碍。她说：“我们谈论的是颠覆。这些都是变革性的技术，它们改变了我们每天花时间的方式，改变了成功的商业模式。”她补充说，要想做出这样的“巨大改变”，就需要社会的支持。科伊尔说，相反，由于人们认为这些好处被少数几个繁荣城市的精英们获得，许多人的不满情绪正在酝酿。根据布鲁金斯学会的数据，到2019年，包括旧金山、圣何塞、波士顿和西雅图在内的八座美国城市将拥有大约38%的科技工作岗位。人工智能新技术尤其集中： 布鲁金斯的马克·穆罗和刘思帆估计，仅15个城市就占了美国人工智能资产和能力的三分之二（仅旧金山和圣何塞就占了约四分之一）。少数几个城市在人工智能的发明和商业化方面占据主导地位，这意味着地理上的贫富差距将继续扩大。这不仅会引发政治和社会动荡，而且正如科伊尔所言，还会阻碍地区经济增长所需的人工智能技术的发展。部分解决方案可能在于以某种方式放松大科技公司对人工智能议程定义的控制。这可能需要联邦政府增加对独立于科技巨头的研究的资助。例如，穆罗和其他一些人曾建议联邦政府提供巨额资金，帮助创建美国区域创新中心。更直接的应对措施是拓宽我们的数字想象力，设想人工智能技术不仅能取代工作岗位，还能在美国不同地区最关心的领域拓展机会，如医疗保健、教育和制造业。【出处】《麻省理工科技评论》2022.4.19

questions.article:
THE UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, a trade body has warned.
Confor chiefs believe urgent action is needed to reduce the country's reliance on timber imports and provide a stable supply of wood for future generations.
Currently only 20 per cent of the UK's wood requirement is homegrown while it remains the second-largest net importer of timber in the world, bringing in around £7.5 billion annually.
Coming at a time of fresh incentives from the UK government for landowners to grow more trees, the trade body says these don't go far enough and fail to promote the benefits of planting them to boost timber supplies.
“Not only are we facing a carbon crisis now, but we will also be facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood," said Stuart Goodall, chief executive of Confor. "For decades we have not taken responsibility for investing in our domestic wood supply, leaving us exposed to fluctuating prices and fighting for future supplies of wood as global demand rises and our own supplies fall."
The UK has ideal conditions for growing wood to build low-carbon homes and is a global leader in certifying that its forests are sustainably managed, Confor say. While around three quarters of Scottish homes are built from Scottish timber, the use of home-grown wood in England is only around 25 per cent.
The causes of the UK’s current position are complex and range from outdated perceptions of productive forestry to the decimation of trees from grey squirrels. It also encompasses significant hesitation on behalf of farmers and other landowners to invest in longer term planting projects.
While productive tree planting can deliver real financial benefits to rural economies and contribute to the UK’s net zero strategy, the focus of government support continues to be on food production and the rewilding and planting of native woodland solely for biodiversity.
The recently launched Woodland Creation Offer, which pledges farmers and landowners £10,000 for each hectare planted, failed to mention timber production, albeit the Forestry Commission's Richard Stanford has since spoken of the importance of it.
Stuart added: "While food production and biodiversity health are clearly of critical importance, we need our land to also provide secure supplies of wood for construction, manufacturing and to contribute to net zero.
“While the UK government has stated its ambition for more tree planting, there has been little action on the ground. Confor is now calling for much greater impetus behind those aspirations to ensure we have enough wood to meet increasing demand.”
1. It can be learned from Paragraph 1 that UK need to ____.
2. According to Confor, UK government fresh incentives ____.
3. The UK exposure to fluctuating wood prices is the result of ____.
4. Which of following causes the shortage of wood supply?
5. What does Goodall think US government should do?

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned from Paragraph 1 that UK needs to ________.	从第一段可以看出，英国需要________。A. increase domestic wood supply	A. 增加国内木材供应B. reduce demand for timber	B. 减少木材需求C. lower wood production costs	C. 降低木材生产成本D. lift control on timber imports	D. 取消对木材进口的控制【答案】A【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词Paragraph 1可以定位到文章第一段第一、二句The UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, Confor has warned. The forestry and wood trade body has called for urgent action to reduce the country’s reliance on timber imports and provide a stable supply of wood for future generations.（Confor警告说，由于未能植树造林生产木材，英国未来将面临一场建筑危机。该林业和木材贸易机构呼吁采取紧急行动，减少英国对木材进口的依赖，为子孙后代提供稳定的木材供应。），说明英国需要减少对进口木材的依赖，增加本土木材供应。因此A选项“增加国内木材供应”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“减少木材需求”和C选项“降低木材生产成本”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“取消对木材进口的控制”，文章说的是减少英国对木材进口的依赖，而不是取消控制，属于反向干扰。

options.B: are believed to come at wrong time
options.D: too costly to put into practice

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Confor, the UK government’s fresh incentives ________.	Confor认为，英国政府的新激励措施 ________。A. can hardly address construction crisis	A. 难以解决建筑危机B. are believed to come at wrong time	B. 被认为来得不是时候C. seem to be misleading for landowners	C. 似乎误导了土地所有者D. will be costly to put into practice	D. 实施起来成本很高【答案】A【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词UK government’s fresh incentives可以定位到文章第二段第一句Coming at a time of fresh incentives from the UK government for landowners to grow more trees, the trade body says these don’t go far enough and fail to promote the benefits of planting them to boost timber supplies.（正值英国政府推出新的激励措施，鼓励土地所有者种植更多树木之际，该行业组织表示，这些措施还不够，未能宣传种植树木对促进木材供应的益处。），说明Confor认为英国政府提出的新的激励措施不能很好地解决建筑危机。因此A选项“难以解决建筑危机”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“被认为来得不是时候”，C选项“似乎误导了土地所有者”和D选项“实施起来成本很高”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.D: wood producers' motive to maximize profits

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The UK’s exposure to fluctuating wood prices is the result of ________.	英国受木材价格波动影响的原因是________。A. government’s inaction on timber imports	A. 政府对木材进口不作为B. inadequate investment in growing wood	B. 对木材种植投资不足C. competition of timber traders at home	C. 国内木材贸易商的竞争D. wood producers’ motive to maximize profits	D. 木材生产商追求利润最大化的动机【答案】B【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词fluctuating wood prices可以定位到文章第二段最后一句For decades we have not taken responsibility for investing in our domestic wood supply, leaving us exposed to fluctuating prices and fighting for future supplies of wood as global demand rises and our own supplies fall.（几十年来，我们一直没有承担起对国内木材供应进行投资的责任，这使我们面临着价格波动的风险，并且随着全球需求的增加和我们自身供应的减少，我们还要为未来的木材供应而奋斗。），说明英国面临木材价格波动的原因是没有对本国木材供应进行足够的投资。因此B选项“对木材种植投资不足”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“政府对木材进口不作为”，文章说的是对本国木材不作为，而不是进口木材，属于反向干扰；C选项“国内木材贸易商的竞争”和D选项“木材生产商追求利润最大化的动机”在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】Which of the following causes the shortage of wood supply in UK?	以下哪项是造成英国木材供应短缺的原因？A. Excessive timber consumption in construction.	A. 建筑业过度消耗木材。B. Unfavorable conditions for growing trees.	B. 不利于树木生长的条件。C. Outdated technology for wood production.	C. 木材生产技术落后。D. Farmers’ unwillingness to plant trees.	D. 农民不愿植树。【答案】D【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词causes the shortage of wood supply in UK可以定位到文章第一段第一句The UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, Confor has warned.（Confor警告说，由于未能植树造林生产木材，英国未来将面临一场建筑危机。），说明造成英国木材供应短缺的原因是没有农民种树。因此D选项“农民不愿植树。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“建筑业过度消耗木材。”，B选项“不利于树木生长的条件。”和C选项“木材生产技术落后。”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.C: Provide more support for productive  tree planting.
options.D: Give priority to pursue net-zero strategy.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What does Goodall think UK government should do?	古道尔认为英国政府应该做什么？A. Subsidize the building of low-carbon homes.	A. 为建造低碳住宅提供补贴。B. Pay attention to rural economy.	B. 关注农村经济。C. Provide more support for tree planting.	C. 为植树造林提供更多支持。D. Give priority to pursue net-zero strategy.	D. 优先推行净零战略。【答案】C【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词UK government可以定位到文章第四段第一句While productive tree planting can deliver real financial benefits to rural economies and contribute to the UK’s net zero strategy, the focus of government support continues to be on food production and the rewinding and planting of native woodland solely for biodiversity.（虽然生产性植树可以为农村经济带来实际的经济效益，并有助于实现英国的净零战略，但政府支持的重点仍然是粮食生产以及仅为生物多样性而重新规划和种植的本土林地。），说明古道尔认为政府的重点支持仍然放在食品生产和仅用于生物多样性的本土林地的重建和种植上，而没有足够关注生产性树木种植对农村经济和英国净零排放战略的实际贡献。因此C选项“为植树造林提供更多支持。”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“为建造低碳住宅提供补贴。”，B选项“关注农村经济。”和D选项“优先推行净零战略。”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。【参考译文】康福警告说，由于未能植树造林生产木材，英国未来将面临一场建筑危机。该林业和木材贸易机构呼吁采取紧急行动，减少英国对木材进口的依赖，为子孙后代提供稳定的木材供应。目前，英国所需的木材中仅有20%来自本土，而英国仍是世界第二大木材净进口国。正值英国政府推出新的激励措施，鼓励土地所有者种植更多树木之际，该贸易机构表示，这些措施还不够，未能宣传种植树木对促进木材供应的益处。康福首席执行官斯图尔特·古德尔说：“我们现在不仅面临着碳危机，而且由于未能植树造林，未来还将面临建筑危机。几十年来，我们一直没有承担起对国内木材供应进行投资的责任，这使我们面临着价格波动的风险，并且随着全球需求的增加和我们自身供应的减少，我们还要为未来的木材供应而奋斗。”康福表示，英国拥有种植木材建造低碳住宅的理想条件，并且在认证其森林的可持续管理方面处于全球领先地位。苏格兰约四分之三的住宅是用苏格兰木材建造的，而英格兰本土木材的使用率仅为25%左右。虽然生产性植树可以为农村经济带来实际的经济效益，并有助于实现英国的净零战略，但政府支持的重点仍然是粮食生产以及仅为生物多样性而重新规划和种植的本地林地。古德尔补充说：“虽然粮食生产和生物多样性显然至关重要，但我们也需要我们的土地为建筑业、制造业提供安全的木材供应，并为实现净零做出贡献。”“虽然英国政府已经表明了增加植树的雄心壮志，但实际行动却很少。” 康福现在呼吁为这些愿望提供更大的动力，以确保我们有足够的木材来满足日益增长的需求。【出处】Forestry Journal网站 2022.2.16

questions.article:
One of the biggest challenges in keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road is convincing them that it is time to turn over the key. “It’s a complete life-changer when someone stops—or is forced to stop—driving,” said former risk manager Anne M. Menke.
“The American Medical Association advises physicians that ‘in situation where clear evidence of substantial driving impairment implies a strong threat to patient and public safety, and where the physician’s advice to discontinue driving privileges is ignored, it is desirable and ethical to notify the Department of Motor Vehicles,’” Menke wrote. “Some states require physicians to report, others allow but do not mandate reports, while a few consider a report breach of confidentiality. There could be liability and penalties if a physician does not act in accordance with state laws on reporting and confidentiality,” she counseled.
Part of the problem in keeping older drivers safe is that the difficulties are addressed piecemeal by different professions with different focuses, including gerontologists, highway administration officials, automotive engineers and others, said gerontologist Elizabeth Dugan. “There’s not a National Institute of Older Driver Studies,” she said. “We need better evidence on what makes drivers unsafe and what can help,” said Dugan.
One thing that does seem to work is requiring drivers to report in person for license renewal. Mandatory in-person renewal was associated with a 31 percent reduction in fatal crashes involving drivers 85 or older, according to one study. Passing vision tests also produced a similar decline in fatal crashes for those drivers, although there appeared to be no benefit from combining the two.
Many old drivers don’t see eye doctors or can’t afford to. Primary care providers have their hands full and may not be able to follow through with patients who have trouble driving because they can’t turn their heads or remember where they are going—or have gotten shorter and haven’t changed their seat settings sufficiently to reach car pedals easily.
As long as there are other cars on the roads, self-driving cars won’t solve the problems of crashes, said Dugan. Avoiding dangers posed by all those human drivers would require too many algorithms, she said. But we need to do more to improve safety, said Dugan. “If we’re going to have 100-year lives, we need cars that a 90-year-old can drive comfortably.”
1.According to Paragraph1, keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road  ______.
2.The American medical associations advice  ______.
3.According to Dugan, efforts to keep older drivers safe _________.
4.Some older drivers have trouble driving because they tend to ___________.
5.Dugan thinks that the solution to the problems of crashes may lie in___________.

options.C: can be a tough task to complete
options.D: will be beneficial to their health

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 1, keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road ________.	根据第一段，让不安全的老年司机远离马路________。A. is a new safety measure	A. 是一项新的安全措施B. has become a disputed issue	B. 已成为一个有争议的问题C. can be a tough task to complete	C. 可能是一项艰巨的任务D. will be beneficial to their health	D. 将有利于他们的健康【答案】C【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road可以定位到文章第一段第一句One big challenge in keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road is convincing them that it is time to turn over the key.（让不安全的老年司机远离马路的一大挑战是说服他们是时候交出钥匙了。），说明让不安全的老年司机远离马路是一项具有挑战性的任务。因此C选项“可能是一项艰巨的任务”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“是一项新的安全措施”，B选项“已成为一个有争议的问题”和D选项“将有利于他们的健康”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The American medical associations advice ________.	美国医学协会的建议________。A. has won support from drivers	A. 赢得了司机的支持B. is generally considered unrealistic	B. 被普遍认为不现实C. is wide dismissed as unnecessary	C. 被广泛认为没有必要D. has met with different responses	D. 得到了不同的回应【答案】D【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词The American medical associations advice可以定位到文章第二段第一句The American Medical Association advises physicians that in situation where clear evidence of substantial driving impairment implies a strong threat to patient and public safety, and where the physician’s advice to discontinue driving privileges is ignored, it is desirable and ethical to notify the Department of Motor Vehicles（美国医学协会建议医生，如果有明确证据表明驾驶能力严重受损，意味着对病人和公共安全构成严重威胁，而且医生提出的停止驾驶的建议被置若罔闻，那么通知机动车辆管理局是可取的，也是合乎道德的），接着说到各个州的反应Some states require physicians to report, others allow but do not mandate reports, while a few consider a report breach of confidentiality.（一些州要求医生报告，另一些州允许但不强制报告，还有一些州认为报告违反保密规定。），说明各州对美国医学协会的建议回应不同。因此D选项“得到了不同的回应”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“赢得了司机的支持”，B选项“被普遍认为不现实”和C选项“被广泛认为没有必要”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】According to Dugan, efforts to keep older drivers safe ________.	杜根认为，为保证老年司机的安全而做出的努力________。A. have brought about big changes	A. 带来了巨大的变化B. need to be well coordinated	B. 需要很好地协调C. have gained public concern	C. 得到了公众的关注D. call for relevant legal support	D. 需要相关的法律支持【答案】B【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词efforts to keep older drivers safe可以定位到文章第三段第一句Part of the problem in keeping older drivers safe is that the difficulties are addressed piecemeal by different professions with different focuses, including gerontologists, highway administration officials, automotive engineers and others, said gerontologist Elizabeth Dugan.（老年学专家伊丽莎白·杜根说，保证老年驾驶者安全的部分问题在于，不同专业的人员，包括老年学专家、高速公路管理官员、汽车工程师和其他人员，以不同的侧重点零敲碎打地解决困难。），说明杜根认为保持老年驾驶员安全的努力是在零散地进行，需要整体协调。因此B选项“需要很好地协调”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“带来了巨大的变化”，C选项“得到了公众的关注”和D选项“需要相关的法律支持”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】Some older drivers have trouble driving because they tend to ________.	一些年长的司机驾驶起来有困难，因为他们往往________。A. stick with bad driving habits	A. 坚持不良的驾驶习惯B. have a weakened memory	B. 记忆力减退C. suffer from chronic pains	C. 长期疼痛D. neglect car maintenance	D. 忽视汽车保养【答案】B【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词have trouble driving可以定位到文章第五段第二句have trouble driving because they can’t turn their heads or remember where they are going（因无法转头或记不住方向而驾驶困难），说明一些年长的司机驾驶困难的原因之一是记不住方向。因此B选项“记忆力减退”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“坚持不良的驾驶习惯”，C选项“长期疼痛”和D选项“忽视汽车保养”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.D: adjusting the age limit for drivers

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Dugan thinks that the solution to the problems of crashes may lie in ________.	杜根认为，解决车祸问题的办法可能在于________。A. upgrading self-driving vehicle	A. 升级自动驾驶汽车B. developing senior-friendly cars	B. 开发老年友好型汽车C. renovating transport facilities	C. 翻新交通设施D. adjusting the age limit for drivers	D. 调整司机年龄限制【答案】B【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词the solution to the problems of crashes可以定位到文章最后一段倒数第一、二句But we need to do more to improve safety, said Dugan. “If we’re going to have 100-year lives, we need cars that a 90-year-old can drive comfortably.”（但我们需要做更多的工作来提高安全性，杜根说。“如果我们要拥有100年的寿命，我们就需要90岁的老人也能舒适驾驶的汽车。”），说明杜根认为解决问题的办法可能在于需要设计一些适合90岁老人驾驶的汽车。因此B选项“开发老年友好型汽车”正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“升级自动驾驶汽车”，文章说到只要路上还有其他汽车，自动驾驶汽车就无法解决车祸问题，属于反向干扰；C选项“翻新交通设施”和D选项“调整司机年龄限制”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。【参考译文】让不安全的老年司机远离道路的一大挑战是说服他们是时候交出钥匙了。前风险经理安妮·M·门克说，当一个人停止驾驶或被迫停止驾驶时，他的生命将彻底改变。门克写道，美国医学协会建议医生，如果有明确证据表明驾驶能力严重受损，意味着对病人和公共安全构成严重威胁，而且医生提出的停止驾驶的建议被置若罔闻，那么通知机动车辆管理局是可取的，也是合乎道德的。“一些州要求医生报告，另一些州允许但不强制报告，还有一些州认为报告违反保密规定。”她建议说：“如果医生不按照各州有关报告和保密的法律行事，可能会承担责任并受到处罚。”老年学专家伊丽莎白·杜根说，保证老年驾驶者安全的部分问题在于，不同专业的人员，包括老年学专家、高速公路管理官员、汽车工程师和其他人员，以不同的侧重点零敲碎打地解决困难。她说：“没有一个国家老年驾驶员研究所。”杜根说：“我们需要更好的证据来证明是什么导致了驾驶员的不安全，以及什么可以提供帮助。”有一点似乎确实有效，那就是要求驾驶员亲自去办理驾照更新手续。一项研究表明，强制要求驾驶员亲自换证与85岁或以上驾驶员的致命车祸减少31%有关。通过视力测试也使这些驾驶员的致命车祸减少了类似的比例，不过将两者结合起来似乎并没有什么好处。许多老司机不看眼科医生或看不起眼科医生。初级保健提供者忙得不可开交，可能无法跟进因无法转头或记不住方向而驾驶困难的患者，或者因身高变矮而没有充分改变座位设置以方便够到汽车踏板的患者。杜根说，只要路上还有其他汽车，自动驾驶汽车就无法解决车祸问题。她说，要避免所有这些人类驾驶员带来的危险，需要太多的算法。但我们需要做更多的工作来提高安全性，杜根说。“如果我们要拥有100年的寿命，我们就需要90岁的老人也能舒适驾驶的汽车。”【出处】《华盛顿邮报》2022.4.24

questions.article:
If you look at the apps on your phone, chances are you have at least one related to your health—and probably several. Whether it is a mental health app, a fitness tracker, a connected health device or something else, many of us are taking advantage of this technology to keep better track of our health in some shape or form. Recent research from the Organization for the Review of Care and Health Applications found that 350,000 health apps were available on the market, 90,000 of which launched in 2020 alone.
While these apps have a great deal to offer, it is not always clear how the personal information we input is collected, safeguarded and shared online. Existing health privacy law, such as the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, is primarily focused on the way hospitals, doctors’ offices, clinics and insurance companies store health records online. The health information these apps and health data tracking wearables are collecting typically does not receive the same legal protections.
Without additional protections in place, companies may share (and potentially monetize) personal health information in a way consumers may not have authorized or anticipated. In 2021, Flo Health faced a Federal Trade Commission（FTC） investigation. The FTC alleged in a complaint that "despite express privacy claims, the company took control of users' sensitivity fertility data and shared it with third parties.” Flo Health and the FTC settled the matter with a Consent Order requiring the company to get app users' express affirmative consent before sharing their health information as well as to instruct the third parties to delete the data they had obtained.
Section 5 of the FTC Act empowers the FTC to initiate enforcement action against unfair or deceptive acts, meaning the FTC can only act after the fact if a company's privacy practices are misleading or cause unjustified consumer harm. While the FTC is doing what it can to ensure apps are keeping their promises to consumers around the handling of their sensitive health information, the rate at which these health apps are hitting the market demonstrates just how immense of a challenge this is.
As to the prospects for federal legislation, commentators suggest that comprehensive federal privacy legislation seems unlikely in the short term. States have begun implementing their own solutions to shore up protections for consumer-generated health data. California has been at the forefront of state privacy efforts with the California Consumer Privacy Act of 2018. Virginia，Colorado and Utah have also recently passed state consumer data privacy legislation.
1.The research findings are cited in Paragraph 1 to show ________.

2.What does the author imply about existing health privacy law?

3.Before sharing its users' health information, Flo Health is required to____.

4. What challenges is the FTC currently faced with?

5.It can be learned from the last paragraph that health data protection ________.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The research findings are cited in Paragraph 1 to show ________.	第一段引用研究结果是为了说明________。A. the prevalence of health apps	A. 健康应用程序的盛行B. the public concern over health	B. 公众对健康的关注C. the popularity of smartphones	C. 智能手机的普及D. the advancement of technology	D. 科技的进步【答案】A【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词The research findings可以定位到文章第一段最后一句Recent research from the Organization for the Review of Care and Health Applications found that 350,000 health apps were available on the market, 90,000 of which launched in 2020 alone.（护理与健康应用审查组织最近的研究发现，市场上有35万个健康应用，其中仅在2020年就有9万个推出。），说明健康应用非常普及。因此A选项“健康应用程序的盛行”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“公众对健康的关注”，C选项“智能手机的普及”和D选项“科技的进步”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: Its coverage needs to be extended.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What does the author imply about existing health privacy law?	作者对现行的健康隐私法有何暗示？A. Its coverage needs to be extended.	A. 需要扩大其覆盖范围。B. Its enforcement needs strengthening.	B. 需要加强执行。C. It has discouraged medical misconduct.	C. 它阻止了医疗不端行为。D. It has disappointed insurance companies.	D. 它让保险公司失望了。【答案】A【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干关键词existing health privacy law可以定位到文章第二段第二、三句Existing health privacy law, such as the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, is primarily focused on the way hospitals, doctors’ offices, clinics and insurance companies store health records online. The health information these apps and health data tracking wearables are collecting typically does not receive the same legal protections.（现有的健康隐私法，如《健康保险可携带性与责任法案》，主要针对医院、医生办公室、诊所和保险公司在线存储健康记录的方式。这些应用程序和健康数据追踪可穿戴设备收集的健康信息通常得不到同样的法律保护。），说明作者认为健康应用和健康数据追踪可穿戴设备收集的健康信息通常不受相同的法律保护，而现有法律的覆盖范围需要扩大。因此A选项“需要扩大其覆盖范围。”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“需要加强执行。”，C选项“它阻止了医疗不端行为。”和D选项“它让保险公司失望了。”在文中均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: seek the approval of the FTC
