questions.article:
Read the following text carefully and then translate the underlined segments into Chinese.
My Dream
My dream has always been to work somewhere in an area between fashion and publishing. Two years before graduating from secondary school, I took a sewing and design course thinking that I would move on to a fashion design course. However, during that course I realised that I was not good enough in this area to compete with other creative personalities in the future, so I decided that it was not the right path for me. Before applying for university I told everyone that I would study journalism, because writing was, and still is, one of my favourite activities. But, to be absolutely honest, I said it, because I thought that fashion and me together was just a dream - I knew that no one, apart from myself, could imagine me in the fashion industry at all!

Suppose you are invited by Professor Williams to give a presentation about Chinese culture to a group of international students. Write a reply to
1）Accept the invitation, and
2）Introduce the key points of your presentation.
You should write neatly on the ANWSER SHEET.
Do not
sign you own name at the end of the letter, use “Li Ming ”instead.
Do not
write the address .

Write your essay on ANSWER SHEET.
You should
1) interpret the chart, and
2) give your comments.
You should write about 150 words .

Happy people work differently. They’re more productive, more creative, and willing to take greater risks. And new research suggests that happiness might influence（1）firms work, too.
Companies located in places with happier people invest more, according to a recent research paper. （2） , firms in happy places spend more on R&D (research and development). That’s because happiness is linked to the kind of longer-term thinking （3） for making investments for the future.
The researchers wanted to know if the （4） and inclination for risk-taking that come with happiness would （5） the way companies invested. So they compared U.S. cities’ average happiness （6） by Gallup polling with the investment activity of publicly traded firms in those areas.
（7） enough, firms’ investment and R&D intensity were correlated with the happiness of the area in which they were （8） .But is it really happiness that’s linked to investment, or could something else about happier cities （9） why firms there spend more on R&D? To find out, the researchers controlled for various （10） that might make firms more likely to invest – like size, industry, and sales – and for indicators that a place was （11） to live in, like growth in wages or population. The link between happiness and investment generally （12） even after accounting for these things.
The correlation between happiness and investment was particularly strong for younger firms, which the authors （13） to “less codified decision making process” and the possible presence of “younger and less （14） managers who are more likely to be influenced by sentiment.” The relationship was （15） stronger in places where happiness was spread more （16） .Firms seem to invest more in places where most people are relatively happy, rather than in places with happiness inequality.
（17） this doesn’t prove that happiness causes firms to invest more or to take a longer-term view, the authors believe it at least （18）at that possibility. It’s not hard to imagine that local culture and sentiment would help （19） how executives think about the future. “It surely seems plausible that happy people would be more forward-thinking and creative and （20） R&D more than the average,” said one researcher.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. why 为什么	B. where 哪里C. how 如何	D. when 什么时候【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】上文指出“幸福感对人工作方式产生影响，使人更高效、创新、也更愿意冒险”，结合关键词“too”可知，上下文之间存在并列关系，因此下文应该也是说“幸福感影响公司运作方式”，选项C表示“方式”。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合上下文语境。【句意】新的研究表明幸福感也可能影响公司如何运作。

【选项释义】A. In return 反过来 B. In particular 尤其是C. In contrast 相反 D. In conclusion 总的来说【考查点】句间逻辑。【解题思路】空格上文指出“位于幸福指数高的地方的公司投资更多”，下文指出“位于幸福地方的公司在研发上投入更多”，由此可知，下文是具体说明公司在哪一方面投资更多，上下文之间存在递进关系，选项B符合。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合句间逻辑。【句意】尤其地，位于幸福之地的公司在研发上投入更多。

【选项释义】A. sufficient 充足的	B. famous 著名的C. perfect 完美的	D. necessary 必要的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合空格上下文的内容，“长远思考”以及“对未来的投资”，结合常识可知，对未来的投资需要长远思考，“长远思考”是必要条件，选项D符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】那是因为幸福感与对未来做投资所必需的那种长远思考相联系。

【选项释义】A. individualism 个人主义 B. modernism 现代主义C. optimism 乐观主义 D. realism 现实主义【考查点】名词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】分析句子结构，定语从句“that come with happiness”修饰上文and连接的两个名词，也就是说幸福感所带来的一个是“冒险倾向”，另一个就是空格处要填的词，结合常识，与幸福感挂钩的只有选项C。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】研究者们想要了解随幸福感而来的乐观和冒险倾向……

【选项释义】A. echo 发出回声 B. miss 想念，错过C. spoil 破坏 D. change 改变【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】文章首段提出论点“幸福感可能影响公司如何运作”，因此本句研究者的研究目的自然也是想要找出随幸福感而来的乐观和冒险倾向是否会影响公司的投资方式，所以选项D符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】研究者们想要了解随幸福感而来的乐观和冒险倾向是否会改变公司的投资方式。

【选项释义】A. imagined 想象 B. measured 衡量，测量C. invented 发明 D. assumed 假定，设想【考查点】动词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】根据关键词“by”可知，U.S. cities’ average happiness与Gallup polling为“动作承受者和动作施加者的关系”，结合常识，城市的平均幸福指数一般由民意测验调查得出，选项B符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】他们将盖洛普民意测验调查得出的美国城市平均幸福指数与那些地区上市公司的投资活动进行比较。

【选项释义】A. Sure 确信的 B. Odd 奇怪的C. Unfortunate 不幸运的 D. Often 经常的【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】空格下文提到“一个地区的幸福指数与公司投资正相关”，这是对上一段研究目的“想要了解幸福指数是否影响公司投资”的肯定，是意料之中的结果，因此A选项与enough搭配，表示“果真”的意思。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】果然，公司的投资和研发力度与所在地的幸福指数相关。

【选项释义】A. advertised 广告的 B. divided 划分的C. overtaxed 课税过重的 D. headquartered 位于总部的【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】空格处句子结构处于修饰the area这一地点的定语从句中，they指代前面的firms公司。上文中提到“Companies located in places”以及“firms in happy places”，因此此处应表示“公司所在地区”。选项D符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】果然，公司的投资和研发力度与其总部所在地的幸福指数相关。

【选项释义】A. explain 解释 B. overstate 夸大C. summarize 概述 D. emphasize 重点强调【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合语境“或者说，和幸福指数较高城市相关的其他方面能否……为何那里的公司在研发上投入更多”，结合常识可知，这里存在因果解释关系，也就是“其他因素解释为什么……”，选项A符合语境。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】或者说，和幸福指数较高城市相关的其他方面能否解释为何那里的公司在研发上投入更多？

【选项释义】A. stages 阶段 B. factors 因素C. levels 等级 D. methods 方法【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合语境“可能促使公司更愿意投资的多种……”，破折号后的“size, industry, and sales”是对上文的举例说明，可知，这些都是促使公司投资的因素，选项B符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】为了查明真相，研究者们控制了可能促使公司更愿意投资的多种因素……

【选项释义】A. desirable 合适的 B. sociable 社交的C. reputable 受尊敬的 D. reliable 可依靠的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】空格词修饰“a place”，用以说明在某地居住的特点。结合下文的例证“工资涨幅和人口增长”，由此可知，这里是说“宜居地方”的指标，选项A符合语境。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】以及一个地方适宜居住的指标，比如工资涨幅或人口增长。

【选项释义】A. resumed 重新开始 B. held 得出C. emerged 浮现 D. broke 破坏【考查点】动词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“幸福指数和投资之间的关系普遍……”，由even的让步转折语气可知，即使在考虑了幸福城市的其他因素后，幸福感和投资之间的关系依然普遍成立，选项B“generally held”为固定搭配，意思是“普遍适用”。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】甚至在考虑了这些情况之后，幸福感和投资之间的关联依然普遍适用。

【选项释义】A. attribute 归因 B. assign 指派C. transfer 转移 D. compare 比较【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】空格上文提到“幸福感和投资之间的关联在较年轻公司尤其明显”，which引导非限制性定语从句，限定的就是上文的内容，并从中提到了“决策过程不那么固守成规”以及“管理者年轻、易受情感影响”，这些都是解释为什么幸福感和投资之间的关联在较年轻公司尤其明显，所以which前后存在因果关系，选项A符合。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】幸福感和投资之间的关联在较年轻公司尤其明显，作者将其归因于“不那么固守成规的决策过程”……

【选项释义】A. serious 严重的，危急的 B. civilized 文明的C. ambitious 有抱负的 D. experienced 熟练的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“较为年轻且……较少的管理者，他们更容易受到情感的影响”，结合常识，选项D符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】幸福感和投资之间的关联在较年轻公司尤其明显，作者将其归因于“不那么固守成规的决策过程”以及可能存在“较为年轻且经验较少的管理者，他们更容易受到情感的影响”。

【选项释义】A. thus 因此 B. instead 反而C. also 同样，也 D. never 从不【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】本段第一句就说到“幸福感和投资之间的关联在较年轻的公司尤其明显（particularly strong）”，结合该句语境“这一关联在幸福感分布的地方……更加明显”，由此可知，这里存在递进关系，所以选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合上下文关系。【句意】这一关联在幸福感分布的地方也更加明显。

【选项释义】A. rapidly 迅速地 B. regularly 定期地，有规律地C. directly 直接地 D. equally 相当地，同样地【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】本句与下一句旨在说明投资与幸福感分布是否均衡之间的关系。下一句说到，公司似乎会对大多数人都相对幸福的地方投入更多资金，而不是分布不均的地方。因此，空格处词语应与下一句中的inequality形成语义对应，表明：在幸福感更均等分布的地方，这种关系也更明显。因此D选项equally符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】这一关联在幸福感较为均等分布的地方也更加明显。

【选项释义】A. After 在……之后 B. Until 直到C. While 然而，当 D. Since 既然，因为【考查点】句间逻辑。【解题思路】本句为主从句，从结构上来看，前一句为从句，后一句是主句。从句中存在否定表达“doesn’t prove”，主句中却有强烈肯定表达“at least…at that possibility”，即主从句间是转折关系。因此C选项while“尽管；而”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合句间逻辑。【句意】尽管这不能证明幸福感会使公司扩大投资或看问题更长远……

【选项释义】A. arrives 到达 B. jumps 跳跃C. hints 暗示 D. strikes 罢工【答案】C【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合语境“尽管这不能证明幸福感会使公司扩大投资或看问题更长远，但作者相信它至少……这种可能性”，前后句意要求空格处词语应与前面的“证明”近义。因此选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】尽管这并不能证明幸福感致使公司增加投资或更有远见，作者相信它至少隐含了那种可能性。

【选项释义】A. shape 形成 B. rediscover 重新发现C. simplify 简化 D. share 分享【考查点】动词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“地方文化和情感有助于……管理者对未来的思考”，结合常识，地方文化和人文情怀等地域因素潜移默化地影响管理者对未来投资的思考，且这种影响可能已经内化为管理者思维的一部分，所以选项A符合语境。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】地方文化和思想感情有助于塑造管理者思考未来的方式。

【选项释义】A. pray for 祈祷 B. lean towards 倾向C. give away 放弃 D. send out 放出【考查点】词组辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“幸福的人更有远见，更加创新，并且比一般人更……研发”，上文就已经表达了“位于幸福指数高的地区的公司在研发上投入更多”，由此可知，幸福的人更愿意在研发上投入，所以选项B符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】幸福的人更有远见，更加创新，并且比一般人更倾向于研发。

questions.article:
It’s true that high-school coding classes aren’t essential for learning computer science in college. Students without experience can catch up after a few introductory courses, said Tom Cortina, the assistant dean at Carnegie Mellon’s School of Computer Science.
However, Cortina said, early exposure is beneficial. When younger kids learn computer science, they learn that it’s not just a confusing, endless string of letters and numbers — but a tool to build apps, or create artwork, or test hypotheses. It’s not as hard for them to transform their thought processes as it is for older students. Breaking down problems into bite-sized chunks and using code to solve them becomes normal. Giving more children this training could increase the number of people interested in the field and help fill the jobs gap, Cortina said.
Students also benefit from learning something about coding before they get to college, where introductory computer-science classes are packed to the brim, which can drive the less-experienced or-determined students away.
The Flatiron School, where people pay to learn programming, started as one of the many coding bootcamps that’s become popular for adults looking for a career change. The high-schoolers get the same curriculum, but “we try to gear lessons toward things they’re interested in,” said Victoria Friedman, an instructor. For instance, one of the apps the students are developing suggests movies based on your mood.
The students in the Flatiron class probably won’t drop out of high school and build the next Facebook. Programming languages have a quick turnover, so the “Ruby on Rails” language they learned may not even be relevant by the time they enter the job market. But the skills they learn — how to think logically through a problem and organize the results — apply to any coding language, said Deborah Seehorn, an education consultant for the state of North Carolina.
Indeed, the Flatiron students might not go into IT at all. But creating a future army of coders is not the sole purpose of the classes. These kids are going to be surrounded by computers — in their pockets, in their offices, in their homes — for the rest of their lives. The younger they learn how computers think, how to
coax
the machine into producing what they want — the earlier they learn that they have the power to do that — the better.
1.Cortina holds that early exposure to computer science makes it easier to（  ）.
3.In delivering lessons for high-schoolers, Flatiron has considered their
（  ）.
3.Deborah Seehorn believes that the skills learned at Flatiron will
（  ）.
4.According to the last paragraph, Flatiron students are expected to
（  ）.
5.The word “coax” (Line4, Para.6) is closest in meaning to（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Cortina holds that early exposure to computer science makes it easier to _____.	科尔蒂纳认为早点接触计算机科学会使_____变得更容易。A. complete future job training	A. 完成未来工作培训B. remodel the way of thinking	B. 重塑思维方式C. formulate logical hypotheses	C. 构想逻辑假设D. perfect artwork production	D. 完善艺术制作【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干定位到文章第二段。首句提到Cortina认为早接触计算机科学有好处，下文中第三句具体说到“对他们来说，转变思维过程并不像对年纪较大的学生那样困难”，not as hard…as正好对应题干中的“make it easier”。换句话说，早接触计算机科学的孩子会更容易转变自己的思维，对应B选项内容。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】In delivering lessons for high-schoolers, Flatiron has considered their _____. 在为高中生上课时，熨斗学校考虑了他们的_____。A. experience A. 经历B. academic backgrounds B. 学术背景C. career prospects C. 职业背景D. interest D. 兴趣【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干中关键词定位到文章第四段第二句“高中生的课程设置是一样的，但我们试着让课程适应他们感兴趣的东西”，这里的we指上文出现的The Flatiron School。可知熨斗学校在为高中生授课时，考虑了他们的兴趣。因此D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: help students learn other computer languages
options.B: have to be upgraded when new technologies come
options.C: need improving when students look for jobs
options.D: enable students to make big quick money

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Deborah Seehorn believes that the skills learned at Flatiron will _____. 黛博拉·希霍恩认为在熨斗学校学到的技能会_____。A. help students learn other computer languages A. 有助于学生掌握其他计算机语言B. have to be upgraded when new technologies come B. 在新科技到来时不得不进行改进C. need improving when students look for jobs C. 在学生找工作时需要提高D. enable students to make big quick money D. 使学生能够快速地赚大钱【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干定位到第五段第三句“但是他们学到的技能——如何有逻辑地充分思考某问题并整理结果——适用于任何一种编码语言（any coding language）”，也就是选项A符合原文。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: compete with a future army of programmers
options.B: stay longer in the information technology industry
options.C: become better prepared for the digitalized world

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to the last paragraph, Flatiron students are expected to _____. 根据最后一段可知，熨斗学校的学生预计会_____。A. compete with a future army of programmers A. 与未来的程序员大军竞争B. stay longer in the information technology industry B. 在IT行业从业较长时间C. become better prepared for the digitalized world C. 为数字化世界做好更充分的准备D. bring forth innovative computer technologies D. 带来创新的计算机技术【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】最后一段第二句表明了熨斗学校的目的不仅限于（not the sole purpose）“培养编码人员”，下文说了孩子未来所面临的一个数字化的状况，也就是说计算机也将继续影响人们的生活，人们越早（younger）接触计算机知识，了解其思维方式，为我所用就越好，所以选项C符合原文。【干扰项排除】A选项“与未来的程序员大军竞争”，原文只提到了“培养程序员大军不是熨斗学校的唯一目的”，无法推出该选项的内容，属于无中生有；B选项“在IT行业从业较长时间”，原文提到的是“熨斗学校的学生可能根本不会从事IT行业”，属于曲解原文；D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The word “coax” (Line4, Para.6) is closest in meaning to _____. 与“coax”（第六段第四行）含义最接近的是_____。A. challenge A. 挑战B. persuade B. 劝服C. frighten C. 吓唬D. misguide D. 误导【考查点】词义推测题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到文章最后一段第四句“他们越早学习电脑如何思维，如何……这一机器去制作自己需要的东西”，从全文主旨来说，让青少年学习计算机编码知识的目的在于利用计算机处理实际问题，选项B在感情色彩上符合“利用计算机来实现目标”。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合词义，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
Biologists estimate that as many as 2 million lesser prairie chickens---a kind of bird living on stretching grasslands—once lent red to the often gray landscape of the Midwestern and southwestern United States. But just some 22,000 birds remain today, occupying about 16% of the species’ historic range.
The crash was a major reason the U.S Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) decided to formally list the bird as threatened. “The lesser prairie chicken is in a desperate situation,” said USFWS Director Daniel Ashe. Some environmentalists, however, were disappointed. They had pushed the agency to designate the bird as “endangered,” a status that gives federal officials greater regulatory power to crack down on threats. But Ashe and others argued that the “threatened” tag gave the federal government flexibility to try out new, potentially less confrontational conservations approaches. In particular, they called for forging closer collaborations with western state governments, which are often uneasy with federal action and with the private landowners who control an estimated 95% of the prairie chicken’s habitat.
Under the plan, for example, the agency said it would not prosecute landowner or businesses that unintentionally kill, harm, or disturb the bird, as long as they had signed a range—wide management plan to restore prairie chicken habitat. Negotiated by USFWS and the states, the plan requires individuals and businesses that damage habitat as part of their operations to pay into a fund to replace every acre destroyed with 2 new acres of suitable habitat. The fund will also be used to compensate landowners who set aside habitat, USFWS also set an interim goal of restoring prairie chicken populations to an annual average of 67,000 birds over the next 10 years. And it gives the Western Association of Fish and Wildlife Agencies (WAFWA), a coalition of state agencies, the job of monitoring progress. Overall, the idea is to let “states” remain in the driver’s seat for managing the species，” Ashe said.
Not everyone buys the win-win rhetoric. Some Congress members are trying to block the plan, and at least a dozen industry groups, four states, and three environmental groups are challenging it in federal court. Not surprisingly, industry groups and states generally argue it goes too far; environmentalists say it doesn’t go far enough. “The federal government is giving responsibility for managing the bird to the same industries that are pushing it to extinction,” says biologist Jay Lininger.
1.The major reason for listing the lesser prairie as threatened is（  ）.
2.The “threatened” tag disappointed some environmentalists in that it
（  ）.
3.It can be learned from Paragraph3 that unintentional harm-doers will not be prosecuted if they
（  ）.
4.According to Ashe， the leading role in managing the species is
（  ）.
5.Jay Lininger would most likely support（  ）.

options.B: the underestimate of the grassland acreage
options.C: a desperate appeal from some biologists

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The major reason for listing the lesser prairie chicken as threatened is _____.	将小草原鸡列为濒危物种的主要原因是_____。A. its drastically decreased population	A. 其数量大幅下降B. the underestimate of the grassland acreage	B. 草原面积被低估C. a desperate appeal from some biologists	C. 一些生物学家的迫切呼吁D. the insistence of private landowners	D. 私有土地所有者的坚持【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】本题找的是原因，关键词出现在文章第二段首句“美国鱼类和野生动物管理局（USFWS）决定正式将这种鸟类列为濒危物种（threatened），这一急剧下降（crash）是主要原因（major reason）”，The crash是指上一段中提到的小草原鸡数量锐减的情况。因此A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: was a give-in to governmental pressure
options.B: would involve fewer agencies in action

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The “threatened” tag disappointed some environmentalists in that it _____. “受威胁”标签令一些环保主义者感到失望是因为它_____。A. was a give-in to governmental pressure A. 是对政府压力的一种屈服B. would involve fewer agencies in action B. 将使参与行动的机构变少C. granted less federal regulatory power C. 赋予了较小的联邦监管权D. went against conservation policies D. 与保护政策相违背【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到原文第二段三、四句，第四句给出了失望（disappointed）的原因“他们敦促该机构将这种鸟类列为‘濒危物种’（endangered），这一定位会给予联邦官员更大的监管权力（greater regulatory power），以打击对该物种的威胁”，也就是说，如今“受威胁”（threatened）的标签意味着所赋予联邦官员的监管权相比他们所期待的“濒危物种”（endangered）会有所减少，因此C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: agree to pay a sum for compensation
options.B: volunteer to set up an equally big habitat
options.C: offer to support the WAFWA monitoring job
options.D: promise to raise funds for USFWS operations

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned from Paragraph 3 that unintentional harm-doers will not be prosecuted if they _____. 从第三段可知，非故意伤害者将不会被起诉，如果他们_____。A. agree to pay a sum for compensation A. 同意支付一笔赔偿金B. volunteer to set up an equally big habitat B. 自愿建立一个同样大小的栖息地C. offer to support the WAFWA monitoring job C. 愿意支持WAFWA的监督工作D. promise to raise funds for USFWS operations D. 承诺为USFWS的运动筹集资金【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】第三段第一句说到“在此项计划之下，例如，该机构表示它不会起诉（not prosecute）非故意杀死、伤害或骚扰该鸟类的土地所有者或企业，只要（as long as）他们已经签署一项为恢复草原鸡栖息地而制定的大范围管理计划”，句中的as long as对应题干中的if，因此as long as后面说到的就是关键。下面第二句列出了签署内容的详细要求“要求在经营中破坏栖息地的个人和企业向一项基金付款（pay into a fund）”。因此A选项符合文意。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】According to Ashe, the leading role in managing the species in _____. 根据阿什所说，在管理该物种中扮演主导角色的是_____。A. the federal government A. 联邦政府B. the wildlife agencies B. 野生动物机构C. the landowners C. 土地所有者D. the states D. 各州【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干定位到文章第三段最后一句“基本理念为：让各州（states）处于管理该物种的主导地位（in the driver’s seat）”，因此D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合原文，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】Jay Lininger would most likely support _____. 杰伊·林恩格最可能支持_____。A. industry groups A. 行业团体B. the win-win rhetoric B. 双赢说辞C. environmental groups C. 环保团体D. the plan under challenge D. 受到挑战的计划【考查点】观点态度题。【解题思路】由题干定位到最后一段第四句“联邦政府（The federal government）是在将管理该物种的责任推卸给那些正在将物种推向灭绝（extinction）的行业”，由此可见，他认为联邦政府应自己负起管理小草原鸡的主要责任，而不应推卸给各州、各行业。这与环保主义者观点一致，选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合原文，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
That everyone’s too busy these days is a cliché. But one specific complaint is made especially mournfully： There’s never any time to read.
What makes the problem thornier is that the usual time-management techniques don’t seem sufficient. The web’s full of articles offering tips on making time to read: “Give up TV” or “Carry a book with you at all times” But in my experience, using such methods to free up the odd 30 minutes doesn’t work. Sit down to read and the flywheel of work-related thoughts keeps spinning-or else you’re so exhausted that a challenging book’s the last thing you need. The modern mind, Tim Parks, a novelist and critic, writes, “is overwhelmingly inclined toward communication…It is not simply that one is interrupted; it is that one is actually inclined to interruption”. Deep reading requires not just time, but a special kind of time which can’t be obtained merely by becoming more efficient.
In fact, “becoming more efficient” is part of the problem. Thinking of time as a resource to be maximized means you approach it instrumentally, judging any given moment as well spent only in so far as it advances progress toward some goal immersive reading, by contrast, depends on being willing to risk inefficiency, goallessness, even time-wasting. Try to slot it as a to-do list item and you’ll manage only goal-focused reading-useful, sometimes, but not the most fulfilling kind. “The future comes at us like empty bottles along an unstoppable and nearly infinite conveyor belt,” writes Gary Eberle in his book Sacred Time, and “we feel a pressure to fill these different-sized bottles (days, hours, minutes) as they pass, for if they get by without being filled, we will have wasted them”. No mind-set could be worse for losing yourself in a book.
So what does work? Perhaps surprisingly, scheduling regular times for reading. You’d think this might fuel the efficiency mind-set, but in fact, Eberle notes, such ritualistic behavior helps us “step outside time’s flow” into “soul time”. You could limit distractions by reading only physical books, or on single-purpose e-readers. “Carry a book with you at all times” can actually work, too-providing you dip in often enough, so that reading becomes the default state from which you temporarily surface to take care of business, before dropping back down. On a really good day, it no longer feels as if you’re “making time to read,” but just reading, and making time for everything else.
1.The usual time-management techniques don’t work because（  ）.
2.The “empty bottles” metaphor illustrates that people feel a pressure to（  ）.
3.Eberle would agree that scheduling regular times for reading helps
（  ）.
4.“Carry a book with you at all times” can work if
（  ）.
5.The best title for this text could be（  ）.

options.A: what they can offer does not ease the modern mind
options.B: what challenging books demand is repetitive reading
options.C: what people often forget is carrying a book with them
options.D: what deep reading requires cannot be guaranteed

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The usual time-management techniques don’t work because _____.	常规的时间管理技巧不起作用的原因是_____。A. what they can offer does not ease the modern mind	A. 它们所提供的东西不能使现代人放松B. what challenging books demand is repetitive reading	B. 具有挑战性的书需要的是反复阅读C. what people often forget is carrying a book with them	C. 人们经常忘记的是随身携带一本书D. what deep reading requires cannot be guaranteed	D. 深度阅读所需要的无法得到保证【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】由题干定位到第二段第三句“但根据我的经验，采用这些窍门挤出个30来分钟并没有什么用（doesn’t work）”，也就是上文提到的时间管理技巧不可行，再定位到第六句“深度阅读（Deep reading）需要的不只是时间，而是一种特殊的时间（a special kind of time），这种时间仅仅靠提高效率（efficient）是无法得到的”，也就是说，常规的时间管理技巧虽然有助于提高效率，但这样挤出来的时间有别于“特殊时间”，不能满足深度阅读所需，选项D正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The “empty bottles” metaphor illustrates that people feel a pressure to _____. “空瓶子”的隐喻表明人们对于_____感到有压力。A. update their to-do lists A. 更新他们的任务清单B. make passing time fulfilling B. 让流逝的时间充盈起来C. carry their plans through C. 完成他们的计划D. pursue carefree reading D. 追求无忧无虑的阅读【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】由题干定位到第三段第四句“未来（future）就像永不停歇、永无止境传送带上的空瓶子（empty bottles）一样向我们走来”，“随着时间的流逝（pass），我们要把这些不同大小的瓶子装满（fill），对此我们倍感压力（pressure）”，也就是说人们是对“让流逝的时间充盈起来”倍感压力，选项B正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】Eberle would agree that scheduling regular times for reading helps _____. 埃伯利赞同安排固定时间段阅读有助于_____。A. encourage the efficiency mind-set A. 激励效率至上的思维模式B. develop online reading habits B. 培养在线阅读的习惯C. promote ritualistic reading C. 促进例行性阅读D. achieve immersive reading D. 实现沉浸式阅读【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】文章第四段中第三句提到了“这种例行行为帮助我们‘从时间流逝中走出来’，进入‘灵魂时间’（soul time）”，对应第三段第三句可知，“灵魂时间”就是指immersive reading沉浸式阅读。因此D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“激励效率至上的思维模式”，原文第四段第三句提到“你或许认为这可能会加剧效率至上的思维倾向”，这句话不属于埃伯利赞的观点，只是为了引出他的观点，属于曲解原文；B选项属于无中生有；C选项“促进例行性阅读”，埃伯利赞并没有提出这个观点，属于曲解原文。

options.A: reading becomes your primary business of the day
options.B: all the daily business has been promptly dealt with
options.C: you are able to drop back to business after reading
options.D: time can be evenly split for reading and business

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】“Carry a book with you at all times” can work if _____. 如果_____，“始终随身携带一本书”会起作用。A. reading becomes your primary business of the day A. 阅读成为你一天当中的首要事务B. all the daily business has been promptly dealt with B. 所有的日常事务都已得到立即处理C. you are able to drop back to business after reading C. 你能够在阅读之后返回到工作中去D. time can be evenly split for reading and business D. 时间可以平均分配给阅读和工作【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】定位文章第四段第五句，破折号后providing即对应题干中的if，因此这个前提就是“能经常沉浸于书中，阅读成为默认状态（default state），而其他事务则变为附带”也就是说，阅读应该成为日常事务之首，A选项与这一点相符合。【干扰项排除】B选项“所有的日常事务都已得到立即处理”，与原文“temporarily surface to take care of business短暂去处理事务”不符，属于曲解原文；C选项“你能够在阅读之后返回到工作中去”，与原文“before dropping back down处理完事务返回阅读”不符，属于曲解原文；D选项“时间可以平均分配给阅读和工作”，不符合“阅读应该为日常事务之首”的观点，属于曲解原文。

options.B: How to Find Time to Read

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The best title for this text could be _____. 本文最佳标题可能是_____。A. How to Enjoy Easy Reading A. 如何享受轻松阅读B. How to Find Time to Read B. 如何找时间阅读C. How to Set Reading Goals C. 如何设定阅读目标D. How to Read Extensively D. 如何广泛阅读【考查点】主旨大意题。【解题思路】第一段提出问题：如今人们抱怨没有时间阅读。接下文第二、三段分析问题：阐释常见时间管理技巧不可行的根本原因。第四段给出建议：安排固定时间阅读，把阅读当作“首要事务”，可知，全文是针对“如何找时间阅读”展开，选项B符合主旨。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

questions.article:
Against a backdrop of drastic changes in economy and population structure, younger Americans are drawing a new 21st-century road map to success, a latest poll has found.
Across generational lines, Americans continue to prize many of the same traditional milestones of a successful life, including getting married, having children, owning a home, and retiring in their sixties. But while young and old mostly agree on what constitutes the finish line of a fulfilling life, they offer strikingly different paths for reaching it.
Young people who are still getting started in life were more likely than older adults to prioritize personal fulfillment in their work, to believe they will advance their careers most by regularly changing jobs, to favor communities with more public services and a faster pace of life, to agree that couples should be financially secure before getting married or having children, and to maintain that children are best served by two parents working outside the home, the survey found.
From career to community and family, these contrasts suggest that in the aftermath of the searing Great Recession, those just starting out in life are defining priorities and expectations that will increasingly spread through virtually all aspects of American life, from consumer preferences to housing patterns to politics.
Young and old converge on one key point: Overwhelming majorities of both groups said they believe it is harder for young people today to get started in life than it was for earlier generations. While younger people are somewhat more optimistic than their elders about the prospects for those starting out today, big majorities in both groups believe those “just getting started in life” face a tougher climb than earlier generations in reaching such signpost achievements as securing a good-paying job, managing debt, and finding affordable housing.
Pete Schneider considers the climb tougher today. Schneider, a 27-year-old auto technician from the Chicago suburbs says he struggled to find a job after graduating from college. Even now that he is working steadily, he said. "I can’t afford to pay my monthly mortgage payments on my own, so I have to rent rooms out to people to mark that happen.” Looking back, he is struck that his parents could provide a comfortable life for their children even though neither had completed college when he was young. “I still grew up in an upper middle-class home with parents who didn’t have college degrees,” Schneider said. “I don’t think people are capable of that anymore.”
1.One cross-generation mark of a successful life is（  ）.
2.It can be learned from Paragraph 3 that young people tend to 
（  ）.
3.The priorities and expectations defined by the young will 
（  ）.
4.Both young and old agree that
（  ）.
5.Which of the following is true about Schneider?

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】One cross-generation mark of a successful life is _____.	成功人生的一个跨时代标志是_____。A. trying out different lifestyles	A. 尝试不同生活方式B. having a family with children	B. 结婚生子C. working beyond retirement age	C. 到退休年龄后仍旧工作D. setting up a profitable business	D. 创建一家盈利企业【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】文章第二段首句说到“跨越几代人，美国人仍然珍视许多相同的成功人生（successful life）的传统里程碑（traditional milestones），包括结婚、生子、拥有自己的房子，以及在60多岁退休”，milestones对应题干中的mark，后面列举的几项就是成功人生的跨时代标志。对比选项可得B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.D: give priority to childcare out side the home

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned from Paragraph 3 that young people tend to _____. 从第三段可以得知年轻人往往_____。A. favor a slower life pace A. 更喜欢较慢的生活节奏B. hold an occupation longer B. 在一个职位上待得更久C. attach importance to pre-marital finance C. 重视婚前财务状况D. give priority to childcare outside the home D. 优先考虑在外面托管孩子【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到第三段最后一句“他们赞同夫妻在结婚生子前应该有经济保障（financially secure）”，也就是选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“更喜欢较慢的生活节奏”，不符合第三段提到的“a faster pace of life生活节奏更快”，属于曲解原文；B选项“在一个职位上待得更久”，不符合第三段提到的“regularly changing jobs频繁更换工作最能推动职业发展”，属于曲解原文；D选项“优先考虑在外面托管孩子”，不符合第三段提到的“children are best served by two parents孩子最好由均在外工作的双亲来抚育”，属于曲解原文。

options.D: reach almost all aspects of American life

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The priorities and expectations defined by the young will _____. 年轻人界定的首要事项和期望将_____。A. become increasingly clear A. 变得日益清晰B. focus on materialistic issues B. 聚焦物质问题C. depend largely on political preferences C. 很大程度上取决于政治偏好D. reach almost all aspects of American life D. 几乎触及美国人生活的所有方面【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干中关键词定位到文章第四段，本段说到“那些刚刚开始生活的人正在确定优先事项和期望，这些优先事项和期望（priorities and expectations）将越来越广泛地渗透到美国生活的各个方面（all aspects of American life）”，D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: the old made more life achievements
options.C: housing loans today are easy to obtain
options.D: getting established is harder for the young

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Both young and old agree that _____. 年轻人和老一辈都认为_____。A. good-paying jobs are less available A. 高薪的工作变得更少B. the old made more life achievements B. 老一辈人的人生成就更多C. housing loans today are easy to obtain C. 今天的住房贷款容易获得D. getting established is harder for the young D. 对年轻人来说立足更困难【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到第五段，该段首先指出年轻人和老一辈人的共识：对今日的年轻人来说，人生起步（get started in life）比之前的几代人更困难（harder）；下文接着提到“在找到高薪工作（good-paying job）、组建家庭（starting a family）、管理债务（managing debt）和拥有可支付性住房（affordable housing）这些标志性成就上，那些‘刚刚人生起步’的人比几代之前的人攀行得更困难”，由此可知，选项D符合原文。【干扰项排除】A、B选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项“今天的住房贷款容易获得”，与文章最后一段“I can’t afford to pay my monthly mortgage payments on my own, so I have to rent rooms out to people to mark that happen.”表述相反，属于反向干扰。

options.A: He found a dream job after graduating from college.
options.B: His parents believe working steadily is a must for success.
options.C: His parents’good life has little to do with a college degree.
options.D: He thinks his job as a technician quite challenging.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following is true about Schneider? 下面哪一项关于施耐德的内容是正确的？A. He found a dream job after graduating from college. A. 他在大学毕业后找到了一份理想的工作。B. His parents believe working steadily is a must for success. B. 他父母认为稳定工作是成功必需。C. His parents’ good life has little to do with a college degree. C. 他父母的上层生活和大学文凭几乎没有关系。D. He thinks his job as a technician quite challenging. D. 他认为他的技师工作颇有挑战性。【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到最后一段第五句“尽管爸妈没有大学文凭（college degrees），但是我仍然在一个上层中产阶级家庭（upper middle-class home）中长大”，由此可推断出选项C。【干扰项排除】A选项“他在大学毕业后找到了一份理想的工作”，原文最后一段第二句提到“大学毕业后很努力找到一份工作”，但没有提及“理想”，属于无中生有；B选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。第二句，文中说到他大学毕业后很艰难才找到第一份工作，并没有说这份工作怎样，排除D选项。C选项正确。

questions.article:
As adults, it seems that we are constantly pursuing happiness, often with mixed results. Yet children appear to have it down to an art-and for the most part they don't need self-help books or therapy. Instead, they look after their wellbeing instinctively, and usually more effectively than we do as grownups. Perhaps it's time to learn a few lessons from them.
1.____________
__
What does a child do when he's sad? He cries. When he's angry? He shouts. Scared? Probably a bit of both. As we grow up, we learn to control our emotions so they are manageable and don't dictate our behaviours, which is in many ways a good thing. But too often we take this process too far and end up suppressing emotions, especially negative ones. That’s about as effective as brushing dirt under a carpet and can even make us ill. What we need to do is find a way to acknowledge and express what we feel appropriately, and then-again ，like children-move on.
2._______
___
__
A couple of Christmases ago, my youngest stepdaughter, who was nine years old at the time, got a Superman T-shirt for Christmas. It cost less than a fiver but she was overjoyed, and couldn't stop talking about it. Too often we believe that a new job, bigger house or better car will be the magic silver bullet that will allow us to finally be content, but the reality is these things have very little lasting impact on our happiness levels. Instead, being grateful for small things every day is a much better way to improve wellbeing.
3.__________________
____
Have you ever noticed how much children laugh? If we adults could indulge in a bit of silliness and giggling, we would reduce the stress hormones in our bodies, increase good hormones like endorphins, improve blood flow to our hearts and even have a greater chance of fighting off infection. All of which, of course, have a positive effect on happiness levels.
4._____________
_____
The problem with being a grown up is that there's an awful lot of serious stuff to deal with---work, mortgage payments, figuring out what to cook for dinner. But as adults we also have the luxury of being able to control our own diaries and it's important that we schedule in time to enjoy the things we love. Those things might be social, sporting, creative or completely random (dancing around the living room, anyone?)--it doesn't matter, so long as they're enjoyable, and not likely to have negative side effects, such as drinking too much alcohol or going on a wild spending spree if you're on a tight budget.
5._____________
__
____
Having said all of the above, it's important to add that we shouldn't try too hard to be happy. Scientists tell us this can backfire and actually have a negative impact on our wellbeing. As the Chinese philosopher Chuang Tzu is reported to have said: “Happiness is the absence of striving for happiness. “And in that, once more, we need to look to the example of our children, to whom happiness is not a goal but a natural byproduct of the way they live.

options.G: Ask for help

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】第二段提到与情绪相关的字眼，并且在最后一句给出建议“express what we feel appropriately”，这一点与C选项Express your emotions“表达情绪”相符合，因此C选项正确。

options.G: Ask for help

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段最后一句作者给出的建议是“每天为小事感恩是改善幸福感的好办法”。E选项“知足常乐”符合文意。

options.G: Ask for help

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段第二句以条件句的形式给出建议：如果我们成年人能尽情地傻笑，干点傻事，就会给我们身体带来很多好处。A选项“Be silly”正好符合这条建议。

options.G: Ask for help

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】第二句说到建议“it’s important that we schedule in time to enjoy the things we love”，安排时间享受我们喜欢的事情很重要。符合B选项Have fun这一点建议。

options.G: Ask for help

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】第一句明确表明了最后一条建议是“we shouldn’t try too hard to be happy”，不应该过分追逐幸福。其实这里说的就是“过度”的问题，如果获得幸福的过程过于艰难，可能适得其反。幸福不是目标，而是自然产生的。因此D选项“不要思虑过度”符合文意。

questions.article:
Translate the following text into Chinese.
The supermarket is designed to lure customers into spending as much time as possible within its doors. The reason for this is simple: The longer you stay in the store, the more stuff you'll see, and the more stuff you see, the more you'll buy. And supermarkets contain a lot of stuff. The average supermarket, according to the Food Marketing Institute, carries some 44,000 different items, and many carry tens of thousands more. The sheer volume of available choice is enough to send shoppers into a state of information overload. According to brain-scan experiments, the demands of so much decision-making quickly become too much for us. After about 40 minutes of shopping, most people stop struggling to be rationally selective, and instead begin shopping emotionally - which is the point at which we accumulate the 50 percent of stuff in our cart that we never intended buying.

Suppose you won a translation contest and your friend Jack wrote an email to congratulate you, and ask advice on translation. Write him a reply to
1)thank him;
2)give your advice.
You should write neatly on the ANWSER SHEET.
Do not
sign you own name at the end of the letter, use “Li Ming ”instead.
Do not
write the address .

Write an essay based on the following chart. you should
1) interpret the chart and
2) give your comments.
You should write about 150 words.
某高校学生旅游目的的调查

In our contemporary culture, the prospect of communicating with—or even looking at—a stranger is virtually unbearable. Everyone around us seems to agree by the way they cling to their phones, even without a __1__ on a subway.
It’s a sad reality—our desire to avoid interacting with other human beings—because there’s __2__ to be gained from talking to the stranger standing by you. But you wouldn’t know it, __3__ into your phone. This universal protection sends the __4__: “Please don’t approach me.”
What is it that makes us feel we need to hide __5__ our screens?
One answer is fear, according to Jon Wortmann, executive mental coach. We fear rejection, or that our innocent social advances will be __6__ as “weird”. We fear we’ll be__7__. We fear we’ll be disruptive. Strangers are inherently __8__ to us, so we are more likely to feel __9__ when communicating with them compared with our friends and acquaintances. To avoid this anxiety, we __10__ to our phones. “Phones become our security blanket,” Wortmann says. “They are our happy glasses that protect us from what we perceive is going to be more __11__”.
But once we rip off the bandaid, tuck our smartphones in our pockets and look up, it doesn’t __12__ so bad. In one 2011 experiment, behavioral scientists Nicholas Epley and Juliana Schroeder asked commuters to do the unthinkable: Start a __13__. They had Chicago train commuters talk to their fellow __14__. “When Dr. Epley and Ms. Schroeder asked other people in the same train station to __15__ how they would feel after talking to a stranger, the commuters thought their __16__ would be more pleasant if they sat on their own,” the New York Times summarizes. Though the participants didn’t expect a positive experience, after they __17__ with the experiment, “not a single person reported having been embarrassed.”
__18__, these commutes were reportedly more enjoyable compared with those without communication, which makes absolute sense, __19__ human beings thrive off of social connections. It’s that __20__: Talking to strangers can make you feel connected.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. ticket 票，入场券	B. permit 允许，许可证C. signal 信号，暗号	D. record 记录，记载【考查点】名词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“我们周围的每一个人似乎都同意这点，他们玩着手机，即使地铁上一点儿……都没有”，结合常识，与“手机”“地铁”相关的是“信号”，选项C符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】我们周围的每一个人似乎都同意这点，他们玩着手机，即使地铁上一点儿信号都没有。

【选项释义】A. nothing 没有什么 B. link 联系，关系C. another 另一 D. much 许多【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】上文第一句是对第一段内容“不与陌生人交流”的总结：“这是个可悲的现实”，然后双破折号的内容是对“sad reality”的进一步解释，空格所在的句子是对其的原因解释“与陌生人交流，我们会从中收获……”，结合语境，作者应该是支持与陌生人交流的，所以选项D符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】与陌生人交流，我们会从中收获很多。
【选项释义】A. beaten 打败 B. guided 带领，引导C. plugged 堵，插入 D. brought 带来【考查点】固定搭配。【解题思路】plug into为固定搭配，意思是“深陷其中，投入”，这里是说“沉浸在手机中”，选项C符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】但是你不知道这一点，一直沉浸在手机中。

【选项释义】A. message 消息 B. cede 割让，放弃C. notice 注意，通告 D. sign 标志，迹象【考查点】名词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合语境“这种普遍存在的盔甲（不要和陌生人交流）传递了‘不要靠近我’这个……”，空格内容是对下文“Please don’t approach me”的概括，另外结合sends与message的搭配关系，可知，选项A符合。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】这种普遍的保护发出一个信号：“请不要靠近我”。
【选项释义】A. under 在……下面 B. beyond 超出C. behind 在……之后 D. from 来自【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】“hide … our screens”与上文“plugged into your phone”相照应，由此可推断出选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】是什么让我们觉得我们需要躲在手机屏幕后面？

【选项释义】A. misinterpreted 误解的 B. misapplied 误用的C. misadjusted 失调的 D. mismatched 不匹配的【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合空格前后的两个关键词“innocent无辜的”、“weird怪异的”，由此可知，这里应该是“被误解”，也就是be misinterpreted as，选项A正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】我们害怕拒绝，或者是我们无辜的社会进步会被误解为“怪异的”。
【选项释义】A. fired 开火，解雇 B. judged 判断，评判C. replaced 取代 D. delayed 推迟，延期【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】空格所在的句子和上下文是并列关系，上文说到“害怕拒绝，被误解为‘怪异’”，下文提到“我们害怕制造混乱”。由此可知，空格处所填的词也是负面的，选项B符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】我们害怕我们会被评判。
【选项释义】A. unreasonable 不合理的 B. ungrateful 忘恩负义的C. unconventional 非常规的 D. unfamiliar 不熟悉的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】空格处所填的词修饰“strangers”，所以结合常识可知，选项D正确，陌生人就是“不熟悉的”。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】陌生人对我们来说有一种固有的陌生感。

【选项释义】A. comfortable 舒服的 B. anxious 焦急的C. confident 自信的 D. angry 生气的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】空格所填的词是表示和陌生人相处的感受，与和friends and acquaintances的感受比较，B选项符合。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】因此与和我们的朋友相识交流相比，与陌生人交流我们更有可能感觉焦虑。
【选项释义】A. attend 参加，出席 B. point 指C. take 拿走，带走 D. turn 转动【考查点】固定搭配。【解题思路】turn to表示“转向，求助于某人/物”，这里是说“我们转向我们的手机”，选项D符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】为了避免这种焦虑，我们转向我们的手机。

【选项释义】A. dangerous 危险的 B. mysterious 神秘的C. violent 暴力的 D. boring 乏味的【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合句子语境“保护我们远离我们认为可能……的事物”可知，空格处填负向词，另外上文提到了“uneasiness不安”“security安全”，可知，选项A与这些词形成呼应。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】保护我们远离我们认为可能危险的事物。

【选项释义】A. hurt 受伤害 B. resist 阻挡，抵制C. bend 弯曲，转弯 D. decay 破坏，衰败【考查点】动词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合but，表示与上文信息形成转折。上文讲到手机是避免“不安”和“焦虑”的“安乐毯”和“保护镜”。本句语境：但是，一旦我们撕下（手机）这个创可贴，把它塞进口袋，抬起头，其实情况没有那么……。结合语境，A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】事实并没有那么糟糕。
【选项释义】A. lecture 讲课，演讲 B. conversation 对话，谈话C. debate 讨论，辩论 D. negotiation 协商，谈判【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】由上文推断此处为研究者进行的一项实验内容，根据下文的“talk to”可知，实验室让研究对象交谈，选项B符合。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】开始交谈。
【选项释义】A. trainees 受训者，实习生 B. employees 员工，雇员C. researchers 研究者 D. passengers 乘客【考查点】名词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】由“train commuters”可知，对应的是“乘客”，选项D符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】他们让芝加哥火车通勤者和他们周围的乘客聊天。
【选项释义】A. reveal 揭示，透露 B. choose 选择C. predict 预言，预测 D. design 设计【考查点】动词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】结合下文从句中的时态“would feel”，这是一个过去将来时，所以填的词要包含时间概念，选项C符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】让同一车站的其他人预测他们和陌生人交谈之后是什么感受……
【选项释义】A. voyage 旅行 B. flight 航班，飞行C. walk 步行 D. ride 骑，搭乘【考查点】关联上下文。【解题思路】结合上文“train commuters火车通勤者”可知，D选项符合语境。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合语境。【句意】通勤者认为如果他们坐在自己的座位上，行程会更愉悦。
【选项释义】A. went through 完成 B. did away 去掉C. caught up 抓住 D. put up 举起【考查点】固定搭配。【解题思路】go through with为固定搭配，意思是“完成”，与下文的“the experiment实验”搭配，意思是“完成实验”。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】当他们完成实验之后……

【选项释义】A. In turn 反过来，转而 B. In particular 尤其，特别C. In fact 事实上，实际上 D. In consequence 因此，结果【考查点】句间逻辑。【解题思路】上文提到那些放下手机，积极与乘客交流的人并没有受到冷落，下文指出，与那些没有对话的人相比，这些人更开心，可见，上下文存在并列或递进关系，所以选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合上下文逻辑。【句意】事实上，据说与那些没有交流的通勤者相比，这些通勤者感到更愉悦。
【选项释义】A. unless 除非 B. since 因为，既然C. if 如果 D. whereas 然而，但是【考查点】句间逻辑。【解题思路】上文指出，与那些没有对话的人相比，那些积极交谈的人更加开心。下文则提到人类的繁荣起源于社会联系。显然，下文是对上文的解释说明，所以选项B正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合句间逻辑。【句意】这个结果完全说得通，因为人类正是因为社会联系而发展的。

【选项释义】A. funny 滑稽的，有趣的 B. simple 简单的，简明的C. logical 合乎逻辑的 D. rare 稀有的【考查点】形容词辨析与语义衔接。【解题思路】空格前的that表示程度，同时还指代冒号后面的信息。上一句解释了“有交谈的旅途更加愉快”这一道理，本句进一步说到“与陌生人交谈让人感受到与社会的联系”，这一点是简单易懂的。因此B选项simple符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】事实很简单：和陌生人交谈能让你感到与他人有联系。

questions.article:
A new study suggests that contrary to most surveys, people are actually more stressed at home than at work. Researchers measured people’s cortisol, which is a stress marker, while they were at work and while they were at home and found it higher at what is supposed to be a place of refuge.
“Further contradicting conventional wisdom, we found that women as well as men have lower levels of stress at work than at home,” writes one of the researchers, Sarah Damaske. In fact women even say they feel better at work, she notes. “It is men, not women, who report being happier at home than at work.” Another surprise is that findings hold true for both those with children and without, but more so for nonparents. This is why people who work outside the home have better health.
What the study doesn’t measure is whether people are still doing work when they’re at home, whether it is household work or work brought home from the office. For many men, the end of the workday is a time to kick back. For women who stay home, they never get to leave the office. And for women who work outside the home, they often are playing catch-up-with-household tasks. With the blurring of roles, and the fact that the home front lags well behind the workplace in making adjustments for working women, it’s not surprising that women are more stressed at home.
But it’s not just a gender thing. At work, people pretty much know what they’re supposed to be doing: working, marking money, doing the tasks they have to do in order to draw an income. The bargain is very pure: Employee puts in hours of physical or mental labor and employee draws out life-sustaining moola.
On the home front, however, people have no such clarity. Rare is the household in which the division of labor is so clinically and methodically laid out. There are a lot of tasks to be done, there are inadequate rewards for most of them. Your home colleagues-your family-have no clear rewards for their labor; they need to be talked into it, or if they’re teenagers, threatened with complete removal of all electronic devices. Plus, they’re your family. You cannot fire your family. You never really get to go home from home.
So it’s not surprising that people are more stressed at home. Not only are the tasks apparently infinite, the co-workers are much harder to motivate.
1.According to Paragraph 1, most previous surveys found that home（ ）.
2.According to Damaske, who are likely to be the happiest at home?
3.The blurring of working women's roles refers to the fact that （ ）.
4.The word "moola" (Paragraph 4) most probably means （ ）.
5.The home front differs from the workplace in that（ ）.

options.A: was an unrealistic place for relaxation
options.B: generated more stress than the workplace
options.C: was an ideal place for stress measurement
options.D: offered greater relaxation than the workplace

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Paragraph 1, most previous surveys found that home _____.	根据第一段，大多数以前的研究发现家_____。A. was an unrealistic place for relaxation	A. 不是休闲放松的理想场所B. generated more stress than the workplace	B. 与工作场所相比，产生了更多压力C. was an ideal place for stress measurement	C. 是测量压力的理想场所D. offered greater relaxation than the workplace	D. 与工作场所相比，提供了更多的休闲【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到首段第一句“一项新的研究（A new study）表明，与大多数调查相反（contrary），实际上人们在家里比在工作中更有压力（more stressed）”，由此可知，正确答案与“新的研究”得出的结论相反，也就是说，选项D正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】According to Damaske, who are likely to be the happiest at home? 在Damaske看来，谁在家里最快乐？A. Working mothers. A. 工作中的母亲。B. Childless husbands. B. 没有孩子的丈夫。C. Childless wives. C. 没有孩子的妻子。D. Working fathers. D. 工作中的父亲。【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干中关键词Damaske定位到文章第二段，第三句说到“在家里，男人比女人更幸福It is men, not women, who report being happier at home than at work”，下面第四句接着说道“more so for nonparents”，也就是说没孩子的男人在家会更加感觉幸福。因此B选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项都不符合原文，属于曲解原文。

options.A: they are both bread winners and housewives
options.B: their home is also a place for kicking back
options.C: there is often much housework left behind
options.D: it is difficult for them to leave their office

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The blurring of working women’s roles refers to the fact that _____. 职场女性的模糊角色指的是_____。A. they are both bread winners and housewives A. 她们既要在职场打拼又要照顾家庭B. their home is also a place for kicking back B. 家也是她们休息的地方C. there is often much housework left behind C. 有太多家务等着她们去做D. it is difficult for them to leave their office D. 她们很难离开办公室【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】题干关键词出现在第三段最后一句，但是没有答案，所以要定位到上文“但对在外工作的女性来说，离开办公室之后，还有很多的家务（catch-up-with-household）等着她们去做”，由此可推断出，选项A符合原文。【干扰项排除】C选项属于以偏概全了，模糊角色还包括“工作”；B、D选项不符合原文，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】The word moola (Paragraph 4) most probably means _____. 单词“moola”（第四段）的意思是_____。A. energy A. 能量B. skills B. 技能C. earnings C. 收入D. nutrition D. 营养【考查点】词义推测题。【解题思路】句中表述的就是一种交易关系“bargain”，前面部分是“员工投入数小时的体力或脑力劳动”，后面是“获得维持生活的……”。很容易推知，moola必然与“薪酬”相关。C选项earnings符合词义。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合词义，属于曲解原文。

options.A: home is hardly a cozier working environment
options.B: division of labor at home is seldom clear-cut
options.C: household tasks are generally more motivating
options.D: family labor is often adequately rewarded

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The home front differs from the workplace in that _____. 家庭和工作场所的差别在于_____。A. home is hardly a cozier working environment A. 家不是舒适的工作环境B. division of labor at home is seldom clear-cut B. 在家里，并无明确分工C. household tasks are generally more motivating C. 家务通常更具激励性D. family labor is often adequately rewarded D. 家务通常可以得到充分奖励【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到倒数第二段第二句“（在家）很少能像工作中那样按部门、系统地进行分工（division of labor）”，由此可知选项B符合原文。【干扰项排除】A、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“家务通常可以得到充分奖励”，与原文“inadequate rewards”不符，属于曲解原文。【参考译文】一项新的研究表明，与大多数调查相反，人们实际上在家里比在工作时压力更大。研究人员测量了人们在工作和在家时的皮质醇（一种压力标志物），发现在本应是避难所的地方，人们的皮质醇水平更高。研究人员之一萨拉·达姆斯克写道：“进一步与传统观点相悖的是，我们发现女性和男性在工作时的压力水平都低于在家时。”她指出，事实上，女性甚至说她们在工作时感觉更好。“报告称，在家比工作更快乐的是男性，而不是女性。”另一个令人惊讶的现象是，研究结果对有孩子的人和没有孩子的人都适用，但对没有孩子的人更适用。这就是为什么外出工作的人健康状况更好的原因。这项研究没有衡量的是，人们在家时是否仍在工作，无论是家务劳动还是从办公室带回家的工作。对许多男性来说，工作日结束时是放松的时候。对于待在家里的女性来说，她们从未离开过办公室。而对于在外工作的女性来说，她们往往要在家务活上迎头赶上。随着角色的模糊化，以及家庭阵线在为职业女性做出调整方面远远落后于工作场所，女性在家中压力更大也就不足为奇了。但这不仅仅是性别问题。在工作中，人们几乎都知道自己应该做什么：工作、赚钱、完成为获得收入而必须完成的任务。交易是非常纯粹的：雇员付出数小时的体力或脑力劳动，雇员获得维持生命的收入。然而，在家庭方面，人们却没有这样的清晰度。很少有一个家庭的分工是如此严谨而有条不紊。需要完成的任务很多，但大部分任务的回报却不足。你的家庭同事——你的家人——对他们的劳动没有明确的奖励；他们需要被说服，或者如果他们是青少年，就会被威胁完全拿走所有的电子设备。另外，他们是你的家人。你不能解雇你的家人。你永远无法从家里真正回家。因此，人们在家压力更大也就不足为奇了。不仅工作显然是无限的，同事也更难激励。【出处】《时代周刊》2014.5.22

questions.article:
For years, studies have found that first-generation college students—those who do not have a parent with a college degree-lag other students on a range of education achievement factors. Their grades are lower and their dropout rates are higher. But since such students are most likely to advance economically if they succeed in higher education, colleges and universities have pushed for decades to recruit more of them. This has created “a paradox” in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has “continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close” an achievement gap based on social class, according to the depressing beginning of a paper forthcoming in the journal Psychological Science.
But the article is actually quite optimistic, as it outlines a potential solution to this problem, suggesting that an approach (which involves a one-hour, next-to-no-cost program) can close 63 percent of the achievement gap (measured by such factors as grades) between first-generation and other students.
The authors of the paper are from different universities, and their findings are based on a study involving 147 students (who completed the project) at an unnamed private university. First generation was defined as not having a parent with a four-year college degree. Most of the first-generation students (59.1 percent) were recipients of Pell Grants, a federal grant for undergraduates with financial need, while this was true only for 8.6 percent of the students with at least one parent with a four-year degree.
Their thesis—that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact—was based on the view that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge about how to deal with the issues that face most college students. They cite past research by several authors to show that this is the gap that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap.
Many first-generation students “struggle to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the ‘rules of the game,’ and take advantage of college resources,” they write. And this becomes more of a problem when collages don’t talk about the class advantage and disadvantages of different groups of students. Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experience, many first-generation students lack sight about why they are struggling and do not understand how students like them can improve.
1.Recruiting more first-generation students has （ ）.
2.The authors of the research article are optimistic because （ ）.
3.The study suggests that most first-generation students （ ）.
4.The author of the paper believe that first-generation students （ ）.
5.We may infer from the last paragraph that（ ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Recruiting more first-generation students has _____.	招录更多的“初代”大学生已经_____。A. reduced their dropout rates	A. 降低了辍学率B. narrowed the achievement gap	B. 缩小了成就差距C. missed its original purpose	C. 偏离了最初目的D. depressed college students	D. 使大学生沮丧【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】由题干定位文章第一段第三句“一旦这样的学生成功地接受了高等教育，他们在经济上更有可能成功”，以及下文第四句“这产生了一种矛盾（a paradox）：招收更多的‘初代’学生，但眼睁睁看着他们失败”，由此可知，结果与原本的设想或目的是相违背的。因此C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“降低了辍学率”，与原文“their dropout rates are higher辍学率更高”不符，属于反向干扰；B选项“缩小了成就差距”，与原文“rather than close an achievement gap而不是缩小成就差距”不符，属于反向干扰；D选项“使大学生沮丧”，原文第一段提到的是“depressing beginning of a paper文章开头不乐观”，该选项属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The author of the research article are optimistic because _____. 论文作者们很乐观是因为_____。A. the problem is solvable A. 此问题能解决B. their approach is costless B. 他们的方式没有任何成本C. the recruiting rate has increased C. 招生率已经提升了D. their findings appeal to students D. 他们的发现对学生有吸引力【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到原文第二段首句“事实上，这篇文章相当乐观（optimistic），因为（as）它简要列举了这个问题的潜在解决方式（solution）”，由此可知，选项A符合原文。【干扰项排除】B选项“他们的方式没有任何成本”，与原文“next-to-no-cost”不符，属于反向干扰；C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.C: are in need of financial support

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The study suggests that most first-generation students _____. 研究显示大多数“初代”学生_____。A. study at private universities A. 在私立学校求学B. are from single-parent families B. 来自单亲家庭C. are in need of financial support C. 需要经济资助D. have failed their college D. 大学过得失败【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到第三段最后一句“大多数（59.1%）‘初代’大学生都接受了旨在为有需要的学生提供经济资助（financial need）的佩尔助学金，而对父母中有人完成本科教育的其他学生而言，这一数据仅维持在8.6%”，也就是说，“初代”大学生接受了经济资助，选项C符合。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: are actually indifferent to the achievement gap
options.B: can have a potential influence on other students
options.C: may lack opportunities to apply for research projects
options.D: are inexperienced in handling their issues at college

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author of the paper believe that first-generation students _____. 论文作者认为“初代”学生_____。A. are actually indifferent to the achievement gap A. 实际上毫不关心成就差距B. can have a potential influence on other students B. 能潜移默化影响其他学生C. may lack opportunities to apply for research projects C. 可能缺少申请研究项目的机会D. are inexperienced in handling their issues at college D. 欠缺处理大学中出现问题的经验【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到原文第四段第一句“‘初代’大学生最欠缺的不是潜力，而是如何处理大多数学生面对问题（deal with the issues that face most college students）的实际能力（practical knowledge）上”，也就是选项D的内容。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: universities often reject the culture of the middle-class
options.B: students are usually to blame for their lack of resources
options.C: social class greatly helps enrich educational experiences
options.D: colleges are partly responsible for the problem in question

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】We may infer from the last paragraph that _____. 从最后一段可推断出_____。A. universities often reject the culture of the middle-class A. 大学经常抵制中产阶级文化B. students are usually to blame for their lack of resources B. 学生经常因缺少资源而受责备C. social class greatly helps enrich educational experiences C. 社会阶层极大地有助于教育经历的丰富D. colleges are partly responsible for the problem in question D. 大学应为探讨中的问题负部分责任【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】本段前面两句提出“初代”学生中存在的与社会阶层有关的问题。第三句以一个“because”所引导的原因状语从句点明中心，明确了问题的来源是美国高校未承认（seldom acknowledge）社会阶层（social class）对学生教育经历的影响，说明高校应该为出现的问题担负部分责任，因此选项D正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。【参考译文】多年来，研究发现，第一代大学生——那些父母没有大学文凭的学生——在一系列教育成就因素上落后于其他学生。他们的成绩较低，辍学率较高。但是，由于这些学生如果在高等教育中取得成功，就最有可能在经济上得到提升，因此，几十年来，高校一直在努力招收更多这样的学生。即将发表在《心理科学》杂志上的一篇论文令人沮丧的开头指出，这就产生了一个“悖论”，即招收第一代学生，但又眼睁睁地看着他们中的许多人失败，这意味着高等教育“继续复制和扩大，而不是缩小基于社会阶层的成绩差距”。但这篇文章实际上相当乐观，因为它概述了解决这一问题的潜在方案，提出一种方法（涉及一个小时、几乎不花钱的课程）可以缩小第一代学生与其他学生之间63%的成绩差距（以成绩等因素衡量）。该论文的作者来自不同的大学，他们的研究结果基于一项涉及一所未具名私立大学147名学生（完成了该项目）的研究。第一代学生的定义是父母中没有一方拥有四年制大学学位。大多数第一代学生（59.1%）都是佩尔助学金的获得者，这是一种为有经济需求的本科生提供的联邦助学金，而在父母至少有一方拥有四年制学位的学生中，这一比例仅为8.6%。他们的论点是，相对适度的干预可能会产生很大的影响，这是基于这样一种观点，即第一代学生最缺乏的可能不是潜力，而是如何处理大多数大学生所面临问题的实用知识。他们引用了几位作者过去的研究，说明要缩小成绩差距，就必须缩小这种差距。他们写道，许多第一代学生“很难适应高等教育的中产阶级文化、学习‘游戏规则’和利用大学资源”。如果高校不讨论不同学生群体的阶级优势和劣势，问题就会更加严重。由于美国高校很少承认社会阶层如何影响学生的教育经历，很多第一代学生不知道自己为什么会陷入困境，也不明白像他们这样的学生如何才能进步。【出处】《Inside Higher Ed》2014.2.16

questions.article:
Even in traditional offices, “the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago,” said Harvard Business School professor Nancy Koehn. She started spinning off examples. “If you and I parachuted back to Fortune 500 companies in 1990, we would see much less frequent use of terms like journey, mission, passion. There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives, but we didn’t talk about energy; we didn’t talk about passion.”
Koehn pointed out that this new era of corporate vocabulary is very “team—-oriented—and not by coincidence. “Let’s not forget sports—in male—dominated corporate America, it’s still a big deal. It’s not explicitly conscious; it’s the idea that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together. There are lots and lots of CEOs in very different companies, but most think of themselves as coaches and this is their team and they want to win.”
These terms are also intended to infuse work with meaning-and, as Khurana points out, increase allegiance to the firm. “You have the importation of terminology that historically used to be associated with non-profit organizations and religious organizations: Terms like vision, values, passion, and purpose,” said Khurana.
This new focus on personal fulfillment can help keep employees motivated amid increasingly loud debates over work-life balance. The “mommy wars” of the 1990s are still going on today, prompting arguments about why women still can’t have it all and books like Sheryl Sandberg’s Lean In, whose title has become a buzzword in its own right. Terms like unplug, offline, life-hack, bandwidth, and capacity are all about setting boundaries between the office and the home. But if your work is your “passion,” you’ll be more likely to devote yourself to it, even if that means going home for dinner and then working long after the kids are in bed.
But this seems to be the irony of office speak: Everyone makes fun of it, but managers love it, companies depend on it, and regular people willingly absorb it. As Nunberg said, “You can get people to think it’s nonsense at the same time that you buy into it.” In a workplace that’s fundamentally indifferent to your life and its meaning, office speak can help you figure out how you relate to your work-and how your work defines who you are.
1.According to Nancy Koehn, office language has become （ ）.
2.“Team”-oriented corporate vocabulary is closely related to （ ）.
3.Khurana believes that the importation of terminology aims to （ ）.
4.It can be inferred that Lean In （ ）.
5.Which of the following statements is true about office speak?

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to Nancy Koehn, office language has become _____.	在Nancy Koehn看来，办公用语已经变得_____。A. more emotional	A. 更具感情B. more objective	B. 更加客观C. less energetic	C. 缺少能量D. less strategic	D. 缺少策略【考查点】观点态度题。【解题思路】本文首段首句就出现了Nancy Koehn的观点，office language即文中的“the lingua franca of corporate America”。Nancy Koehn教授认为美国企业的之常用语相对于20年前变得“much more emotional（更感性）”和“much more right-brained（更加右脑化）”，A选项符合原文。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项都不是办公用语的特点，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】“Team”-oriented corporate vocabulary is closely related to _____. 以“团队”为导向的办公通用语和_____密切相关。A. historical incidents A. 历史事件B. gender difference B. 性别差异C. sports culture C. 体育文化D. athletic executive D. 运动行政【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到第二段二、三、四句，这几句话都是举例子论证第一句话“以‘团队’为导向”，第二句话中出现了关键词“sports”，第三句话中出现了“coach”和“team”，第四句话中出现了“coach”和“team”、“win”，这些词都与运动有关，所以选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】Khurana believes that the importation of terminology aims to _____. Khurana认为引进术语是为了_____。A. revive historical terms A. 振兴过去使用的术语B. promote company image B. 提升公司形象C. foster corporate cooperation C. 促进公司合作D. strengthen employee loyalty D. 加强员工忠诚度【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到原文第三段第一句“正如另一个教授Khurana指出的那样，这些用语旨在赋予工作更多的意义，增强员工的忠诚度（allegiance）”，由此可知，选项D符合原文。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】It can be inferred that Lean In _____. 可以推断出Lean In一书_____。A. voices for working women A. 为职业女性申辩B. appeals to passionate workaholics B. 吸引了激昂的工作狂C. triggers debates among mommies C. 在妈咪之间引发了争论D. praises motivated employees D. 赞扬积极的员工【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到第四段第二句“源于20世纪90年代的‘妈妈战争’，在今天仍然持续。这就引发了一系列为何女性不能拥有一切的争论，还催生了Sheryl Sandberg的书（Lean In）”，由此可知，这本书肯定与女性相关，另外，本段第一句提到了“work-life balance”，结合可推断这句话谈论的是工作中对于女性的不公平，选项A正确。【干扰项排除】C选项“在妈咪之间引发了争论”，与原文表达的“争论催生出Lean In”不符，属于因果倒置，曲解原文；B、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: Managers admire it but avoid it
options.B: Linguists believe it to be nonsense
options.C: Companies find it to be fundamental
options.D: Regular people mock it but accept it

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following statements is true about office speak? 关于办公用语，下列哪个选项是正确的？A. Managers admire it but avoid it. A. 经理们喜欢但却避免使用。B. Linguists believe it to be nonsense. B. 语言学家认为其一无是处。C. Companies find it to be fundamental. C. 公司认为其是基础。D. Regular people mock it but accept it. D. 普通大众虽戏谑，但却接受了。【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干定位到最后一段首句“看起来，这对办公用语极具讽刺意味（irony）：每个人都对它嘲笑不止（makes fun of it），但经理层们却喜欢它，公司发展也取决于它，普通人（regular people）也甘愿接受（absorb）它”，由此可知，选项D符合原文。【干扰项排除】A选项“经理们喜欢但却避免使用”，与原文经理的态度不符，属于曲解原文；B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。哈佛商学院教授南希·科恩说：“即使在传统的办公室里，美国企业的通用语言也比20年前变得更加情绪化，更加右脑化。”她开始举例说明。“如果你和我空降回到1990年的《财富》500强企业，我们会发现旅程、使命、激情等术语的使用频率要低得多。我们有目标、有战略、有目的，但我们不谈能量；我们不谈激情。”科恩指出，新时代的企业词汇非常“以团队为导向”，这并非巧合。“我们不要忘记，在男性占主导地位的美国企业中，体育仍然是一件大事。这不是明确的意识，而是我是教练，你们是我的团队，我们在一起的理念。有很多不同公司的首席执行官，但大多数都认为自己是教练，这是他们的团队，他们想要赢。这些术语还旨在为工作注入意义，正如库拉纳指出的那样，增加对公司的忠诚度。“你会输入一些历史上与非营利组织和宗教组织相关的术语：比如愿景、价值观、激情和目的，”库拉纳说。在关于工作与生活平衡的争论日益激烈的情况下，这种对个人成就感的新关注有助于保持员工的积极性。20世纪90年代的“妈妈之战”至今仍在继续，引发了关于为什么女性仍然不能拥有一切的争论，以及像谢丽尔·桑德伯格的《向前一步》这样的书籍，其书名本身已成为一个热门词汇。拔掉插头、离线、生活窍门、带宽和能力等术语都是关于在办公室和家庭之间划定界限。但是，如果你的工作是你的“激情”所在，你就更有可能全身心地投入其中，即使这意味着回家吃晚饭，然后在孩子们上床睡觉后长时间工作。但这似乎是对办公室语言的讽刺：每个人都在嘲笑它，但管理者喜欢它，公司依赖它，普通人也心甘情愿地吸收它。正如纽伯格所说：“你可以让人们认为这是无稽之谈，同时你也会对它深信不疑。在一个从根本上对你的生活及其意义漠不关心的工作场所，办公室用语可以帮助你弄清你与工作的关系，以及你的工作是如何定义你的。【出处】《大西洋》2014.4.24

questions.article:
Many people talked of the 288,000 new jobs the Labor Department reported for June, along with the drop in the unemployment rate to 6.1 percent, as good news. And they were right. For now it appears the economy is creating jobs at a decent pace. We still have a long way to go to get back to full employment, but at least we are now finally moving forward at a faster pace.
However, there is another important part of the jobs picture that was largely overlooked. There was a big jump in the number of people who repot voluntarily working part-time. This figure is now 830,000 (4.4 percent) above its year ago level.
Before explaining the connection to the Obamacare, it is worth making an important distinction. Many people who work part-time jobs actually want full-time jobs. They take part-time work because this is all they can get. An increase in involuntary part-time work is evidence of weakness in the labor market and it means that many people will be having a very hard time making ends meet.
There was an increase in involuntary part-time in June, but the general direction has been down. Involuntary part-time employment is still far higher than before the recession, but it is down by 640,000 (7.9 percent) from is year ago level.
We know the difference between voluntary and involuntary part-time employment because people tell us. The survey used by the Labor Department asks people if they worked less than 35 hours in the reference week. If the answer is “yes”, they are classified as worked less than 35 hours in that week because they wanted to work less than full time or because they had no choice. They are only classified as voluntary part-time workers if they tell the survey taker they chose to work less than 35 hours a week.
The issue of voluntary part-time relates to Obamacare because one of the main purposes was to allow people to get insurance outside of employment. For many people, especially those with serious health conditions or family members with serious health conditions, before Obamacare the only way to get insurance was through a job that provided health insurance.
However, Obamacare has allowed more than 12 million people to either get insurance through Medicaid or the exchanges. These are people who may previously have felt the need to get a full-time job that provided insurance in order to cover themselves and their families. With Obamacare there is no longer a link between employment and insurance.
1.Which part of the jobs picture are neglected?
2.Many people work part-time because they （ ）.
3.Involuntary part-time employment in the US （ ）.
4.It can be learned that with Obamacare, （ ）.
5.The text mainly discusses（ ）.

options.A: The prospect of a thriving job market.
options.B: The increase of voluntary part-time market.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which part of the jobs picture are neglected?	工作中的哪一部分被忽略掉了？A. The prospect of a thriving job market.	A. 用工市场的繁荣前景。B. The increase of voluntary part-time market.	B. 自愿兼职工作数量的增加。C. The possibility of full employment.	C. 全民就业的可能性。D. The acceleration of job creation.	D. 加速提供工作岗位。【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】定位文章第二段一、二句“然而，我们忽略（overlooked）了工作中的另一重要部分：报告表明，兼职就业（working part-time）的人数大幅度增加了”，由此可知，选项B正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项都不是被忽略的部分，与题干无关，属于曲解原文。

options.A: prefer part-time jobs to full-time jobs.
options.B: feel that is enough to make ends meet.
options.C: cannot get their hands on full-time jobs.
options.D: haven't seen the weakness of the market.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Many people work part-time because they _____. 许多人从事兼职工作是因为他们_____。A. prefer part-time jobs to full-time jobs A. 与全职工作相比，更喜欢兼职工作B. feel that is enough to make ends meet B. 感觉这足以平衡他们的开支C. cannot get their hands on full-time jobs C. 不能获取全职工作D. haven’t seen the weakness of the market D. 未能发现市场的疲软【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干定位到文章第三段第三句“They take part-time work because this is all they can get”，原因是“他们只能找到这样的工作”，也就是他们无法找到全职工作。对应C选项内容。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: is harder to acquire than one year ago.
options.B: shows a general tendency of decline.
options.C: satisfies the real need of the jobless.
options.D: is lower than before the recession.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Involuntary part-time employment in the US _____. 美国的非自愿兼职工作_____。A. is harder to acquire than one year ago A. 与一年前相比，更难获得B. shows a general tendency of decline B. 总体呈现下降的趋势C. satisfies the real need of the jobless C. 满足了失业者真正的需求D. is lower than before the recession D. 人数比经济衰退前更少【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】由题干定位到文章第四段第一句“6月，非自愿兼职（involuntary part-time）工作者的人数是增加了，但总体方向仍是下降的（general direction has been down）”，由此可知，选项B符合原文。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: it is no longer easy for part-timers to get insurance
options.B: employment is no longer a precondition to get insurance
options.C: it is still challenging to get insurance for family members
options.D: full-time employment is still essential for insurance

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be learned that with Obamacare, _____. 可以得知Obamacare _____。A. it is no longer easy for part-timers to get insurance A. 加大了兼职工作者获取保险的难度B. employment is no longer a precondition to get insurance B. 就业与否不再是获取保险的前提条件C. it is still challenging to get insurance for family members C. 使得家庭成员获取保险仍有难度D. full-time employment is still essential for insurance D. 全职就业仍是获取保险的关键【考查点】事实细节题。【解题思路】根据题干定位最后一段最后一句“奥巴马医改Obamacare让就业和保险之间的关系不复存在了（no longer a link between employment and insurance）”，也就是选项B的内容。【干扰项排除】A选项“加大了兼职工作者获取保险的难度”，与原文最后一段“奥巴马医改正是为了使兼职员工也有可能获得保险”不符，属于曲解原文；C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项“全职就业仍是获取保险的关键”，与原文“就业和保险之间的关系不复存在”不符，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】The text mainly discusses _____. 这篇文章主要谈论了_____。A. employment in the US A. 美国的就业情况B. part-timer classification B. 兼职工作的分类C. insurance through Medicaid C. 医疗救济保险D. Obamacare’s trouble D. 奥巴马医改的困境【考查点】主旨大意题。【解题思路】文章第一、二段就以转折递进讲述了就业形势的一个重要方面被忽略，下文围绕这个方面展开，因此本文主要谈论的是美国就业方面的问题。【干扰项排除】B选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项原文提到了一点，属于以偏概全；D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。【参考译文】许多人认为，美国劳工部公布的6月份新增28.8万个就业岗位以及失业率降至6.1%是个好消息。他们是对的。目前看来，经济正在以不错的速度创造就业机会。要恢复充分就业，我们还有很长的路要走，但至少我们现在终于在以更快的速度向前迈进。然而，就业形势的另一个重要部分在很大程度上被忽视了。自愿从事兼职工作的人数大幅增加。目前，这一数字比去年同期高出83万（4.4%）。在解释与奥巴马医改的关系之前，值得做一个重要的区分。许多从事兼职工作的人实际上都希望获得全职工作。他们从事兼职工作是因为他们只能得到这份工作。非自愿兼职工作的增加证明劳动力市场疲软，这意味着许多人将很难维持生计。6月份非自愿兼职人数有所增加，但总体趋势是下降的。非自愿兼职就业人数仍远远高于经济衰退前的水平，但比去年同期减少了64万人（7.9%）。我们之所以知道自愿和非自愿兼职就业之间的区别，是因为人们告诉了我们。劳工部使用的调查询问人们，他们在参考周内的工作时间是否少于35小时。如果答案是“是”，他们就被归类为在该周工作少于35小时，因为他们想工作少于全职或因为他们别无选择。只有当他们告诉调查员他们选择每周工作少于35小时时，他们才会被归类为自愿兼职工作者。自愿兼职的问题与奥巴马医改有关，因为奥巴马医改的主要目的之一是允许人们在工作之外获得保险。对于许多人来说，特别是那些有严重健康问题或家庭成员有严重健康问题的人，在奥巴马医改之前，获得保险的唯一途径是通过一份提供医疗保险的工作。然而，奥巴马医改使1200多万人可以通过医疗补助计划或交易所获得保险。这些人以前可能觉得有必要找一份提供保险的全职工作，以便为自己和家人提供保障。有了奥巴马医改，就业和保险之间不再有联系。【出处】《United Steelworks》2014.7.15

questions.article:
Unfortunately, life is not a bed of roses. We are going through life facing sad experiences. Moreover, we are grieving various kinds of loss: a friendship, a romantic relationship or a house. Hard times may hold you down at what usually seems like the most inopportune time, but you should remember that they won’t last forever.
When our time of mourning is over, we press forward, stronger with a greater understanding and respect for life. Furthermore, these losses make us mature and eventually move us toward future opportunities for growth and happiness. I want to share these ten old truths I’ve learned along the way.
1. _____
Fear is both useful and harmful. This normal human reaction is used to protect us by signaling danger and preparing us to deal with it. Unfortunately, people create inner barriers with a help of exaggerating fears. My favorite actor Will Smith once said, “Fear is not real. It is a product of thoughts you create. Do not misunderstand me. Danger is very real. But fear is a choice.” I do completely agree that fears are just the product of our luxuriant imagination.
2. _____
If you are surrounded by problems and cannot stop thinking about the past, try to focus on the present moment. Many of us are weighed down by the past or anxious about the future. You may feel guilt over your past, but you are poisoning the present with the things and circumstances you cannot change. Value the present moment and remember how fortunate you are to be alive. Enjoy the beauty of the world around and keep the eyes open to see the possibilities before you. Happiness is not a point of future and not a moment from the past, but a mindset that can be designed into the present.
3. _____
Sometimes it is easy to feel bad because you are going through tough times. You can be easily caught up by life problems that you forget to pause and appreciate the things you have. Only strong people prefer to smile and value their life instead of crying and complaining about something.
4. _____
No matter how isolated you might feel and how serious the situation is, you should always remember that you are not alone. Try to keep in mind that almost everyone respects and wants to help you if you are trying to make a good change in your life, especially your dearest and nearest people. You may have a circle of friends who provide constant good humor, help and companionship. If you have no friends or relatives, try to participate in several online communities, full of people who are always willing to share advice and encouragement.
5. _____
Today many people find it difficult to trust their own opinion and seek balance by gaining objectivity from external sources. This way you devalue your opinion and show that you are incapable of managing your own life. When you are struggling to achieve something important you should believe in yourself and be sure that your decision is the best. You live in your skin, think your own thoughts, have your own values and make your own choices.

options.B: Don't fear responsibility for your life
options.D: Most of your fears are unreal
options.G: There are many things to be grateful for

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】通读此段，可以发现“fear”多次出现，比如“Fear is not real惧怕不是真实的”，“fears are just the product of our luxuriant imagination惧怕只是丰富想象的产物”，由此可知选项D符合本段重点“大多数恐惧并非真实存在”。

options.B: Don't fear responsibility for your life
options.D: Most of your fears are unreal
options.G: There are many things to be grateful for

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段第一句给出了作者的建议“try to focus on the present moment”，在这类文章中显然就是本段的语义重心。本段中还多次出现“the present”，因此标题也必然相关。E选项“思考当下”符合文意。

options.B: Don't fear responsibility for your life
options.D: Most of your fears are unreal
options.G: There are many things to be grateful for

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段第一句讲述面临困难时人容易有怎样的心理；第二句说到困难面前人容易如何作为；第三句对前面两句表述的内容进行否定，并给出作者的建议“困难面前要选择微笑，并要珍惜生活”。结合第二句说到人们容易忘记感激已经拥有的东西，分析选项可知G选项“许多东西需要感恩”符合题意。

options.B: Don't fear responsibility for your life
options.D: Most of your fears are unreal
options.G: There are many things to be grateful for

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段第一句“不管觉得多么孤独，事情多么严重，始终谨记：你不是孤身一人”，这是本段的中心句，选项A“你并非独自一人”与此句表达一致。

options.B: Don't fear responsibility for your life
options.D: Most of your fears are unreal
options.G: There are many things to be grateful for

sub_questions.analysis: 【解题思路】本段最后一句提到“过自己的，想自己的，有自己的价值观，自己做决定”，这是属于作者的建议，选项C“铺就自己独特的人生之路”相符合。

questions.article:
Directions: Translate the following text from English into Chinese. Write your translation on ANSWER SHEET.
Think about driving a route that’s very familiar. It could be your commute to work, a trip into town or the way home. Whichever it is, you know every twist and turn like the back of your hand. On these sorts of trips it’s easy to zone out from the actual driving and pay little attention to the passing scenery. The consequence is that you perceive that the trip has taken less time than it actually has.
This is the well-travelled road effect: people tend to underestimate the time it takes to travel a familiar route.
The effect is caused by the way we allocate our attention. When we travel down a well-known route, because we don’t have to concentrate much, time seems to flow more quickly. And afterwards, when we come to think back on it, we can’t remember the journey well because we didn’t pay much attention to it. So we assume it was shorter.

Suppose your university is going to host a summer camp for high school students. Write a notice to
1) briefly introduce the camp activities, and
2) call for volunteers.
You should write about 100 words on the ANSWER SHEET.
Do not
use your name or the name of your university.
Do not
write your address.

Write an essay based on the following chart. In your writing, you should
1) interpret the chart, and
2) give your comments.
You should write about 150 words.

Thinner isn’t always better. A number of studies have
1
that normal-weight people are in fact at higher risk of some diseases compared to those who are overweight. And there are health conditions for which being over-weight is actually

3

4
