Should a leader strive to be loved or feared? This question, famously posed by Machiavelli, lies at the heart of Joseph Nye’s new book. Mr. Nye, a former dean of the Kennedy School of Government at Harvard and one-time chairman of America’s National Intelligence Council, is best known for promoting the idea of “soft power”, based on persuasion and influence, as a counterpoint to “hard power”, based on coercion（强迫）and force.
Having analyzed the use of soft and hard power in politics and diplomacy in his previous books, Mr. Nye has now turned his attention to the relationship between power and leadership, in both the political and business spheres. Machiavelli, he notes, concluded that “one ought to be both feared and loved, but as it is difficult for the two to go together, it is much safer to be feared than loved." In short, hard power is preferable to soft power. But modern leadership theorists have come to the opposite conclusion.
The context of leadership is changing, they observe, and the historical emphasis on hard power is becoming outdated. In modern companies and democracies, power is increasingly diffused and traditional hierarchies （等级制） are being undermined, making soft power ever more important. But that does not mean coercion should now take a back seat to persuasion, Mr. Nye argues. Instead, he advocates a synthesis of these two views. The conclusion of The Powers to Lead, his survey of the theory of leadership, is that a combination of hard and soft power, which he calls “smart power”, is the best approach.
The dominant theoretical model of leadership at the moment is, apparently, the “transformational leadership pattern ”. Anyone allergic （反感）to management term will already be running for the exit, but Mr. Nye has performed a valuable service in rounding up and summarizing the various academic studies and theories of leadership into a single, slim volume. He examines different approaches to leadership, the morality of leadership and how the wider context can determine the effectiveness of a particular leader. There are plenty of anecdotes and examples, both historical and contemporary, political and corporate.
Alas, leadership is a slippery subject, and as he depicts various theories, even Mr. Nye never quite nails the jelly to the wall. He is at his most interesting when discussing the moral aspects of leadership—in particular, the question of whether it is sometimes necessary for good leaders to lie—and he provides a helpful 12-point summary of his conclusions. A recurring theme is that as circumstances change, different sorts of leaders are required； a leader who thrives in one environment may struggle in another, and vice versa. Ultimately that is just a fancy way of saying that leadership offers no easy answers.
1.From the first two paragraphs we may learn that Mr. Machiavelli’s idea of hard power is （  ）.
2.Which of the following makes soft power more important today according to Mr.Nye?
3.In his book The Powers to Lead, Mr. Nye has examined all the following aspects of leadership EXCEPT
（  ）.
4.Mr. Nye’s book is particularly valuable in that it
（  ）.
5.According to the author, the most interesting part of Mr. Nye’s book lies in his （  ）.

options.D: contrary to that of modern leadership theorists

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】From the first two paragraphs we may learn that Mr. Machiavelli’s idea of hard power is____.	从前两段我们可以了解到马基雅维利先生对硬权利的看法____。A. well accepted by Joseph Nye	A. 被约瑟夫·奈接受B. very influential till nowadays	B. 至今仍很有影响力C. based on sound theories	C. 基于可靠的理论D. contrary to that of modern leadership theorists	D. 与现代领导理论家的观点相反【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】阅读前两段，我们可以把答案信息定位到第二段第二句。该句详述了马基雅维利的观点。第三句对他的观点进行总结“hard power is preferable to soft power”表明硬权利比软权利更可取。第四句则紧随其后，指出现代领导理论家提出了截然相反的观点。由此可知，关于硬权利，马基雅维利的观点与现代领导理论家的观点相反，因此D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C在前两段没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】Which of the following makes soft power more important today according to Mr. Nye? 奈先生认为，以下哪一项使软权利在今天更重要？A. Coercion is widespread. A. 威压普遍存在。B. Morality is devalued. B. 道德是贬值的。C. Power is no longer concentrated. C. 权力不再集中。D. Traditional hierarchies are strengthened. D. 传统的等级制度得到了加强。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干中soft power more important可定位到第三段第二句。此句意思为：……权力在日益分散，传统的等级制度逐渐瓦解，这些都使软权利变得越来越重要。即权力的日益分散是软权力愈发重要的因素之一。C项中的no longer concentrated与原文中的diffused （分散的）对应。【干扰项排除】A项关键词coercion（威压）在第三段第三句提到：But that does not mean coercion should now take a back seat to persuasion（但这并不意味着威压现在应该退居说服之后），说明威压即硬权利是重要的，因此与题干中软权力更重要相反，属于反向干扰。B项文中提到“the morality of leadership and how the wider context can determine the effectiveness of a particular leader”（领导力的道德性以及如何在更广泛的背景下决定一个特定领导者的有效性）说明morality重要并没有贬值，这里属于反向干扰。D项在第三段第二句中traditional hierarchies（等级制）are being undermined（传统的等级制度正在被破坏）与选项信息相反，属于反向干扰。

【选项释义】In his book The Powers to Lead, Mr. Nye has examined all the following aspects of leadership EXCEPT_____. 在他的著作《领导的权力》中，奈先生考察了领导的以下所有方面，除了_____。A. authority A. 权威，威信B. context B. 背景C. approaches C. 方法，态度D. morality D. 道德【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干中的has examined定位至第四段第三句。分析该句内容可知，奈先生在《领导的权力》一书中examines了三个方面，唯独没有提到authority，故选A。【干扰项排除】B项对应句中的how the wider context can determine the effectiveness of a particular leader；C对应句中的different approaches to leadership；D对应句中的the morality of leadership；

options.A: makes little use of management terms
options.C: serves as an exit for leadership researchers
options.D: sets a model for contemporary corporate leaders

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Mr. Nye’s book is particularly valuable in that it_____. 奈先生的书特别有价值的地方在于_____。A. makes little use of management terms A. 很少使用管理术语B. summarizes various studies concisely B. 简明扼要地总结各种研究C. serves as an exit for leadership researchers C. 作为领导力研究人员的出口D. sets a model for contemporary corporate leaders D. 为当代企业领袖树立榜样【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干中的valuable可定位到第四段第二句。作者在本句中先是对奈先生的书给予了积极的评价，称其为a valuable service，继而以介词in引出了valuable的原因：rounding up and summarizing the various academic studies…into a single, slim volume（将各种涉及领导力的学术研究成果和理论收集整理，并总结成一本短小精悍的书册）。显然，B选项是对原文的合理判断，故正确。【干扰项排除】A项中的management terms（管理术语）可以在第四段第二句找到信息Anyone allergic to management term will already be running for the exit（任何对管理术语反感的人，都已经准备退出了），然后用but引出这本书有价值的地方，推知奈先生的书里有很多管理的术语，选项与文中信息相反，属于反向干扰。C、D选项中的关键词如exit，contemporary corporate虽然在原文中的定位出可以找到，但与题干无关，属于出处错误。

【选项释义】According to the author, the most interesting part of Mr. Nye’s book lies in his_____. 根据作者的说法，奈先生书中最有趣的部分在于他的_____。A. view of changeable leadership A. 可变领导观B. definition of good leadership B. 良好领导的定义C. summary of leadership history C. 领导历史总结D. discussion of moral leadership D. 道德领导探讨【考查点】细节事实题【解题思路】根据题干中的most interesting定位到第五段第二句。该句明确表示：书中探讨领导的道德问题的部分最有意思。D选项是对句中discussing the moral aspects of leadership的同义转述，故正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

questions.article:
Americans don’t like to lose wars. Of course, a lot depends on how you define just what a war is. There are shooting wars-the kind that test patriotism and courage-and those are the kind at which the U.S excels. But other struggles test those qualities too. What else was the Great Depression or the space race or the construction of the railroads? If American indulge in a bit of flag—when the job is done, they earned it.
Now there is a similar challenge. Global warming. The steady deterioration(恶化) of the very climate of this very planet is becoming a war of the first order, and by any measure, the U.S. produces nearly a quarter of the world’s greenhouse gases each year and has stubbornly made it clear that it doesn’t intend to do a whole lot about it. Although 174 nations approved the admittedly flawed Kyoto accords to reduce carbon levels, the U.S. walked away from them. There are vague promises of manufacturing fuel from herbs or powering cars with hydrogen. But for a country that tightly cites patriotism as one of its core values, the U.S. is taking a pass on what might be the most patriotic struggle of all. It’s hard to imagine a bigger fight than one for the survival of a country’s coasts and farms, the health of its people and stability of its economy.
The rub is, if the vast majority of people increasingly agree that climate change is a global emergency, there’s far less agreement on how to fix it. Industry offers its pans, which too often would fix little. Environmentalists offer theirs, which too often amount to native wish lists that could weaken American’s growth. But let’s assume that those interested parties and others will always bent the table and will always demand that their voices be heard and that their needs be addressed. What would an aggressive, ambitious, effective plan look like-one that would leave the U.S. both environmentally safe and economically sound?
Halting climate change will be far harder. One of the more conservative plans for addressing the problem calls for a reduction of 25 billion tons of carbon emissions over the next 52 year. And yet by devising a consistent strategy that mixes and blends pragmatism(实用主义) with ambition, the U.S. can, without major damage to the economy, help halt the worst effects of climate change and ensure the survival of its way of life for future generations. Money will do some of the work, but what’s needed most is will. "I’m not saying the challenge isn’t almost overwhelming," says Fred Krupp. "But this is America, and America has risen to these challenges before."
1.What does the passage mainly discuss?
2.From the last sentence of paragraph 2 we may learn that the survival of a country’s coasts and farms, the health of its people and the stability of its economy is（  ）.
3.Judging from the context, the word "rub"(Line 1, Para.3)probably means
（  ）.
4.What is the author’s attitude toward America’s policies on global warming?
5.The paragraphs immediately following this passage would most probably deal with（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What does the passage mainly discuss?	这篇文章主要讨论了什么？A. Human wars	A. 人类战争B. Economic crisis	B. 经济危机C. America’s environmental policies	C. 美国的环保政策D. Global environment in general	D. 总体全球环境【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】作者在第一段首先指出Americans don’t like to lose wars（美国人不喜欢打败仗），继而在第二段顺势引出a war of the first order（首要的战争）——全球变暖，即环境恶化问题，并阐述了美国在应对全球变暖问题上的错误方针政策。第三、四两段则就美国如何应对全球变暖提出了相关解决方案，即一些有利政策。可见，美国的环境政策是本文讨论的重点，故C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不能通过文章逻辑推理出来。

options.B: a fight no one can win

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】From the last sentence of paragraph 2 we may learn that the survival of a country’s coasts and farms, the health of its people and the stability of its economy is_______. 从第2段的最后一句话我们可以了解到，一个国家的海岸和农场的生存，人民的健康和经济的稳定是_______。A. of utmost importance A. 至关重要的B. a fight no one can win B. 一场没人能赢的战斗C. beyond people’s imagination C. 超出了人们的想象D. a less significant issue D. 一个不那么重要的问题【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干关键词survival of a country’s coasts and farms, the health of its people and the stability of its economy可定位到第二段最后一句。该句的主干是It’s hard to imagine a bigger fight than one（很难想象有比……更重要的战争了），其中one通过for加以解释，即题干中的关键词。言下之意survival of a country’s coasts and farms, the health of its people and the stability of its economy比fight还要重要，则A选项与句意吻合，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B项与只是提到了这是一个难以想象的bigger fight，没有说能不能打赢这场fight，属于曲解原文；C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；D项与正确答案A项完全相反，根据解析可知，这里属于反向干扰。

【选项释义】By Judging from the context, the word “rub” (Line 1, Para.3) probably means_______. 从上下文来看，单词“rub”（第3段第1行）可能意味着_______。A. friction A. 不和；摩擦B. contradiction B. 矛盾C. conflict C. 争执，分歧D. problem D. 问题【考查点】词汇推断题。【解题思路】根据题干可定位到第三段第一句。该句的主干是The rub is，that引导的表语从句是对rub的解释说明。该表语从句中提到：越多人认识到气候变化这一全球危机的急迫性，就越难在解决方案上达成共识。从句的内容无疑向读者展示了一个难以解决的问题。由此推知，rub一词意为“问题”，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C三个选项均为近义词，含有“矛盾，不和”之意，带入文中，不符合原文逻辑。

【选项释义】What is the author’s attitude toward America’s policies on global warming? 作者对美国的全球变暖政策持什么态度？A. Critical A. 批判的B. Indifferent B. 冷漠的C. Supportive C. 支持的D. Compromising D. 妥协的【考查点】观点态度题【解题思路】作者在第二段中提到了美国应对全球变暖所采取的政策，第三、四句中使用了stubbornly，walked away from them和vague promises这些带有负面色彩的词，说明作者对美国政策的不满。且倒数第二句提到解决全球变暖问题是最具爱国意义的战争，美国却拒不参与，可见作者是在批评美国的政策，故A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不可以通过原文逻辑推理出来。

options.A: the new book written by Fred Krupp
options.B: how America can fight against global warming
options.C: the harmful effects of global warming
options.D: how America can tide over economic crisis

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The paragraphs immediately following this passage would most probably deal with_____. 这篇文章后面的段落很可能是关于_____的。A. the new book written by Fred Krupp A. 弗雷德·克鲁普写的新书B. how America can fight against global warming B. 美国如何对抗全球变暖C. the harmful effects of global warming C. 全球变暖的有害影响D. how America can tide over economic crisis D. 美国如何渡过经济危机【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】文章第四段第一句指出遏制全球变暖非常艰难，第三句提出美国可以采用的应对方针，第五、六句引用弗雷德・克鲁普的话表明美国应该有信心战胜这一挑战。所以接下来的段落应是美国战胜此挑战的具体方法。另外，本文属于英文议论文，议论文往往先提出问题，接着分析问题，最后解决问题，因此后面的段落应围绕美国如何解决全球变暖问题展开。综上所述，B选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不能通过文章逻辑推理出来。

questions.article:
With the nation’s financial system teetering （蹒跚）on a cliff, the compensation arrangements for executives of the big banks and other financial firms are coming under close examination again.
Banker’s excessive risk-taking is a significant cause of this financial crisis and has contributed to others in the past. In this case, it was fueled by low interest rates and kept going by a false sense of security created by a debt-fueled bubble in the economy.
Mortgage lenders gladly lent enormous sums to those who could not afford to pay them back, dividing the loans and selling them off to the next financial institution along the chain, which took advantage of the same high-tech securitization （证券化）to load on more risky mortgage-based assets.
Financial regulation will have to catch up with the most irresponsible practices that led banks down in this road, in hopes of averting the next crisis, which is likely to involve different financial techniques and different sorts of assets. But it is worth examining the root problem of compensation schemes that are tied to short-term profits and revenues, and thus encourage bankers to take irresponsible levels of risk.

Directions:
It is known that text message （手机短信）has both advantages and disadvantages. Some people think it is a blessing, while others regard it as a hell. In this section, you are asked to write an essay on text message. You can take either stand and provide specific reasons and examples to support your idea. You should write at least 150 words on ANSWER SHEET 2.
Oil is an important（  ）material which can be processed into many different products, including plastics.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. raw天然的，未加工的	B. bleak 荒凉的C. flexible 灵活的	D. fertile 肥沃的，富饶的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】根据定语从句“which can be processed into many different products”可知，oil是一个可以被加工成其他物品的原材料，A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】石油是一种重要的原材料，可以加工成许多不同的产品，包括塑料。

questions.article: The high living standards of the US cause its present population to（  ）25 percent of the world’s oil.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. assume 假设；认为	B. consume 消耗，消费C. resume 继续；重新开始	D. presume 料想；假设【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】填入的动词应该与oil构成动宾关系，只有B选项consume“消耗”符合题意，表示“消耗石油”【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】美国的高生活水平导致其目前的人口消耗了世界25%的石油。

questions.article: You shouldn’t be so（  ）---I didn’t mean anything bad in what I said.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. sentimental 感性的	B. sensible 理智的，合理的C. sensitive 敏感的	D. sophisticated 老练的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】根据空格后面的回复“I didn’t mean anything bad in what I said.”可知尽管没有说什么坏的话，“you”仍觉得受到了冒犯，所以可以得知“you”是敏感的，故选C。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】你不应该这么敏感——我说的话没有恶意。

questions.article: Picasso was an artist who fundamentally changed the（  ）of art for later generations.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. philosophy 哲学	B. concept 概念，观念C. viewpoint 观点	D. theme 主题【考查点】名词辨析【解题思路】空格的限定是空格后面的所有格，表示“后世艺术的……”因此B选项符合题意，“后世艺术的观念”。【干扰项排除】A、C选项不符合语境；D选项有一定的干扰性，但theme表示主题时尤指（谈话、书籍、电影等的）题目，主题，因此与题目不符，排除。【句意】毕加索是一位从根本上改变了后世艺术观念的艺术家。

questions.article: Member states had the option to（  ）from this agreement with one year’s notice.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. deny否认	B. object 反对C. suspect 怀疑	D. withdraw 退出；撤回【考查点】动词辨析和固定搭配【解题思路】withdraw from 表示“退出”，符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项都不可以和from构成短语，A、C项都是及物动词，object和介词to构成短语，表示“反对”。故排除。【句意】成员国可以选择提前一年通知退出本协定。

questions.article: The two countries achieved some progress in the sphere of trade relations, traditionally a source of （  ）irritation.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. mutual 相互的	B. optional 可选择的C. neutral 中立的	D. parallel 平行的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】空格处用以形容irritation（刺激），并且空格所在的这一个短语是用来修饰前文中的trade relations（贸易关系），众所周知贸易就双方相互进行交易的过程，所以A项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语境。【句意】两国在贸易关系领域取得了一些进展，传统上贸易关系是相互刺激的根源。

questions.article: Williams had not been there during the（  ）moments when the kidnapping had taken place.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. superior 更强的，更好的	B. rigorous 严格的C. vital 至关重要的	D. unique 独特的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】“when the kidnapping had taken place”（当绑架发生的时候）作定语从句来修饰先行词moments，moments又被空格处修饰，所以C选项vital符合这个定语从句表达的含义。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语境。【句意】在绑架发生的关键时刻，威廉姆斯不在现场。

questions.article: Travel around Japan today, and one sees foreign residents a wide（  ）of jobs.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. range 范围	B. field 领域C. scale 规模，范围	D. area 领域【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】考察固定搭配 a wide range of “各种各样的……”【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不能构成a wide…of的短语搭配。【句意】今天环游日本，你会看到外国居民有各种各样的工作。

questions.article: Modern manufacturing has （  ）a global river of materials into a stunning array of new products.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. translated 翻译	B. transformed 转换C. transferred 转让	D. transported 运输【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】空格处与into构成相关的短语，B项transform A into B意为把A转换为B，符合句意，因此B为正确答案。【干扰项排除】C、D不能与into构成短语；A项translate A into B意为把A翻译成B，不符合题目含义，故排除。【句意】现代制造业将一条全球性的物质之河转变为一系列令人惊叹的新产品。

questions.article: Lightning has been the second largest storm killer in the US over the past 40 years and is（  ）only by flood.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. exceeded 超过	B. excelled 胜过，优于C. excluded 排除	D. extended 扩大，延长【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】题目中的second largest storm killer表示空格处进行了一个排位，因此空格处要填入“超过”的含义，故A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B选项虽然有超过的含义，但尤指“to do extremely well and even better than you usually do”（胜过平时），在这里不符合题意，故排除；C、D选项不符合题目的含义，排除。【句意】在过去40年里，闪电一直是美国第二大风暴杀手，仅次于洪水。

questions.article: Voices were（  ）as the argument between the two motorists became more bad-tempered.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. swollen 膨胀，肿胀	B. increased 增加C. developed 发展	D. raised 抬高的【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】空格处动词的发出对象是voice（声音），声音的幅度用提高减弱来形容，故D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项swollen是swell的过去式，表示肿胀，常用来形容物体的外观变化，故不符合逻辑关系；B选项increase表示数量、价值的增多，增加，故不符合逻辑关系；C选项develop表示发展、壮大，常用来形容某物变得更强、更好，故不符合逻辑关系。【句意】随着两个司机之间的争论变得越来越激烈，他们的声音也提高了。

questions.article: Some sufferers will quickly be restored to prefect health,（  ）others will take a longer time.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. which 哪一个	B. where 哪里C. when 什么时候	D. whereas 然而【考查点】从句的连接词用法【解题思路】根据some…others…以及quickly和longer time之间语义的转折判断空格处需要填入表达转折关系的关系词，故D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项都不符合逻辑关系。【句意】一些患者将很快恢复到完美的健康状态，而另一些患者则需要更长的时间。

questions.article: My brother likes eating very much but he isn’t very（  ）about the food he eats.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. special 特别的	B. peculiar 怪异的，奇怪的C. particular 特定的，某一的	D. unusual 不寻常的，独特的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】通过前文的描述，空格处应该表达食物类别的数量，表示“他不是只吃某一特定的食物”故C项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A项special的思是：“特别的”“特殊的”“特设的”“专门的”，着重指某事物具有自己特有的性质、性格或个性，非同一般的，不同寻常的，与同类明显相异，与题意不符，排除；B项peculiar表示“strange or unusual, especially in a way that is unpleasant or worrying”有贬义的意味，排除；D项unusual表示不同于其他物，在这里不符合题意，排除。【句意】我兄弟很喜欢吃，但他对吃的食物不是很挑剔。

questions.article: Britain might still be part of France if it weren’t（  ）a disastrous flood 200.000 years ago, according to scientists from Imperial College in London.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. upon 在……之上	B. with 和……在一起C. in 在……里面	D. for 对于；因为【考查点】介词辨析。【解题思路】空格处所在的条件状语从句的含义为“如果不是……一场200000年前的灾难性的洪水”可知这里要填入表原因的介词，故D项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合题目的含义和逻辑。【句意】伦敦帝国理工学院的科学家表示，如果不是20万年前的一场灾难性洪水，英国可能仍然是法国的一部分。

questions.article: The water prize is an international award that（  ）outstanding contributions towards solving global water problems.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. recognizes 承认，表彰	B. requires 需要C. releases 释放，发行	D. relays 接替，转接【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】that引导定语从句来修饰award（奖），所以A项recognize做表彰的含义是符合题意的。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不符合语义逻辑。【句意】水奖是一项国际奖项，旨在表彰对解决全球水问题做出的杰出贡献。

questions.article: In its 14 years of（  ） , the European Union has earned the scorn of its citizens and skepticism from the United States.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. endurance 忍耐力，持久力	B. emergence 出现，显现C. existence 存在	D. eminence 著名，卓越【考查点】名词辨析【解题思路】根据空格后面的句子的含义“它遭到了本国公民的蔑视和美国的怀疑，”可以推知空格处应该填入存在的含义，故C项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B项虽然含有“存在之意”但着重强调“出现”的动态意为，与14年的长期的，表状态的时间标志词不符，故排除；A、D选项不符合语义逻辑。【句意】在欧盟成立的14年里，它遭到了本国公民的蔑视和美国的怀疑。

questions.article: His excuse for being late this morning was his car had（  ）in the snow.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. started up 开始	B. got stuck 卡住，困住C. set back 推迟	D. stood by 支持【考查点】词组辨析【解题思路】空格处的动词词组的发出者是“his car”，地点状语是“in the snow”，又根据前文提到“他早上迟到的理由是……”推知B项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合语义逻辑。【句意】他今天早上迟到的理由是他的车陷在雪里了。

questions.article: （  ）widespread belief cockroaches (螳螂) would not take over the world if there were no around to step on them.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. In view of 鉴于，考虑到	B. Thanks to 由于，幸亏C. In case of 万一，假设	D. Contrary to 与……相反【考查点】词组辨析【解题思路】空格后的题目含义为“……人们普遍认为，如果没有人来踩蟑螂，蟑螂就不会统治世界，”根据常识，人们看到蟑螂往往会去踩死他们，所以空格处应该填入与widespread belief相反的含义，故D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不符合题目的含义。【句意】与普遍认为的相反的是，如果没有周围的人来踩蟑螂，蟑螂就不会占领世界。

questions.article: Consciously or not, ordinary citizens and government bureaucrats still（  ）the notion that Japanese society is a unique culture.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. fit in with 适应，与……一致	B. look down on 看不起C. cling to 坚持	D. hold back 隐瞒；阻止【考查点】词组辨析【解题思路】空格前后的题目含义为“无论有意无意，普通公民和政府官员仍然……日本社会是一种独特的文化。”推知C项cling to“坚持”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合语义逻辑。【句意】无论有意无意，普通公民和政府官员仍然坚持认为日本社会是一种独特的文化。

questions.article: As you can see by yourself, things（  ）to be exactly as the professor had foreseen.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. turned in 上交，归还	B. turned out 结果是C. turned up 出现	D. turned down 拒绝【考查点】词组辨析【解题思路】根据空格前后文的含义“正如你自己所看到的那样，事情……正如教授所预见的那样。”推知B项符合题意【干扰项排除】C项turn up表出现时，尤指某件物品不见之后突然出现，也表示在规定的时间出现在了规定的地点，与题目逻辑不符，排除；A、D选项不符合语义逻辑。【句意】正如你自己所看到的那样，事情最终完全如教授所预见的那样。

questions.article:
Olympic Games are held every four years at a different site, in which athletes（1）different nations compete against each other in a
（2）of sports. There are two types of Olympics, the Summer Olympics and the Winter Olympics.
In order to（3）the Olympics, a city must submit a proposal to the International Olympic Committee (IOC). After all proposals have been
（4）, the IOC votes. If no city is successful in gaining a majority in the first vote, the city with the fewest votes is eliminated, and voting continues with（5）rounds, until a majority winner is determined. Typically the Games are awarded several years in advance, （6）the winning city time to prepare for the Games. In selecting the（7）of the Olympic Games, the IOC considers a number of factors, chief among them which city has, or promises to build, the best facilities, and which organizing committee seems most likely to（8） the Games effectively.
The IOC also（9）which parts of the world have not yet hosted the Games.
（10）, Tokyo, Japan, the host of the 1964 Summer Games, and Mexico City, Mexico, the host of the 1968 Summer Games, were chosen（11）to popularize the Olympic movement in Asia and in Latin America.
（12）the growing importance of television worldwide, the IOC in recent years has also taken into（13） the host city’s time zone.
（14）the Games take place in the United States or Canada, for example, American television networks are willing to pay（15）higher amounts for television rights because they can broadcast popular events（16）, in prime viewing hours.
（17） the Games have been awarded, it is the responsibility of the local organizing committee to finance them. This is often done with a portion of the Olympic television （18） and with corporate sponsorships, ticket sales, and other smaller revenue sources. In many（19）there is also direct government support.
Although many cities have achieved a financial profit by hosting the Games, the Olympics can be financially（20）. When the revenues from the Games were less than expected, the city was left with large debts.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. in 在……里面	B. for 对于，因为C. of 属于……的	D. from 来自【考查点】句间逻辑【解题思路】athletes（运动员）与空格后different nations（不同的国家）之间的关系。按逻辑推理，本句的意思应该是：来自不同国家的运动员将在各类比赛中竞相角逐。因此D选项from（来自）符合题意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】奥运会每四年在不同的地点举行一次，来自不同国家的运动员在……运动项目中相互竞争。

【选项释义】A. lot 大量 B. number 数量C. variety 种类 D. series 系列【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】根据常识可知，奥运会包含多种不同的比赛项目。C选项variety可构成词组a variety of，意为“各种各样的,”强调种类繁多，表达出了原文中“来自不同国家的运动员将在各类比赛项目中竞相角逐”的意思，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A项a lot of和B项a number of都表示许多的，大量的，本着只有一个正确答案的原则，排除这两项答案；D项a series of表示一系列的，尤指互相有关联的东西，与题意不符，故排除。【句意】奥运会每四年在不同的地点举行一次，来自不同国家的运动员在不同的运动项目中相互竞争。

【选项释义】A. host 主办 B. take 做，采用C. run 管理 D. organize 组织【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】本句的意思是：想要……奥运会，一个城市必须向国际奥委会递交申请。A选项host（主办）与Olympics搭配，意为“举办奥运会”，符合文意及逻辑。也可根据词汇重现进行选择，第三段第一句中的hosted the Games就是一种提示，因此可知A选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B、C、D项可以通过词汇重现进行排除。【句意】为了举办奥运会，一个城市必须向国际奥委会（IOC）提交一份提案。

【选项释义】A. supported 支持 B. submitted 提交C. substituted 替换 D. subordinated 使居次要地位【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】本句的意思是：在所有的申请都……了以后，国际奥委会开始投票表决。B选项submitted意为“提交”，与 have been连用构成被动语态，且与之前的proposals（申请，提案）搭配,表示“待所有的申请被提交了之后……”，符合文意，故为正确答案。本题也属于词汇重现题。此外,空格前一句中提到submit a proposal（提交申请），由此亦可知B选项是正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】所有提案提交后，国际奥委会进行投票。

【选项释义】A. suggestive 暗示的 B. successful 成功的C. successive 连续的 D. succeeding 紧接着的，随后的【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】空格提到“在首轮投票中，倘若没有一个申办城市能够成功获得多数的选票,得票最少的城市将会被淘汰。”后半段提到“然后投票继续进行……，直到确定获得多数票的城市为主办城市。”显然，在这个过程中需要经过连续几轮的投票才能确定当选城市。C选项successive意为“连续的，接连的”，与rounds（回合）搭配合理，符合文意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】如果没有一个城市在第一轮投票中成功获得多数票，得票最少的城市将被淘汰，投票将继续进行连续几轮，直到确定获得多数票的城市。

【选项释义】A. letting 让 B. setting 放，置C. permitting 准许，允许 D. allowing 允许【考查点】上下文语义和近义词辨析【解题思路】从本句结构上判断，空格处的动词后接双宾语，即间接宾语the winning city（获胜城市），直接宾语time（时间）to prepare for the Games（准备奥运会）为time的补语，D项allowing（允许，使能够）不仅可接双宾语，且代入文中的意思“以使获胜城市有时间准备奥运会”，符合上下文逻辑，因此为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B选项不符合题目含义；C项不符合语法规则。【句意】通常情况下，奥运会提前几年授予，让获胜城市有时间准备奥运会。

【选项释义】A. site 地点，场地 B. spot 地点，场所C. location 地点，位置 D. place 地点，场所【考查点】近义词辨析和词汇复现【解题思路】四个选项均有“地方,场所”的意思，而A选项site具体用于表示提供专门用途或特定活动的地点，也可以用来表示事件的发生地，与文章语境相符，故正确。本题也可根据词汇再现进行判断。文章开头就提到Olympic Games are held every four years at a different site，由此也可以确定正确答案为A选项。【干扰项排除】B项spot指相对较小的特定地点或事物所在地；C项location指某物设置的方向或地点；D项place含义广泛，是最普通用词，既可指很小的地点，又可指很大很远的地方或场所。【句意】在选择奥运会场地时，国际奥委会考虑了许多因素，其中主要包括哪个城市拥有或承诺建造最好的设施，以及……

【选项释义】A. state 陈述，声明 B. stage 举办，上演C. start 开始 D. sponsor 赞助【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】根据空格前文提到的信息“国际奥委会选择举办地时考虑的主要因素”因而可以判断出，本句的意思应该是：哪个城市看起来最可能有效地承办奥运会。B选项stage作动词时意为“举办；上演”，符合文意，故B为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不符合逻辑。【句意】在选择奥运会场地时，国际奥委会考虑了许多因素，其中主要包括哪个城市拥有或承诺建造最好的设施，以及哪个组委会最有可能有效地举办奥运会。

【选项释义】A. thinks 认为 B. reckons 认为；估算C. considers 考虑；认为 D. calculates 计算；推测【考查点】近义词辨析和词汇复现【解题思路】根据本句中的副词also（也）可以判断，本段内容是上一段内容的延续。上文中提到了国际奥委会在选择主办城市时所考虑的主要因素，所以本段将继续讨论奥委会考虑的其他因素。由此可以推断出C选项considers（考虑，思考）符合文意要求，故为正确答案。本题也可利用词汇再现进行确定，二段末句the IOC considers a number of factors中的considers起到提示作用，由此也可得出C选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A项think为普通用词，指按照自己的意见提出看法。B项指对人或事作全面“权衡”，把各方面意见考虑进去后得出结论，虽然都有“认为，考虑”之意，但鉴于词汇重现都可以排除；D选项不符合语境，排除。【句意】国际奥委会还考虑了世界上哪些地区尚未举办奥运会。

【选项释义】A. For instance 比如 B. As a result 因此C. In brief 简而言之 D. On the whole 大体上【考查点】词组辨析和句间逻辑【解题思路】上文提到“国际奥委会也会考虑世界上哪些地方还未举办过奥运会”下文紧接着提到日本的东京和墨西哥的墨西哥城，对前文进行举例说明。A选项For instance意为“比如，例如”，符合上下文逻辑关系，因此为正确选项。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】例如，1964年夏季奥运会的东道主日本东京和1968年夏季运动会的东道主墨西哥城被选中……

【选项释义】A. in time 及时 B. in part 部分地，某种程度上地C. in case 万一 D. in common 共同的【考查点】词组辨析和逻辑关系【解题思路】本句所表达的意思是“东京和墨西哥城之所以当选奥运会的主办城市，其目的是为了在亚洲和拉丁美洲推广奥林匹克运动。”这是对本段第一句的举例说明。可见，在亚洲和拉丁美洲推广奥林匹克运动是奥委会最终选择这两个城市所考虑的因素之一，即这两个城市当选的原因之一。B选项In part意为“部分地”，符合文意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】例如，1964年夏季奥运会的东道主日本东京和1968年夏季运动会的东道主墨西哥城被选中，部分原因是为了在亚洲和拉丁美洲推广奥林匹克运动。

【选项释义】A. Since 因为；自从 B. Because 因为C. As for 至于 D. Because of 因为【考查点】关联词辨析和句间逻辑【解题思路】空格后“the growing importance of television worldwide”意为“电视转播在全球变得日益重要”其次“the IOC in recent years has also taken into 33 the host city’s time zone.”是本句的主干部分，意为：国际奥委会也会……主办城市的时区问题。显然，前者应该是后者的原因，而且空格之后是一个名词短语。D选项Because of意为“因为，由于”，可与名词（短语）连用，且符合上下文逻辑关系，所以为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B项虽然表示原因，但语法上只能用于连接句子，故排除；C项不符合原文含义和逻辑。【句意】由于电视在世界范围内的重要性日益增加，国际奥委会近年来……

【选项释义】A. amount 数量 B. account 账户C. accord 协议 D. acclaim 赞扬【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】由本句中的also一词可知，本段的内容仍旧延续上文的话题，而上文涉及国际奥委会在选择主办城市时考虑的诸多因素。由此推断，本句的意思应该是：近年来，国际奥委会也会将主办城市的时区问题纳入考虑因素中。B选项account与空格前的take into构成固定搭配take…into account，意为“考虑，把……纳入考虑之中”。此处为了保持句子的平衡，将take的宾语the host city’s time zone后置。因此B选项符合文意，是正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D不构成固定搭配。【句意】由于电视在世界范围内的重要性日益增加，国际奥委会近年来也考虑到了主办城市的时区。

【选项释义】A. However 然而 B. Whatever 无论什么C. Whenever 无论何时 D. Wherever 无论哪里【考查点】引导词辨析及句间逻辑【解题思路】根据所给的四个选项判断本句的前半句是从句部分，意为“在美国或加拿大举办奥运会，”后半句为主句部分，意思是“美国电视网络乐于支付……更高额的费用购买电视转播权……”由句中的for example可知，本句是对上句的举例。根据上文提到的时差问题，可知此处应填入与时间有关的词。C选项Whenever意为“无论何时；每当”，符合文意，所以为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不符合逻辑关系。【句意】例如，每当奥运会在美国或加拿大举行时，美国电视网络都愿意……

【选项释义】A. greatly 非常地，极大地 B. handsomely 慷慨地；漂亮地C. meaningfully 有目的地 D. significantly 显著地【考查点】副词辨析及句间逻辑【解题思路】根据副词的基本用法可知，空格中所需的副词修饰其后的higher一词，因此该副词应具有修饰形容词比较级的功能。D选项significantly（明显地，相当地，显著地）可用于修饰形容词的比较级。例如：His work has been significantly better this year.（今年他的工作明显较好）。【干扰项排除】A项虽然表示极大地含义，常用于动词或者现在分词前，表示为“very much”，但不可用于修饰比较级；C、B不符合句子含义；【句意】例如，每当奥运会在美国或加拿大举行时，美国电视网络都愿意为电视转播权支付更高的费用……

【选项释义】A. live 活的；现场直播地 B. living 活着的C. alive 活着的 D. lively 活泼的，生动的【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】由空格所在句中的动词broadcast可知，空格处需填副词，修饰broadcast。A选项live可以作副词，意为“现场地，实况地”意为“现场直播”，符合语义逻辑。因此，A选项是正确选项。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项都是形容词逻辑不对应。【句意】例如，每当奥运会在美国或加拿大举行时，美国电视网络都愿意为电视转播权支付更高的费用，因为它们可以在黄金时段直播热门事件。

【选项释义】A. Until 直到……为止 B. Unless 除非C. Whether 是否 D. Once 一旦【考查点】引导词辨析及句间逻辑【解题思路】空格后的意思是“……被授予了主办权，主办城市组委会就有责任为奥运会筹措资金。”根据逻辑推理，前半句应该是后半句的前提或条件，因为如果没有获得主办权，主办城市组委会为奥运会筹措资金的责任也就无从谈起。D选项Once作连词时，表条件，意为“一旦”，符合上下文逻辑关系，故正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】一旦奥运会获奖，当地组委会就有责任为其提供资金。

【选项释义】A. incomes 收入 B. interests 利息C. revenues 收益；税收 D. returns 回报，收益【考查点】近义词辨析【解题思路】由前文的finance（提供资金）可知，本句涉及奥运会的资金来源，这句话意为“除了企业赞助、门票收入和其他小额收入来源，还包括奥运会电视……”C选项revenues主要指政府或企业的收入，代入文章符合文意。本题也可根据词汇再现进行判断。本句最后提到other smaller revenue sources，由此可知，奥运会电视转播也是一种revenue（收入）来源，故可确定C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D可以根据词汇再现进行排除。【句意】这通常是通过奥运会电视收入的一部分以及企业赞助、门票销售和其他较小的收入来源来实现的。

【选项释义】A. cases 情况 B. conditions 条件C. chances 机会 D. circumstances 环境；状况【考查点】近义词辨析【解题思路】根据上文可知，本句表达的是除了上文提到的资金来源，还有政府的直接资助。A选项cases意为“情况”，尤指影响个人或群体的状况和情形，与介词in搭配表示“在……情况下”，符合逻辑，是正确选项。【干扰项排除】C项含义不符，排除；B项condition常常表示身体或者精神上的状况；D项circumstance表示在……情况下时，与under搭配；【句意】在许多情况下，也有直接的政府支持。

【选项释义】A. safe 安全的 B. risky 有风险的C. tempting 诱人的 D. feasible 可行的【考查点】上下文语义【解题思路】根据Although（尽管）引导的让步状语从句可知，后半句（主句部分）的含义应该与前半句（从句部分）所表达的内容是转折关系。前半句的意思是说，许多城市从举办奥运会中获益，那么后文的意思应该与“获益”相反。B选项risky意为“冒险的，有风险的”，符合文意，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均无法与空格前后构成相应逻辑关系。【句意】虽然许多城市通过举办奥运会获得了财务利润，但奥运会在财务上可能存在风险。

questions.article:
Last weekend Kyle MacDonald in Montreal threw a party to celebrate the fact that he got his new home in exchange for a red paper clip. Starting a year ago, MacDonald bartered the clip for increasingly valuable stuff, including a camp stove and free rent in a Phoenix flat. Having announced his aim (the house) in advance, MacDonald likely got a boost from techies eager to see the Internet pass this daring test of its networking power. “My whole motto (座右铭) was ‘Start small, think big, and have fun’, ” says MacDonald, 26, “I really kept my effort on the creative side rather than the business side.”
Yet as odd as the MacDonald exchange was, barter is now big business on the Net. This year more than 400,000 companies worldwide will exchange some $10 billion worth of goods and services on a growing number of barter sites. These Web sites allow companies to trade products for a virtual currency, which they can use to buy goods from other members. In Iceland, garment-maker Kapusalan sells a third of its output on the booming Vidskiptanetid exchange, earning virtual money that it uses to buy machinery and pay part of employee salaries. The Troc-services exchange in France offers more than 4,600 services, from math lessons to ironing.
This is not a primitive barter system. By creating currencies, the Internet removes a major barrier—what Bob Meyer, publisher of Barter News, calls “the double coincidence of wants.” That is, two parties once not only had to find each other, but also an exchange of goods that both desired. Now, they can price the deal in virtual currency.
Barter also helps firms make use of idle capacity. For example, advertising is “hugely bartered” because many media, particularly on the Web can supply new ad space at little cost. Moreover, Internet ads don’t register in industry-growth statistics, because many exchanges are arranged outside the formal exchanges.
Like eBay, most barter sites allow members to “grade” trading partners for honesty quality and so on.. Barter exchanges can allow firms in countries with hyperinflation or nontradable currencies to enter global trades. Next year, a nonprofit exchange called Quick Lift Two (QL2) plans to open in Nairobi, offering barter deals to 38,000 Kenyan farmers in remote areas. Two small planes will deliver the goods. QL2 director Gacii Waciuma says the farmers are excited to be “liberated from corrupt middlemen.” For them, barter evokes a bright future, not a precapitalist past.
1.The word “techies” (Line 4, Para 1) probably refers to those who are（  ）.
2.Many people may have deliberately helped Kyle because they
（  ）.
3.The Internet barter system relies heavily on
（  ）.
4.It is implies that Internet advertisements can help
（  ）.
5.Which of the follow is true of QL2 according to the author?

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The word “techies” (Line 4, Para 1) probably refers to those who are ____.	“技术人员”一词（第4行，第1段）可能指的是那些_____的人。A. afraid of technology	A. 害怕技术B. skilled in technology	B. 技术娴熟C. ignorant of technology	C. 对技术一无所知D. incompetent in technology	D. 技术不称职【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干中的techies一词可直接定位至第一段第三句。根据techies之后的后置定语eager to see the Internet...of its networking power可推断，techies应该是极其关注网络或对网络感兴趣的人。从之前的boost（帮助，支持）可进一步推断，techies能够为麦克唐纳的网上易货交易提供帮助和支持，那也就意味着他们熟悉网络技术。由此可见，B选项符合题意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: were eager to identify with his motto
options.C: liked his goal announced in advance
options.D: hoped to prove the power of the Internet

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Many people may have deliberately helped Kyle because they ______. 许多人可能故意帮助凯尔，因为他们_____。A. were impressed by his creativity A. 对他的创造力印象深刻B. were eager to identify with his motto B. 渴望认同他的座右铭C. liked his goal announced in advance C. 喜欢他提前宣布的目标D. hoped to prove the power of the Internet D. 希望证明互联网的力量【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干信息定位至第一段第三句。该句中MacDonald likely got a boost from techies是对题干中Many people may have deliberately helped Kyle的同义转述，而该句后半部分则道出了其原因：因为他们渴望看到互联网能够通过这一关于其网络功能的大胆测试，即证明网络的巨大力量。D选项是对文中eager to see the Internet pass this daring test of its networking power的同义转述，故正确。【干扰项排除】A项文中没有提及，属于无中生有；B项和C项问题是一样的，虽然在定位句中看到相关的信息，但与题目无关，属于出处错位。

options.C: the quality of goods or services

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The Internet barter system relies heavily on_____. 互联网易货系统在很大程度上依赖于_____。A. the size of barter sties A. 易货交易的规模B. the use of virtual currency B. 虚拟货币的使用C. the quality of goods or services C. 商品或服务的质量D. the location of trading companies D. 贸易公司所在地【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干信息barter system可定位至第三段的首句。接着往后阅读，发现这一段围绕着currency（货币）展开。根据三段可知最后一句可知“人们可借由虚拟货币为交易定价，实现以货易货。”由此可见，虚拟货币是网络易货交易所依赖的交易媒介。综上所述，B选项符合题意，故正确。【干扰项排除】A、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；C项在第二段的第二句有提及，但与题干无关，属于出处错位。

【选项释义】It is implies that Internet advertisements can help_____. 这意味着互联网广告可以帮助_____。A. companies make more profit A. 公司盈利更多B. companies do formal exchanges B. 公司进行正式交流C. media register in statistics C. 统计中的媒体注册D. media grade barter sites D. 媒体级易货网站【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干关键词advertising（广告）可定位至第四段第二句。该句以广告业为例，说明网络广告以空闲位置来交换利润。该例证是为了论证首句的观点：易货交易能帮助公司利用闲置的生产力。言下之意，网络广告能够帮助公司利用闲置生产力来获得利润，因此A选项符合题意，为正确选项。【干扰项排除】B、C项可以在第四段最后一句找到信息，但根据“Internet ads don’t register in industry-growth statistics, because many exchanges are arranged outside the formal exchanges.”（互联网广告没有在行业增长统计中登记，因为许多交易安排在正式交易之外。）其中的否定可以判断B、C项是错误的，属于反向干扰。D选项在第五段第一句有提及，但与题干无关，属于出处错误。

options.A: It is criticized for doing business in a primitive way.
options.B: It aims to deal with hyperinflation in some countries.
options.C: It helps get rid of middlemen in trade and exchange.
options.D: It is intended to evaluate the performance of trading partners.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the follow is true of QL2 according to the author? 根据作者的说法，以下哪一项对于QL2是正确的？A. It is criticized for doing business in a primitive way A. 人们批评它做生意的方式很原始B. It aims to deal with hyperinflation in some countries B. 它旨在应对一些国家的恶性通货膨胀C. It helps get rid of middlemen in trade and exchange C. 它有助于摆脱贸易和交换中的中间商D. It is intended to evaluate the performance of trading partners D. 其目的是评估贸易伙伴的业绩【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】据题干信息QL2可定位至第五段第五句。该句指出，农户对于能够“摆脱奸猾的中间商”感到非常兴奋，此外选项中的get rid of是对原文中be liberated from的同义转述，换言之，QL2提供的易货交易可以让农户“摆脱奸猾的中间商”。可见，C选项与原文的表述一致，故C选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、D选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；B项在第五段的第二句有涉及，“Barter exchanges can allow firms in countries with hyperinflation or nontradable currencies to enter global trades.”（易货交易可以允许恶性通货膨胀或不可交易货币国家的公司进入全球贸易）与选项所说的解决国家的通货膨胀不符合，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
The lives of very few Newark residents are untouched by violence: New Jersey’s biggest city has seen it all. Yet the murder of three young people, who were forced to kneel before being shot in the back of the head in a school playground on August 4th, has shaken the city. A fourth, who survived, was stabbed and shot in the face. The four victims were by all accounts good kids, all enrolled in college, all with a future. But the cruel murder, it seems, has at last forced Newarkers to say they have had enough.
Grassroots organizations, like Stop Shooting, have been flooded with offers of help and support since the killings. Yusef Ismail, its co-founder, says the group has been going door-to-door asking people to sign a pledge of non-violence. They hope to get 50,000 to promise to “stop shooting, start thinking, and keep living.” The Newark Community Foundation, which was launched last month, announced on August 14th that it will help pay for Community Eye, a surveillance(监视) system tailored towards gun crime.
Cory Booker who became mayor 13 months ago with a mission to revitalize the city, believes the surveillance program will be the largest camera and audio network in any American city. More than 30 cameras were installed earlier this summer and a further 50 will be installed soon in a seven-square mile area where 80% of the city’s recent shootings have occurred. And more cameras are planned.
When a gunshot is detected, the surveillance camera zooms in on that spot. Similar technology in Chicago has increased arrests and decreased shootings. Mr. Booker plans to announce a comprehensive gun strategy later this week.
Mr. Booker, as well as church leaders and others, believes (or hopes) that after the murder the city will no longer stand by in coldness. For generations, Newark has been paralyzed by poverty—almost one in three people lives below the poverty line—and growing indifference to crime.
Some are skeptical. Steve Malanga of the conservative Manhattan Institute notes that Newark has deep social problems: over 60% of children are in homes without fathers. The school system, taken over by the state in 1995, is a mess. But there is also some cause for hope. Since Mr. Booker was elected, there has been a rise in investment and re-zoning for development. Only around 7% of nearby Newark airport workers used to come from Newark; now, a year, the figure is 30%.Mr Booker has launched a New York-style war on crime. So far this year, crime has fallen 11% and shootings are down 30%(through the murder rate looks likely to match last year’s high).
1.What happened in Newark, New Jersey on August 4th?
2.Judging from the context, the “Community Eye”(Line5,Para 2) is（  ）.
3.We learn from the passage that Newark has all the following problems EXCEPT
（  ）.
4.Mayor Booker’s effort against crime seem to be
（  ）.
5.The best title for the passage may be（  ）.

options.A: The Newark residents witnessed a murder.
options.B: Four young people were killed in a school playground.
options.C: The new mayor of Newark took office.
options.D: Four college students fell victim to violence.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What happened in Newark, New Jersey on August 4th?	8月4日在新泽西州纽瓦克发生了什么？A. The Newark residents witnessed a murder.	A. 纽瓦克居民目睹了一起谋杀案。B. Four young people were killed in a school playground.	B. 四名年轻人在学校操场上被杀。C. The new mayor of Newark took office.	C. 纽瓦克的新市长就职了。D. Four college students fell victim to violence.	D. 四名大学生成为暴力的受害者。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干信息可定位至一段。本段第二句描述了三个年轻人被杀害的经过，接着第三句又提到第四位受害人是幸存者。从第四句可知这四名受害人的简单情况，即他们都是大学生。综合以上信息可知，D选项“四名大学生沦为暴力的受害者”是对上述内容的合理概括，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；B项文章提到三名年轻人被杀，第四名幸存，选项属于曲解原文。

options.A: a watching system for gun crime

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Judging from the context, the “Community Eye” (Line5, Para 2) is _____. 从上下文来看，“社区之眼”（第5行，第2段）是_____。A. a watching system for gun crime A. 枪支犯罪监控系统B. a neighborhood protection organization B. 社区保护组织C. an unprofitable community business C. 无利可图的社区企业D. a grassroots organization D. 基层组织【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干信息Community Eye可定位在第二段最后一句。该句对Community Eye作了简单介绍，用同位语a surveillance system tailored towards gun crime说明其是一种专门针对枪击犯罪的监视系统。由此可见，A选项为正确答案，其中watching和for分别是对原文中的surveillance（监视）和tailored towards（为……而设）的同义替换。【干扰项排除】B、C选项在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。D选项的信息对应的是第二段第一句，属于出处错误。

【选项释义】We learn from the passage that Newark has all the following problems EXCEPT_____. 我们从文章中了解到，纽瓦克有以下所有问题，除了_____。A. violence A. 暴力B. flood B. 洪水C. poverty C. 贫穷D. indifference D. 冷漠【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】B选项是根据文中第二段首句出现的flooded一词设置的干扰项，其在文中意为“大量收到……”，不属于社会问题，因此B选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A项浏览全文可知，纽瓦克市的暴力问题是贯穿全文的一个重大社会问题；C、D项第五段提到的贫困和冷漠问题则是隐藏在暴力问题之后的另外两大社会问题；文中第六段也提及纽瓦克市还存在家庭、教育以及就业等深层社会问题。

【选项释义】Mayor Booker’s effort against crime seem to be ______ 布克市长打击犯罪的努力似乎是______A. idealistic A. 理想主义的B. impractical B. 不明智的C. effective C. 有效的D. fruitless D. 徒劳的【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干关键词Mayor Booker可定位在文章末段第六句。该句提到布克市长发起了一场纽约式的抵制犯罪运动，接着第七句又说“到今年为止，纽瓦克市的犯罪率已经下降了11%，枪击案件也下降了30%（尽管谋杀犯罪率似乎与去年持平）”。由此可知，布克市长抵制犯罪的努力确实取得了一定的成效。因此C选项合乎题意，为正确选项。【干扰项排除】A、B、D项不能通过定位段推理出来，属于无中生有。

options.A: Stop Shooting, Start Thinking, and Keep Living
options.B: Efforts to Fight against Gun Crimes
options.C: A Mission to Revitalize the City

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The best title for the passage may be _____. 这篇文章最好的标题可能是_____。A. Stop Shooting, Start Thinking, and Keep Living A. 停止拍摄，开始思考，继续生活B. Efforts to Fight against Gun Crimes B. 努力打击枪支犯罪C. A Mission to Revitalize the City C. 振兴城市的使命D. Violent Murders in Newark D. 纽瓦克的暴力谋杀案【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】文章首段以8月4日发生在纽瓦克市的枪击谋杀案引入主题；从二段到四段，文章主要介绍了纽瓦克市为反对枪支犯罪而采取的种种行动和措施；五、六段则剖析了枪击事件背后的社会问题，并说明市长为解决社会问题所付出的努力，落脚点在于表明其在抵制枪支犯罪方面所做的努力是卓有成效的。综上所述，全文主要介绍了纽瓦克市上下为对抗枪支犯罪所做的努力，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项不能通过文章线索推理出来。

questions.article:
According to a recent survey on money and relationships, 36 percent of people are keeping a bank account from their partner. While this financial unfaithfulness may appear as distrust in a relationship , in truth it may just be a form of financial protection.
With almost half of all marriages ending in divorce, men and women are realizing they need to be financially savvy, regardless of whether they are in a relationship.
The financial hardship on individuals after a divorce can be extremely difficult, even more so when children are involved. The lack of permanency in relationships, jobs and family life may be the cause of a growing trend to keep a secret bank account hidden from a partner; in other words, an "escape fund”.
Margaret’s story is far from unique. She is a representative of a growing number of women in long-term relationships who are becoming protective of their own earnings.
Every month on pay day, she banks hundreds of dollars into a savings account she keeps from her husband. She has been doing this throughout their six-year marriage and has built a nest egg worth an incredible $100,000 on top of her pension.
Margaret says if her husband found out about her secret savings he’d hurt and would interpret this as a sign she wasn’t sure of the marriage.” He’d think it was my escape fun so that financially I could afford to get out of the relationship if it went wrong. I know you should approach marriage as being forever and I hope ours is, but you can never be sure.”
Like many of her fellow secret savers, Margaret was stung in a former relationship and has since been very guarded about her own money.
Coming clean to your partner about being a secret saver may not be all that bad. Take Colleen for example, who had been saving secretly for a few years before she confessed to her partner. ”I decided to open a savings account and start building a nest egg of my own. I wanted to prove to myself that I could put money in the bank and leave it there for a rainy day.”
“When John found out about my secret savings, he was a little suspicious of my motives. I reassured him that this was certainly not an escape fund that I feel very secure in our relationship. I have to admit that it does feel good to have my own money on reserve if ever there are rainy days in the future. It’s sensible to build and protect your personal financial security.”
1.The trend to keep a secret bank account is growing because（  ）.
2.The word “savvy”(Line2,Para 2) probably means
（  ）.
3.Which inference can we make about Margaret?
4.The author mentions Colleen’s example to show
（  ）.
5.Which of the following best summary this passage?

options.A: escape fund helps one through rainy days
options.B: days are getting harder and harder

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The trend to keep a secret bank account is growing because______.	藏私房钱的趋势正在增长，因为______。A. escape fund helps one through rainy days	A. 逃生基金助人渡过难关B. days are getting harder and harder	B. 日子越来越难过了C. women are money sensitive	C. 女人对金钱很敏感D. financial conflicts often occur	D. 财务冲突经常发生【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干信息可定位至第三段第二句。该句中a growing trend to keep a secret bank account与题干中“The trend to keep a secret bank account is growing”一致。根据该句中的因果逻辑可知，the cause of（……的原因）之前的内容应是题干所问信息。该处明确指出：生活中的种种不稳定（情感关系、工作和家庭生活的维持缺乏持久性）导致人们开始存私房钱，这些都是A项中的rainy day，因此A选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B项，原文虽然提到了当人们的生活艰难的时候需要私房钱作为不时之需，但选项中日子过得越来越难而存私房钱并没有提及，这里属于偷换概念。C、D项在文中没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】The word “savvy” (Line2, Para2) probably means_______. 单词“savvy”（第2行，第2段）可能意味着_______。A. suspicious A. 怀疑的B. secure B. 安全的C. shrewd C. 精明的D. simple D. 简单的【考查点】词汇推断题【解题思路】此处需联系上下文进行判断。根据上文可知：人们存私房钱可能是为了寻求经济上的保障。根据下文可知：离婚使个人经济面临困境。而第二段前半部分提及：近乎一半的婚姻以离婚而告终。那么，在这种情况下，男女双方就需要在经济上有所考虑，未雨绸缪。而financially shrewd（精明的）可以理解为“有经济忧患意识的”，符合题意，故C选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项不能根据文章的逻辑结构推出。

【选项释义】Which inference can we make about Margaret? 我们可以对玛格丽特作出什么推断？A. She is a unique woman A. 她是一个独立的女人B. She was once divorced B. 她离过婚C. She is going to retire C. 她要退休了D. She has many children D. 她有许多小孩【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】原文第七段提到玛格丽特在以前的婚姻中受过伤害，这说明她离过婚。B选项中的once divorced（曾经离过婚）与原文中的former relationship（以前的婚姻关系）相对应，符合题意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.A: any couple can avoid marriage conflicts
options.B: privacy within marriage should be respected
options.C: everyone can save a fortune with a happy marriage
options.D: financial disclosure is not necessarily bad

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author mentions Colleen’s example to show_____. 作者引用科琳的例子来说明_____。A. any couple can avoid marriage conflicts A. 任何夫妻都可以避免婚姻冲突B. privacy within marriage should be respected B. 婚姻中的隐私应该得到尊重C. everyone can save a fortune with a happy marriage C. 每个人都可以通过幸福的婚姻节省财富D. financial disclosure is not necessarily bad D. 财务披露不一定不好【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干信息Colleen’s example可定位至第八段。本段第一句提到向配偶坦白存私房钱或许并非坏事，紧接着文章就列举了柯林的例子对第一句（即本段的主题句）进行解释和说明。因此，D选项是对原文的合理推断，符合题意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不能通过文章信息推理出来。

【选项释义】Which of the following best summary this passage? 以下哪一项是对这篇文章最好的总结？A. Secret Savers A. 秘密存款B. Love Is What It’s Worth B. 爱是值得的C. Banking Honesty C. 钱款诚信D. Once Bitten, Twice Shy D. 一朝被蛇咬，十年怕井绳【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】本文以调查数据开头，说明很多人会瞒着另一半持有秘密存款，作者认为这种做法是寻求经济保障的一种方式。接着第二段和第三段分析了该现象背后的原因，然后在第四段到第九段以两位女性的例子说明秘密存款可以使女性在经济上更加有安全感。综合可知，“秘密存款”是文章的主线，因此A选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项不能通过文章逻辑推理出来。

questions.article:
“The word ‘protection’ is no longer taboo (禁忌语)”. This short sentence, uttered by French President Nicolas Sarkozy last month, may have launched a new era in economic history. Why? For decades, Western leaders have believed that lowering trade barriers and tariffs was a natural good. Doing so, they reasoned, would lead to greater economic efficiency and productivity, which in turn would improve human welfare. Championing free trade thus became a moral, not just an economic, cause.
These leaders, of course, weren’t acting out of unselfishness. They knew their economies were the most competitive, so they’d profit most from liberalization. And developing countries feared that their economies would be swamped by superior Western productivity. Today, however, the tables have turned—though few acknowledge it. The West continues to preach free trade, but practices it less and less. Asian, meanwhile, continues to plead for special protection but practices more and more free trade.
That’s why Sarkozy’s words were so important: he finally injected some honesty into the trade debates. The truth is that large parts of the West are losing faith in tree trade, though few leaders admit it. Some economists are more honest. Paul Krugman is one of the few willing to acknowledge that protectionist arguments are returning. In the short run, there will be winners and losers under free trade. This, of course, is what capitalism is all about. But more and more of these losers will be in the West, Economists in the developed world used to love quoting Jonoph Schumpeter, who said that ‘creative destruction” was an essential part of capitalist growth. But they always assumed that destruction would happen over there. When Western workers began losing jobs, suddenly their leaders began to lose faith in their principles, Things have yet to reverse completely. But there’s clearly a negative trend in a Western theory and practice.
A little hypocrisy (虚伪) is not in itself a serious problem. The real problem is that Western governments continue to insist that they retain control of the key global economic and financial institutions while drifting away from global liberalization. Lock at what’s happening at the IMF (International Monetary Fund). The Europeans have demanded that they keep the post of managing director. But all too often, Western officials put their own interests above everyone else’s when they dominate these global institutions.
The time has therefore come for the Asians-who are clearly the new winners in today’s global economy-to provide more intellectual leadership in supporting free trade: Sadly, they have yet to do so. Unless Asians speak out, however, there’s a real danger that Adam Smith’s principles, which have brought so much good to the world, could gradually die. And that would leave all of us, worse off, in one way or another.
1.It can be inferred that “protection” (Line 1, Para.1) means（  ）.
2.The Western leaders preach free trade because
（  ）.
3.By “the tables have turned” (Line 3-4, Para. 2) the author implies that
（  ）.
4.The Western economies used to like the idea of “creative destruction” because it
（  ）.
5.The author uses “IMF” was an example to illustrate the point that（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】It can be inferred that “protection” (Line 1, Para.1) means________.	可以推断，“protection”（第1行，第1段）是指________。A. improving economic efficiency	A. 提高经济效益B. ending the free-trade practice	B. 结束自由贸易惯例C. lowering moral standard	C. 降低道德标准D. raising trade tariffs	D. 提高贸易关税【考查点】词汇推断题【解题思路】根据题干信息可定位到第一段第一句。该句提到：protection一词不再是禁忌语了。换句话说，protection一词以前一直是被人们所忌讳的。既然protection以前一直是禁忌语，那么自然就不是好事。又从第一段第四句可知：几十年来，西方领导人一直坚信降低贸易壁垒和关税是自然而然的好事。由此可见，protection的含义应该与降低贸易壁垒和关税呈对立关系。D选项“提高贸易关税”与原文中的lowering trade banners and tariffs（降低贸易壁垒和关税）在含义上正好相反，符合题意，故D选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项不能通过文章逻辑推理出来。

options.A: it is beneficial to their economies
options.B: it is supported by developing countries
options.C: it makes them keep faith in their principles
options.D: it is advocated by Joseph Schumpeter and Adam Smith

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The Western leaders preach free trade because________. 西方领导人鼓吹自由贸易是因为________。A. it is beneficial to their economies A. 这对他们的经济是有益的B. it is supported by developing countries B. 它得到了发展中国家的支持C. it makes them keep faith in their principles C. 这使他们对自己的原则保持信心D. it is advocated by Joseph Schumpeter and Adam Smith D. 它是由约瑟夫·熊彼特和亚当·斯密倡导的【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干关键词western leaders，free trade可定位至原文的第一段第五、六句“For decades, Western leaders have believed that lowering trade barriers and tariffs was a natural good. Doing so, they reasoned, would lead to greater economic efficiency and productivity…”（几十年来，西方领导人一直认为，降低贸易壁垒和关税是一件自然的好事。他们认为，这样做将提高经济效率和生产力……）。其中前半句对应题干中的preach（鼓吹），后半句则对应A项的表达。还可追溯到第二段第二句，该句提到西方国家会从自由贸易中获得最多的利益。因此A选项符合题意，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B项与原文相反，文章在第二段第三句提到发展中国家害怕他们的经济被西方的先进生产力取代，因此发展中国家是不支持的，属于反向干扰；C选项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；D选项中的Joseph Schumpeter and Adam Smith在文章中被提及，但与题目无关，属于出处错误。

options.A: the Western leaders have turned self-centered
options.B: the Asian leaders have become advocates of free trade
options.C: the developed economies have turned less competitive
options.D: the developing economies have become more independent

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】By “the tables have turned” (Line 3-4, Para. 2) the author implies that________. 作者通过“形势已经扭转”（第3-4行，第2段）暗示：________。A. the Western leaders have turned self-centered A. 西方领导人变得以自我为中心B. the Asian leaders have become advocates of free trade B. 亚洲领导人已成为自由贸易的倡导者C. the developed economies have turned less competitive C. 发达经济体的竞争力有所下降D. the developing economies have become more independent D. 发展中经济体变得更加独立【考查点】推理判断题。【解题思路】根据题干信息可定位到第二段第四句。该句指出如今的形势已经发生了逆转。可见，此处承接上文，对其进行否定。本段第二句提到：发达国家的经济最具竞争力，因此会从自由贸易中获得最多的利益。所以可以推断，此处的逆转指的是发达国家经济的竞争力由大到小的转变。因此，C选项符合题意，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B原文没有提及，属于无中生有。D选项在定位段最后一句有提及“Asian, meanwhile, continues to plead for special protection but practices more and more free trade.”（与此同时，亚洲人继续呼吁特别保护，但实行越来越多的自由贸易）表示亚洲没有变得更独立，只是实行越来越多的自由贸易而已，属于曲解原文。

options.A: set a long-term rather than short-turn goal
options.B: was an essential part of capitalist development
options.C: contained a positive rather than negative mentality
options.D: was meant to be the destruction of developing economies

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The Western economies used to like the idea of “creative destruction” because it________. 西方经济体过去喜欢“创造性破坏”的想法，因为它________。A. set a long-term rather than short-turn goal A. 设定长期目标而不是短期目标B. was an essential part of capitalist development B. 是资本主义发展的重要组成部分C. contained a positive rather than negative mentality C. 包含积极而非消极的心态D. was meant to be the destruction of developing economies D. 意味着对发展中经济体的破坏【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干关键词creative destruction定位在第三段第八句。该句提到：“创造性毁灭”是资本主义发展的必要组成部分。之后第九句陈述了他们喜欢引用此名言的原因，即他们总是假设毁灭（即经济的破坏）应该发生在对方身上。根据上下文判断，第九句中的over there（对方）应该是指与发达国家相对的发展中国家。因此，D选项是对原文的合理推断，符合题意，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A项原文没有提及，属于无中生有；B项在定位句可以找到原句，但这个选项不是西方经济喜欢creative destruction的原因，而是对creative destruction的定义，属于偷换概念；C项可以根据第三段的最后一句话排除：But there’s clearly a negative trend in a Western theory and practice.（但在西方的理论和实践中，显然存在着一种消极的趋势）属于反向干扰。

options.A: European leaders are reluctant to admit they are hypocritical
options.B: there is an inconsistency between Western theory and practice
options.C: global institutions are not being led by true globalization advocates
options.D: European countries’ interests are being ignored by economic leaders

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author uses “IMF” was an example to illustrate the point that_______. 作者以“国际货币基金组织”为例来说明这一点_______。A. European leaders are reluctant to admit they are hypocritical A. 欧洲领导人不愿承认他们虚伪B. there is an inconsistency between Western theory and practice B. 西方理论与实践不一致C. global institutions are not being led by true globalization advocates C. 全球机构并非由真正的全球化倡导者领导D. European countries’ interests are being ignored by economic leaders D. 欧洲国家的利益正被经济领导人忽视【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干关键词“IMF”定位在第四段第三句。该句及第四、五句列举了IMF（国际货币基金组织）面临的窘境：欧洲国家要求担任领导人；而西方官员在控制这些全球机构时，总是会出现将自己的利益凌驾于他人利益之上的情形。该例子出现在第二句之后，它们之间是例证关系。第二句指出：西方政府在渐渐背离全球贸易自由化的同时，却仍然坚持对重要的经济和金融机构的控制权。可见，作者用IMF的事例是为了说明“背离全球贸易自由化的西方国家仍然控制着主要机构，”C选项符合题意，为正确选项。【干扰项排除】A项可以定位到第三段第一句“A little hypocrisy (虚伪) is not in itself a serious problem.”（有一点虚伪不是严重的问题，与选项表达的含义不符，属于偷换概念；B项可以定位到第三段最后一句，原文表达的是negative trend（消极的趋势），而不是选项中的inconsistency（不一致），属于偷换概念；D项定位到第四段最后一句：西方政府把他们自身的利益凌驾于人民之上。因此国家的利益是受到重视的，与选项表达相反，属于反向干扰。

questions.article: The term “business model” first came into widespread use with the invention of personal computer and the spreadsheet (空白表格程序). Before the spreadsheet, business planning usually meant producing a single forecast. At best, you did a little sensitivity analysis around the projection. The spreadsheet ushered in a much more analytic approach to planning because every major line item could be pulled apart, its components and subcomponents analyzed and tested. You could ask what- if questions about the critical assumptions on which your business depended---for example, what if customers are more price-sensitive than we thought?---and with a few keystrokes, you could see how any change would play out on every aspect of the whole. In other words, you could model the behavior of a business. Before the computer changed the nature of business planning, most successful business models were created more by accident than by elaborate design. By enabling companies to tie their marketplace insights much more tightly to the resulting economics, spread sheet made it possible to model business before they were launched.

Prior to the 20th century, many languages with small numbers of speakers survived for centuries. The increasingly interconnected modern world makes it much more difficult for small language communities to live in relative isolation, a key factor in language maintenance and preservation.
It remains to be seen whether the world can maintain its linguistic and cultural diversity in the centuries ahead. Many powerful forces appear to work against it :population growth, which pushes migrant populations into the world’s last isolated locations; mass tourism; global telecommunications and mass media; and the spread of gigantic global corporations. All of these forces appear to signify a future in which the language of advertising, popular culture, and consumer products become similar. Already English and a few other major tongues have emerged as global languages of commerce and communication. For many of the world’s peoples, learning one of these languages is viewed as the key to education, economic opportunity, and a better way of life.
Only about 3,000 languages now in use are expected to survive the coming century. Are most of the rest doomed in the century after that?
Whether most of these languages survive will probably depend on how strongly cultural groups wish to keep their identity alive through a native language. To do so will require an emphasis on bilingualism（mastery of two languages）. Bilingual speakers could use their own language in smaller spheres---at home, among friends, in community settings---and a global language at work, in dealings with government, and in commercial spheres. In this way, many small languages could sustain their cultural and linguistic integrity alongside global languages, rather than yield to the homogenizing（同化的）forces of globalization.
Ironically, the trend of technological innovation that has threatened minority languages could also help save them. For example, some experts predict that computer software translation tools will one day permit minority language speakers to browse the Internet using their native tongues. Linguists are currently using computer—aided learning tools to teach a variety of threatened languages.
For many endangered languages, the line between revival and death is extremely thin. Language is remarkably resilient（有活力的）,however. It is not just a tool for communicating, but also a powerful way of separating different groups, or of demonstrating group identity. Many indigenous（原生的，土著的）communities have shown that it is possible to live in the modern world while reclaiming their unique identities through language.
1.Minority languages can be best preserved in（  ）.
2.According to Paragraph 2, that the world can maintain its linguistic diversity in the future is
（  ）.
3.According to the author, bilingualism can help
（  ）.
4.Computer technology is helpful for preserving minority languages in that it
（  ）.
5.In the author’s view, many endangered languages are（  ）.

options.D: following the tradition of the 20th  century

sub_questions.analysis: 1.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第二个阅读考点:理解文中的具体信息根据题干信息定位至一段2句。该句指出现代世界使小语种群更加难以在相对孤立的环境中生存,而这一环境正是小语种得以维持和传承的关键因素。所以,小语种能够得以最好保持的条件是:在相对孤立的环境里。C选项是对原文中 for small language communities to live in relative isolation的同义转述,故正确。2.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第五个阅读考点:进行一定的判断和推理根据题干关键词 maintain its linguistic diversity可回文定位到二段1句,由该句中的 It remains to be seen(有待观察)和 whether(是否)可以判断作者的语气是不确定的,因此A选项正确。3.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第二个阅读考点:理解文中的具体信息根据题干中的关键词 bilingualism定位至四段2句。该段2句指出要保持文化群体的身份特性,需要把重点放在对双语的掌握上。接着在3、4句作者指出双语使用者可在小范围内使用小语种,而在其他场合使用通用语以维持自身文化和语言的完整性,D选项符合题意,为正确答案。其中 maintain是4句中 sustain(维持)的同义替换;而 linguistic and cultural identity是 cultural and linguistic integrity的同义转述。4.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第五个阅读考点:进行一定的判断和推理根据题干关键词 Computer technology(电脑技术)可回文定位到五段。五段1句中提到威胁小语种的技术革新趋势也能够帮助挽救它们。而2、3句则通过两个具体的事例说明电脑技术可以帮助小语种使用者用母语浏览网页以及计算机工具可以辅助小语种教学,B选项恰好对应例证的两个方面,即对小语种的 using和 learning,因此为正确答案。5.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第六个阅读考点:理解作者的意图、观点或态度根据题干关键词 endangered languages可定位至六段。该段首句指出许多濒危语言的复兴和消亡只是一线之差,然后用 however进行转折,说明语言是具有非凡活力的。根据转折处命题原则,作者强调的重点在后一句。同时,该段3句也用 a powerful way说明了小语种的功用,4句更是用具体的事例说明小语种复兴的可能性。综上所述,C选项为正确答案。

options.B: homogenize the world’s languages and cultures
options.C: global languages reach home and community settings
options.D: speakers maintain their linguistic and cultural identity

options.B: facilitates the learning and using of those languages
