options.D: a unique way of bringing different groups together

questions.article:
Everyone，it seems，has a health problem。After pouring billions into the National Health Service，British people moan about dirty hospitals，long waits and wasted money. In Germany the new chancellor, Angela Merkel, is under fire for suggesting changing the financing of its health system. Canada’s new Conservative Prime Minister, Stephen Harper, made a big fuss during the election about reducing the country’s lengthy medical queues. Across the rich world, affluence, ageing and advancing technology are driving up health spending faster than income.
But nowhere has a bigger health problem than America. Soaring medical bills are squeezing wages, swelling the ranks of the uninsured and pushing huge firms and perhaps even the government towards bankruptcy. Ford’s announcement this week that it would cut up to 30.000 jobs by 2012 was as much a sign of it’s “legacy ” health –care costs as of the ills of the car industry. Pushed by polls that show health care is one of his main domestic problems and by forecasts showing that the retiring baby-boomers (生育高峰期出生的人) will crush the government’s finances, George Bush is to unveil a reform ;plan in next week’s state-of –the –union address.
America’s health system is unlike any other. The Unite States spends 16% of its GDP on health, around twice the rich-country average, equivalent to $6,280 for every American each year. Yet it is the only rich country that does not guarantee universal health coverage. Thanks to an accident of history, most Americans receive health insurance through their employer, with the government picking up the bill for the poor and the elderly.
This curious hybrid（混合物） certainly has its strengths. Americans have more choice than anybody else, and their health-care system is much more innovative. Europeans’ bills could be much higher if American medicine were not doing much of their Research and Development（R&D）for them. But there are also huge weaknesses. The one most often cited—especially by foreigners—is the army of uninsured. Some 46 million Americans do not have cover. In many cases that is out of choice and , if they fall seriously ill, hospitals have to treat them. But it is still deeply unequal. And there are also shocking inefficiencies: by some measures, 30% of American health spending is wasted.
Then there is the question of state support. Many Americans disapprove of the “socialized medicine” of Canada and Europe. In fact, even if much of the administration is done privately, around 60% of America’s health-care bill ends up being met by the government. Proportionately, the American state already spends as much on health as the OECD（Organization of Economic Cooperation and Development）average, and that share is set to grow as the baby-boomers run up their Medicare bills and ever more employers avoid providing health-care coverage. America is, in effect, heading towards a version of socialized medicine by default.
1.Health problems mentioned in the passage include all the following except（  ）.
2.Ford’s announcement of cutting up to 30,000 jobs by 2012 indicates that Ford
（  ）.
3.In the author’s opinion, America’s health system is 
（  ）.
4.It is implied in the passage that
（  ）.
5.From the last paragraph we may learn that the “socialized medicine’ is（  ）.

options.A: poor hospital conditions in U. K.
has the biggest health problem of the car industry
options.B: has made profits from its health-care legacy
options.C: has accumulated too heavy a health-care burden
options.D: owes a great deal of debt to its employees

options.A: America’s health system has its strengths and weaknesses
options.B: the U. S. government pays medical bills for the poor and the elderly
options.C: some 46 million Americans do not have medical insurance
options.D: Europeans benefit a lot from America’s medical research

options.A: a practice of Canada and Europe
options.B: a policy adopted by the U. S. government

questions.article:
When Thomas Keller, one of America’s foremost chefs, announced that on Sept. I he would abolish the practice of tipping at Per Se. his luxury restaurant in New York City, and replace it with European-style service charge, I knew three groups would be opposed: customers, servers and restaurant owners. These three groups are all committed to tipping——as they quickly made clear on Web sites. To oppose tipping , it seems, is to be anticapitalist , and maybe even a little French..
But Mr. Keller is right to move away from tipping—and it’s worth exploring why just about everyone else in the restaurant world is wrong to stick with the practice.
Customers believe in tipping because they think it makes economic sense. “Waiters know that they won’t get paid if they don’t do a good job” is how most advocates of the system would put it. To be sure, this is a tempting, apparently rational statement about economic theory, but it appears to have little applicability to the real world of restaurants.
Michael Lynn, an associate professor of consumer behavior and marketing at Cornell’s School of Hotel Administration, has conducted dozens of students of tipping and has concluded that consumers assessments of the quality of service correlate weakly to the amount they tip.
Rather, customers are likely to tip more in response to servers touching them lightly and leaning forward next to the table to make conversation than to how often their water glass is refilled——in other words, customers tip more when they like the server, not when the service is good. Mr. Lynn’s studies also indicate that male customers increase their tips for female servers while female customers increase their tips for male servers,.
What’s more, consumers seem to forget that the tip increases as the bill increases. Thus, the tipping system is an open invitation to what restaurant professionals call “upselling”: every bottle of imported water, every espresso and every cocktail is extra money in the server’s pocket. Aggressive upwelling for tips is often rewarded while low-key, quality service often goes unrecognized.
In addition, the practice of tip pooling, which is the norm in fine-dining restaurants and is becoming more in every kind of restaurant above the level of a greasy spoon, has ruined whatever effect voting with your tip might have had on an individual waiter. In an unreasonable outcome, you are punishing the good waiters in the restaurant by not tipping the bad one. Indeed, there appear to be little connection between tipping and good service .
1.It may be inferred that a European-style service（  ）.
2.Which of the following is NOT true according to the author?
3.According to Michael Lynn’s studies, waiters will likely get more tips if they
（  ）.
4.We may infer from the context that “upselling”（Line 3，Para. 6） probably means
（  ）.
5.This passage is mainly about （  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 1.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第五个阅读考点:进行一定的判断和推理根据题干中的 a European- style service可定位到一段1句。该句提到 Thomas Keller要取消自家饭店的小费制度,即不再收取小费,并以欧式服务的收费方式( European- style service charge)来代替。由此推知,欧式服务实现了 Thomas Keller不收小费的目的，换言之该服务是 tipping-free(不收小费)的,故A选项正确。2.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第二个阅读考点:理解文中的具体信息题干中没有明确的信号词,考生需浏览选项,根据选项中的关键词逐一回文定位,进行判断。B选项中出现了 Waiters,即 servers,并谈及该人群的态度,因此可定位至一段1、2句。1句指出,包括 servers在内的三大群体均反对取消小费制度;2句也进一步表明他们坚决支持小费制度。servers鲜明的态度足以说明其很在乎小费,B选项的表述与此不符,故为正确答案。3.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第二个阅读考点:理解文中的具体信息本题遵循长难句处命题的原则。根据题干中的关键信息 waiters will likely get more tips可定位至五段1句，该句破折号后的内容提到,顾客多付小费是因为喜欢这个服务员,而不是因为其服务周到，换言之，服务员若想多拿小费，就得获得顾客的喜欢,即贏得顾客的好感。C选项是对原文的同义转述,故正确。4.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第四个阅读考点:根据上下文推断重要生词或词组的含义根据题干信息可迅速定位到六段2句。该句提到:小费制度会引发“ upselling ”行为,而该句句首的Thus则暗示,1句应为这一结果的原因,即,“ the tip increases as the bill increases(账单数额越高,需要支付的小费就越多)”,换言之,正因为账单数额与小费数额成正比,所以要获得高额小费,就必须增加账单数额,这就催生了“ upselling ”行为。由此可见,“ upselling ”能使账单数额增加。再由冒号后对“ upselling ”的进一步解释可知,对 imported water(进口水)、 espresso(浓咖啡)、cocktail(鸡尾酒)等的销售即为“ upselling ”的真正含义,而根据常识可知,这类物品均为价格高昂的奢侈商品,由此推之, “upselling”应指的是出售更贵的商品,故D选项正确。5.本题主要考查的是大纲规定的第一个阅读考点:理解主旨要义本文是典型的阐释体论说文:第一段通过列举具体事例引出取消小费制度这一主题;第二段提出作者的观点—取消小费制度是正确的;后面五段逐层深入,详细论述了小费制度的弊端,如与服务质量无关、引发“向上销售”、小费均分不合理等,而这些弊端正是取消小费制度强有力的原因。由此可知,A选项reasons to abolish the practice of tipping是对全文大意的合理概括,故为正确答案。

options.A: Tipping is a common practice in the restaurant world.
options.D: Tipping has little connection with the quality of service.
serve customers of the same sex

options.A: reasons to abolish the practice of tipping

questions.article:
“I promise.” “I swear to you it’ll never happen again.” “I give you my word.” “Honestly. Believe me.” Sure, I trust. Why not? I teach English composition at a private college. With a certain excitement and intensity. I read my students’ essays, hoping to find the person behind the pen. As each semester progresses, plagiarism（剽窃）appears. Not only is my intelligence insulted as one assumes I won’t detect a polished piece of prose from an otherwise-average writer, but I feel a sadness that a student has resorted to buying a paper from a peer. Writers have styles like fingerprints and after several assignments, I can match a student’s work with his or her name even if it’s missing from the upper left-hand corner.
Why is learning less important than a higher grade-point average（GPA）? When we’re threatened or sick, we make conditional promises. “If you let me pass math I will ….” “Lord, if you get me over this before the big homecoming game I’ll….” Once the situation is behind us, so are the promises. Human nature? Perhaps, but we do use that cliché（陈词滥调）to get us out of uncomfortable bargains. Divine interference during distress is asked; gratitude is unpaid. After all, few fulfill the contract, so why should anyone be the exception. Why not ?
Six years ago, I took a student before the dean. He had turned in an essay with the vocabulary and sentence structure of PhD thesis. Up until that time, both his out-of-class and in-class work were
borderline passing
.
I questioned the person regarding his essay and he swore it I’d understand this copy would not have the time and attention an out-of-class paper is given, but he had already a finished piece so he understood what was asked. He sat one hour, then turned in part of a page of unskilled writing and faulty logic. I confronted him with both essays. “I promise…., I’m not lying. I swear to you that I wrote the essay. I’m just nervous today.”
The head of the English department agreed with my finding, and the meeting with the dean had the boy’s parents present. After an hour of discussion, touching on eight of the boy’s previous essays and his grade-point average, which indicated he was already on academic probation（留校察看）, the dean agreed that the student had plagiarized. His parents protested, “He’s only a child” and we instructors are wiser and should be compassionate. College people are not really children and most times would resent being labeled as such…. Except in this uncomfortable circumstance.
1.According to the author, students commit plagiarism mainly for（  ）.
2.The sentence “Once the situation is behind us, so are the promises” implies that 
（  ）.
3.The “borderline passing”（ Line 3，Para. 3） probably means
（  ）.
4.The boy’s parents thought their son should be excused mainly because
（  ）.
5.Which of the following might serve as the title of this passage?

options.B: some students tend to break their promises
options.C: the promises are always behind the situation
options.D: we cannot judge the situation in advance, as we do to the promises

questions.article:
Powering the great ongoing changes of our time is the rise of human creativity as the defining feature of economic life. Creativity has come to be valued, because new technologies, new industries and new wealth flow from it. And as a result, our lives and society have begun to echo with creative ideas. It is our commitment to creativity in its varied dimensions that forms the underlying spirit of our age.
Creativity is essential to the way we live and work today, and in many senses always has been. The big advances in standard of living—not to mention the big competitive advantages in the marketplace—always have come from “better recipes, not just more cooking. ” One might argue that’s not strictly true. One might point out, for instance, that during the long period from the early days on the Industrial Revolution to modern times, much of the growth in productivity and material wealth in the industrial nations came not just from creative inventions like the steam engine, but from the widespread application of “cooking in quantity ” business methods like massive division of labor, concentration of assets, vertical integration and economies of scale. But those methods themselves were creative developments.

Directions:
In this section, you are asked to write an essay based on the following table. Describe the table and state your opinion. You should write at least 150 words on ANSWER SHEET 2.
Accidents in a Chinese City (2005)
In some countries girls are still（  ）of a good education.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. denied 否定；拒绝接受；节制	B. declined 减少；下降；衰落C. derived 获得；起源于；提取	D. deprived 剥夺；使丧失【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】be deprived of为固定搭配，表示“被剥夺”，代入句中符合题意，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项deny一般跟介词to搭配，如：Access to the information was denied to them.（他们无法得到这个情报。）；B选项decline可与介词by搭配，表示下降的幅度；C选项derive一般跟介词from搭配，如：He derived great pleasure from painting.（他从绘画中得到极大的乐趣。）；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】在一些国家，女孩仍然被剥夺了接受良好教育的机会。

questions.article: As the years passed, the memories of her childhood（  ）away.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. faded 逐渐消失；（使）褪色；枯萎	B. disappeared 消失；失踪；不复存在C. flashed 闪光；（快速）出示；闪现	D. fired 射击；启动；解雇【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】根据空格后的介词away可知，只有A选项能与之形成搭配，即fade away“逐渐消失”，符合题意，故A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不能与介词away进行搭配，故排除。【句意】随着岁月的流逝，她对童年的记忆也渐渐淡去了。

questions.article: Brierley’s book has the（  ）of being both informative and readable。

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. inspiration 灵感；启发灵感的人（或事物）	B. requirements 要求；必要条件C. myth 错误的观点；神话	D. merit 优秀品质；价值；优点【考查点】名词辨析【解题思路】根据空格后的信息“既翔实又易读”可知，这是在描述书本的“优点”，故D选项“优秀品质；价值；优点”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“灵感；启发灵感的人（或事物）”、B选项“要求；必要条件”和C选项“错误的观点；神话”均不符合语境。【句意】布赖尔利的书具有信息量大、可读性强的优点。

questions.article: If I have any comments to make, I’ll write them in the（  ）of the book I’m reading.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. edge 边缘；锋；优势；临界点	B. page （书、报纸、文件等的）页，面C. margin 页边空白；白边	D. side 一边；方向；侧面【考查点】名词辨析【解题思路】根据句意“如果我有什么评论，我会写在正在读的书的……”可知，这里是指“在书的空白处写评论”，故C选项“页边空白”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“边缘；锋；优势；临界点”、B选项“（书、报纸、文件等的）页，面”和D选项“一边；方向；侧面”均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】如果我有什么评论，我会写在正在读的书的页边空白处。

questions.article: My（  ）would really trouble me if I wore a fur coat.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. consciousness 知觉；思想；感知	B. consequence 结果；后果；重要性C. constitution 宪法；体质；组成	D. conscience 良知；内疚【考查点】名词辨析【解题思路】根据句意“如果我穿一件皮大衣，我的……真的会困扰我”可推知，这里是指“我的良心不安”，故D选项“良知；内疚”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“知觉；思想；感知”、B选项“结果；后果；重要性”和C选项“宪法；体质；组成”均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】如果我穿一件皮大衣，我的良心会很不安的。

questions.article: When the post fell（  ）, Dennis Bass was appointed to fill it.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. empty 空的；空洞的；空虚的；无知的	B. vacant （地方）空着的；（职位）空缺的C. hollow 空心的；凹的；虚伪的；无用的	D. bare 赤裸的；光秃秃的；无遮盖的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】根据“post（工作，职位）”和“Dennis Bass was appointed to fill it（丹尼斯·巴斯被任命填补空缺）”可知，这里是指“（职位）空缺的”，故B选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“空的；空洞的；空虚的；无知的”、C选项“空心的；凹陷的；虚伪的；无用的”和D选项“赤裸的；光秃秃的；无遮盖的”均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】当这个职位空缺时，丹尼斯·巴斯被任命填补。

questions.article: Mother who takes care of everybody is usually the most （  ）person in each family.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. considerate 为（他人）着想的；体贴的	B. considerable 相当重要的；值得尊敬的C. considering 考虑到；就……而言；鉴于	D. constant 持续不断的；不变的；忠诚的【考查点】词义辨析【解题思路】空格处应填入形容词修饰名词person，也就是上文提到的Mother；根据修饰Mother的定语从句“照顾每个人”可推知，只有A选项“为（他人）着想的；体贴的”符合题意。【干扰项排除】B选项“相当重要的；值得尊敬的”和D选项“持续不断的；不变的；忠诚的”不符合语境；C选项“考虑到；就……而言；鉴于”不是形容词；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】照顾每个人的母亲通常是每个家庭中最体贴的人。

questions.article: For ten years the Greeks（  ）the city of Troy to separate it from the outside.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. captured 俘获；夺取；吸引；记录；拍摄	B. occupied 侵占；占领；占据C. destroyed 摧毁；毁灭；破坏	D. surrounded 围绕；包围【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】根据“separate it from the outside（把特洛伊城与外界隔开）”可推知，D选项“围绕；包围”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“俘获；夺取；吸引；记录；拍摄”、B选项“侵占；占领；占据”和C选项“摧毁；毁灭；破坏”均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】希腊人将特洛伊城包围了十年，使之与外界隔绝。

questions.article: Other guests at yesterday’s opening, which was broadcast（  ）by the radio station, included Anne Melntosh and Mayor.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. live 活的；实况转播的	B. alive 活着的；活泼的；有生气的C. living 活的；现存的	D. lively 活泼的；生动的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】根据题干的broadcast（播放）可以推断，这里是指“现场直播”，live（实况转播的）符合题意，故A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“活着的；活泼的；有生气的”、C选项“活的；现存的”和D选项“活泼的；生动的”均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】当天，在广播电台现场直播的开幕式上，还有安妮•麦金托什和市长等人出席。

questions.article: A New Zealand man was recently（  ）to life imprisonment for the murder of an English tourist, Monica Cantwell.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. punished 处罚；对……判罪；自责	B. accused 控告；控诉；谴责C. sentenced 判决；宣判；判刑	D. put 放；猛推；将……送往【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】根据“life imprisonment（无期徒刑；终身监禁）”可知，这里是指“被判终身监禁”，故C选项“判决；宣判；判刑”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项punish一般跟介词for或with搭配，而题目中是to；B选项accuse和介词of搭配使用；D选项“放；猛推；将……送往”不符合语境；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】最近，一名新西兰男子因谋杀一名英国游客莫妮卡·坎特维尔被判终身监禁。

questions.article: The past 22 years have really been amazing, and every prediction we’ve made about improvements have all come（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. truly 真实地；完全地；诚挚地；准确地	B. true 真实的；正确的C. truth 事实；真实性；真理	D. truthful 诚实的；真实的【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】结合四个选项和题干中的come可知，这里考查固定搭配come true，表示“实现，成真；成为现实”，代入句中符合题意，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合固定搭配，故排除。【句意】过去的22年真的很神奇，我们所做的每一个关于改善的预测都实现了。

questions.article: The teachers tried to （  ）these students that they could solve the complicated problem, however, they just didn’t see the point.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. convince 使确信；说服	B. encourage 鼓励；怂恿；刺激C. consult 咨询；商量；查阅	D. concern 关于；涉及；使忧虑【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】根据“...these students that”可知，A选项可与之形成固定搭配convince somebody that...，表示“使某人确信……”，符合题意，故A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不能和“...these students that”构成固定搭配，故排除。【句意】老师试图使这些学生确信他们可以解决这个复杂的问题，然而，他们就是不明白这一点。

questions.article: I’m（  ）to think that most children would like their teachers to be their friends rather than their commanders.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. subjected 遭受，蒙受；遭遇，经历	B. supposed 预期，推断；假定；认为C. declined 下降，衰退；拒绝，谢绝	D. inclined 倾向于，有……的趋势【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】根据句意“我……认为大多数孩子喜欢他们的老师成为他们的朋友，而不是他们的指挥官”可知，D选项inclined to“倾向于，有……的趋势”更符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、	C选项意思不符合语境；B选项suppose的用法一般是be supposed to，表示“认为，推断”，如：This combination of qualities is generally supposed to be extremely rare.（一般认为，同时具有这样一些品质极为罕见。）；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】我倾向于认为大多数孩子喜欢他们的老师成为他们的朋友，而不是他们的指挥官。

questions.article: She is under the impression that he isn’t a（  ）person for he wouldn’t tell her where and when he went to university.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. genius 天赋，天资；天才，智商很高的人	B. generous 慷慨的；大量的C. genuine 真正的；真诚的，真心的	D. genetic 基因的；遗传学的【考查点】形容词辨析【解题思路】根据空格后的内容“他不肯告诉她他何时何地上的大学”可知，他在她的印象中应该不是真诚的人，故C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】在她的印象中，他不是一个真诚的人，因为他不肯告诉她他在哪里上的大学，什么时候上的。

questions.article: The first glasses of Coca Cola were drunk in 1886。 The drink was first（  ）by a US chemist called John Pemberton.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. formed 构成；创立；使成形	B. made 制造；铺床；造成C. found 发现；找出，查明	D. done 做【考查点】动词辨析【解题思路】根据“1886年，人们喝到了第一杯可口可乐”可知，这里是强调“这种饮料最早是由一位化学家发明的”，B选项make一般指创造某个东西，通常与食物、钱、声音等相关，符合语境，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项form一般指“构成；创立；使成形”，如：These hills were formed by glaciation.（这些山丘是冰川作用形成的。）；C选项find指“发现；找出，查明”，如：Can you find my bag for me?（你能帮我找我的包吗？）；D选项do一般指做某个活动或动作，通常和家务、工作、学习等相关，如：do the dishes（洗盘子）；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】1886年，人们喝到了第一杯可口可乐。这种饮料最早是由一位名叫约翰·彭伯顿的美国化学家发明的。

questions.article: These two chemicals（  ）with each other at a certain temperature to produce a substance which could cause an explosion.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. interact 相互交流，互动；相互作用	B. attract 吸引；使喜爱；引起……的好感C. react 作出反应；起化学反应	D. expel 把……开除；驱逐；排出【考查点】缺考查点【解题思路】根据句意“这两种化学物质在一定温度下……，产生一种能引起爆炸的物质”可知，这里是指“两种物质起化学反应”，C选项react with符合此意，故选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A选项interact with表示“相互影响；相互作用”，如：They learn how to interact with their peers.（他们学习如何与同龄人互动。）；B选项attract一般和介词to连用；D选项expel表示“把……开除；驱逐；排出”；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】这两种化学物质在一定温度下发生反应，产生一种能引起爆炸的物质。

questions.article: （  ）they can get people in the organization to do what must he done, they will not succeed.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. Since 自……以来；因为，由于	B. Unless 除非；如果不C. If 如果	D. Whether 不管，无论；是否【考查点】连词辨析【解题思路】前一句讲“他们可以让组织里的人做必须做的事情”，后一句讲“他们不会成功”，由此可知，这里暗含的意思是：如果他们不能让组织里的人做必须做的事情，他们就不会成功，故B选项“如果不”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】如果他们不能让组织里的人做必须做的事情，他们就不会成功。

questions.article: Once you have started a job, you should do it（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. in practice 在实践中；实际上，事实上	B. in theory 理论上C. in earnest 认真地；真正地	D. in a hurry 立即，匆忙【考查点】短语辨析【解题思路】根据句意“一旦你开始一份工作，你应该……完成它”可知，C选项“认真地”更符合语境，故选项C正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】一旦你开始一份工作，你应该认真地完成它。

questions.article: Although they new library service has been very successful, its future is （  ）certain.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. at any rate 无论如何，至少	B. by no means 决不，一点都不C. by all means 一定，务必；尽一切办法	D. at any cost 无论如何；不惜任何代价【考查点】短语辨析【解题思路】根据转折连词although可知前后句子的意思相反，前一句讲“新的图书馆服务非常成功”，所以后一句的意思应该是“它的前途是不确定的”，B选项by no means表达否定的含义，符合语境，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】虽然新的图书馆服务非常成功，但它的未来还不确定。

questions.article: To my surprise, at yesterday’s meeting he again（  ）the plan that had been disapproved a week before.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. brought about 导致，引起；发生	B. brought out 公布，出版；使出现；激发C. brought up 提出；养育，抚养	D. brought down 降低；打倒；击落【考查点】词组辨析【解题思路】根据空格后的“the plan”可知，这里是指“提出计划”，故C选项brought up “提出”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项与“the plan”搭配均不符合语境，故排除。【句意】令我惊讶的是，在昨天的会议上，他又提出了一周前被否决的计划。

On Tuesday, the Labor Department said the

Typically, increases in the Producer Price Index indicate similar changes in the consumer index

（20） . In fact, many businesses like automakers have been aggressively discounting their products.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】A. indicate 表明，说明（动词原形）	B. to indicate 表明，说明（不定式）C. indicating 表明，说明（现在分词）	D. indicated 表明，说明（过去分词）【考查点】非谓语动词的用法和关联上下文【解题思路】选项中的动词indicate意为“表明，说明”，代入文中，本句的意思是“七月份批发价格涨幅超过预期，也高于消费价格涨幅，这表明各个行业仍然在保护消费者……”。显而易见，indicate的主语是前面整个句子的内容，相当于非限定性定语从句which indicates...。因此空格处应该填入表示主动关系的现在分词indicating，故C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不符合以上分析，故排除。【句意】七月份批发价格涨幅超过预期，也高于消费价格涨幅，这表明各个行业仍然在保护消费者……。

【选项释义】A. of 属于；……的；……的一部分 B. to 朝；位于……；到某处；到某状态C. by 由，被；通过，凭借…… D. from 从……起；从……开始；来自【考查点】固定搭配【解题思路】根据句意“保护消费者……能源成本提高而带来的全面冲击”可知，D选项from能与原文中的动词protect构成固定搭配protect...from...，表示“保护……免受……”，符合句意，故D选项为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不能与之构成固定搭配，不符合题意，故排除。【句意】七月份批发价格涨幅超过预期，也高于消费价格涨幅，这表明各个行业仍然在保护消费者免受能源成本提高而带来的全面冲击。

【选项释义】A. that 用作关系代词，引导从句；那 B. which 哪一个；用作关系代词，引导从句C. it 它；这；那 D. this 这，这个；这样【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】空格所在的句子处于两个逗号之间，空格前为名词The Producer Price Index（生产价格指数），而空格后为动词measures（衡量），what引导的从句作measures的宾语，由此可知，空格所在句缺少主语；而The Producer Price Index与动词measures存在逻辑上的主谓关系，因而空格处应填入定语从句的关系词，其代替先行词The Producer Price Index作从句的主语，又由于先行词与定语从句之间有逗号隔开，则这个关系词必须能够用来引导非限定性定语从句，故B选项which为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A选项that不能引导非限制性定语从句；C、D选项不能引导从句；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】衡量生产者从商品和服务中获得收益的生产者价格指数在7月份……了百分之一。

【选项释义】A. rise 增加，提高；升起 B. rises 增加，提高；升起（rise的第三人称单数）C. rose 增加，提高；升起（rise的过去式） D. raised 举起；增加，提高（raise的过去式）【考查点】关联上下文和近义词辨析【解题思路】根据所给四个备选项的提示，空格处需填入动词来表示“增长，增加”之意；再根据下文“...reported yesterday”可以确定此处的动词应该用一般过去式。C选项rose是不及物动词rise的一般过去式，意为“（数量）增加，增长”，将其代入文中符合句意，故C选项是正确答案。【干扰项排除】A、B选项不是一般过去式，不符合语境；D选项raised是及物动词raise的一般过去式，其后必须加宾语，可用于被动语态，如：The price was raised to $10.（价格涨到十英镑。）；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】衡量生产者从商品和服务中获得收益的生产者价格指数在7月份上升了百分之一。

【选项释义】A. that 引导从句；那 B. what ……的事物；多么；引导从句C. which 哪一个；引导从句 D. this 这，这个；这样【考查点】名词性从句和倍数表达【解题思路】此处double为前置限定词，后接名词表示倍数；由于空格后接的是一个句子，所以需要连接词来引导名词性从句，而从句中谓语动词had been expecting缺少宾语，其意思是“经济学家预计的数量或数目”，what表示“……的事物”，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项that引导名词性从句时不充当句子成分；C选项which引导名词性从句时指代有范围的事物，表示“哪一个”；D选项不能引导从句；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】比经济学家的预期高出一倍，并且与6月份持平的价格相比出现了大幅回升。

【选项释义】A. food 养料；食物，食品 B. grain 谷物，谷粒；颗粒，细粒C. crop 庄稼；收成，产量 D. diet 日常饮食【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】根据原词再现，从第三段末的“excluding food and energy”可知，A选项food为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合以上分析，故排除。【句意】除食品和能源之外，生产价格核心指数上升了0.4%。

【选项释义】A. less 较少，更少；较小，更小 B. lower 更低的C. higher 更高的 D. more 更多的【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】空格前讲“生产价格核心指数上升了0.4%”，空格讲“...than the 0.1 percent”；由“than”可知，这里是将涨幅0.4%与0.1%作比较，而就上升程度而言，0.4%显然高于0.1%。C选项higher是形容词high的比较级形式，意为“较高的”，主要用于形容数量、程度或水平较高，符合题意，故C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、D选项均不能表示上涨幅度更高，不符合题意，故排除。【句意】生产者价格的核心指数上升了0.4%，高于经济学家……的0.1%。

【选项释义】A. said 说，讲；宣称，说明；认为 B. reported 报道；据说；报告C. calculated 计算；估计 D. forecast 预测，预报【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】上文讲“比经济学家的预期（had been expecting）高出一倍，并且与6月份持平的价格相比出现了大幅回升”，而本句提到“生产价格核心指数上升了0.4%，高于经济学家……的0.1%”，由此可知，空格中所需的动词应该与前文提到的expecting（预计，预期）意思相近，故D选项forecast“预测，预报”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均不符合上下语境，故排除。【句意】生产价格核心指数上升了0.4%，高于经济学家预测的0.1%。

【选项释义】A. expectable 能预期的；意料中的 B. unexpected 想不到的，意外的C. expectation 期待，预期；期望，指望 D. expecting 期待；企盼【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】本句表达的意思是“轿车和卡车价格……上涨是导致生产价格指数上涨的重要因素”。根据上文可知，生产价格指数的上涨高于经济学家的预计，由此可以推断，引起生产价格指数上涨的原因应该是始料未及的。B选项unexpected意为“想不到的，意外的”，符合题意，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合以上分析，不符合题意，故排除。【句意】轿车和卡车价格意外上涨是导致生产价格指数上涨的重要因素。

【选项释义】A. prices 价格 B. costs 费用；损失C. charges 费用；手续费；运费 D. values 价值；价值观念【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】上一句提到“轿车和卡车价格意外上涨是导致生产价格指数上涨的重要因素”，讨论的是价格（prices）上涨的问题，由此可推知，这里讲的也是“消费者支付的商品和服务价格上涨的幅度”，故A选项“价格”符合题意。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均与上文的内容不相符，故排除。【句意】周二，美国劳工部表示，7月份消费者支付的商品和服务价格整体上涨0.5%。

【选项释义】A. down 在下方；在较低的位置；向下 B. from 从……起；从……开始；来自C. to 朝；位于……；到某处 D. up 向上，上升【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】从本句后半部分可知，消费者为商品和服务所支付的价格，如果除去食品和能源两项则增长了0.1%。由此推断，上句提到的总体趋势也应该是上升的。D选项up意为“向上，上升”，与下文的“up 0.1 percent”相呼应，故选项D正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均符合上下文语境，故排除。【句意】周二，美国劳工部表示，7月份消费者支付的商品和服务价格整体上涨0.5%，扣除食品和能源的价格上涨0.1%。

【选项释义】A. Much 许多 B. Most 最多；大多数C. Most of 大部分，大多数的；绝大多数 D. Much of 许多【考查点】限定词的用法和关联上下文【解题思路】由于空格后的名词短语the overall rise中有定冠词the且根据句意“总体上涨……由能源成本的上升引起的”可知，C选项Most of“大部分，大多数”可与之后的名词短语the overall rise连用，符合上下文语境，故C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B选项一般后面直接跟名词，不需要加冠词the；D选项Much of表示“许多”，修饰“the overall rise”不合逻辑；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】消费者价格和生产者价格的总体上涨大部分是由能源成本造成的。

【选项释义】A. was 是（is的过去式） B. were 是（are的过去式）C. is 是 D. are 是【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】从上文可知，本句所陈述的内容发生在七月份，是过去的情况，因此这里应该使用过去时态。另外，本句的主语是the overall rise，属于可数名词单数，所以谓语动词应该采用第三人称单数形式。综上所述，A选项was符合题意，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合以上分析，故排除。【句意】消费者价格和生产者价格的总体上涨大部分是由能源成本造成的。

【选项释义】A. fall 下降，降落（动词原形） B. fell 下降，降落（fall的过去式）C. falls 下降，降落（fall的第三人称单数） D. has fallen 下降，降落（fall的现在完成时）【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】根据此句中的时间状语in July可以确定本句时态应该为一般过去时。B选项fell是动词fall的一般过去式，符合题意，所以该选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项的时态均与时间状语in July不相符，故排除。【句意】7月份食品批发价格下降0.3%。

【选项释义】A. Comparing with 与…相比 B. In comparison 相比之下；与……比较C. Compared with 与…相比 D. Compare to 与…相比【考查点】非谓语形式【解题思路】由于空格后的句子是完整的，所以这里应填入compare的非谓语形式；而compare与Wholesale prices属于动宾关系，故C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项表示主谓关系，不符合题意；B选项In comparison后应加介词to或with，如：The tallest buildings in London are small in comparison with New York’s skyscrapers.（伦敦最高的建筑物与纽约的摩天大厦一比就相形见绌。）；D选项Compare to不属于非谓语形式，不符合题意；以上均不符合【句意】相比于2004年7月，批发价格上涨了4.6%。

【选项释义】A. dropped 掉落；减少，降低 B. declined 下降；拒绝；衰退C. lifted 举起；抬升；提高，增加 D. climbed 爬升，升高；上升，升值【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】由本句前半句可知，与2004年7月相比，批发价格上涨了4.6%，句子最后提到“这是自1995年以来最快的增长速度”；再结合上文中提到的核心指数的相关信息可推知，此处所表达的“the core rate（核心增长率）”应该是上升的。D选项climbed意为“上升，爬升”，符合原文逻辑，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B选项均不符合上下语境；C选项lifted是及物动词，后面必须加宾语或使用被动语态，如：Interest rates were lifted yesterday.（昨天利率提高了。）；以上均不符合题意，故排除。

【选项释义】A. as 在……时候；虽然，尽管；因为，由于 B. so 所以；因此C. while 与……同时；而，然而 D. when 什么时候；何时【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】本句前半部分的意思是“通常情况下，生产价格指数的增长预示着消费者物价指数也会发生类似的变化”。后半句的意思是“……企业最终会把增加的成本转移给消费者”。由此可推知，前后句子存在因果逻辑关系，因为如果生产价格指数增长，那就意味着企业成本的提高，而企业则会把增加的成本以更高的消费价格形式转嫁给消费者，让消费者进行补偿，结果就会导致消费者物价指数的上升。由此可见，前半句是结果，后半句是原因。A选项as意为“因为，由于”，引导原因状语从句，符合题意，故该选项正确。【干扰项排除】B、C、D选项均不符合以上分析，故排除。【句意】通常情况下，生产价格指数的增长预示着消费者物价指数也会发生类似的变化，因为企业最终会把增加的成本转移给消费者。

【选项释义】A. And 和，与 B. But 但是C. Yet 仍然，还 D. Still 还，依旧；仍然【考查点】句间逻辑【解题思路】根据本句的主干部分“that has not been the...”和40题的选项提示可知，本句的意思是说“这次物价上涨的情况不同”。可见，本句与上文为转折关系。B选项But是并列连词，常用于表示转折，符合题意，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合以上分析，故排除。【句意】但是这次物价上涨……。

【选项释义】A. at 在（表示存在的地点、位置、空间） B. by在……期间C. in 在……期间；在……以后 D. to 到，至【考查点】介词的用法和关联上下文【解题思路】空格所在从句的谓语动词started使用了一般过去式，由此可知，本句的时间状语应该为过去的某个时间。A选项at与句中的the end of构成词组at the end of，意为“在……末/底”，代入文中表示“在2001年底”，符合题意，故A选项正确。【干扰项排除】B选项by常常用于完成时；C选项in后面一般加较长的时间段；D选项to the end表示动作延续“到……的终点为止”，不能用于短暂性动词；以上均不符合题意，故排除。【句意】但在2001年底开始的大部分扩张中，……并非如此。

【选项释义】A. condition 状况，状态；条件，环境 B. situation 情况，形势；突发情况C. matter 事情，问题；事态，情况 D. case 具体情况，实例【考查点】关联上下文【解题思路】结合这四个备选项，本句的意思是“但是在2001年底爆发的这次物价上涨，……却并非如此”。D选项case意为“具体情况，实例”，用于表示某个事件或某种状况具体的、典型的事例，这里是指2001年底的这次物价上涨与上句所表述的一般情况不同，符合语境，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项condition一般指物质环境或身体状况，如：The plants grow best in cool, damp conditions.（这种植物最适合在阴凉、潮湿的环境下生长。）；B选项situation尤指形势、状况，如：We have all been in similar embarrassing situations.（我们都遇到过类似的尴尬局面。）；C选项matter一般指问题或事态，通常用复数形式，如：Matters were made worse by a fire in the warehouse.（仓库失火使得事态更为严重。）；以上均不符合上下文语境，故排除。【句意】但在2001年底开始的大部分扩张中，情况并非如此。

questions.article:
Office jobs are among the positions hardest hit by computation (计算机自动化). Word processors and typists will lose about 93,000 jobs over the next few years, while 57,000 secretarial jobs will vanish. Blame the PC: Today, many executives type their own memos and carry there” secretaries” in the palms of their hands. Time is also hard for stock clerks, whose ranks are expected to decrease by 68,000. And employees in manufacturing firms and wholesalers are being replaced with computerized systems.
But not everyone who loses a job will end up in the unemployment line. Many will shift to growing positions within their own companies. When new technologies shook up the telecomm business, telephone operator Judy Dougherty pursued retraining. She is now a communications technician, earning about $ 64,000 per year. Of course, if you’ve been a tollbooth collector for the past 30 years, and you find yourself replaced by an E-ZPass machine, it may be of little consolation(安慰) to know that the telecom field is booming.
And that’s just it: The service economy is fading: welcome to the expertise(专门知识) economy. To succeed in the new job market, you must be able to handle complex problems. Indeed, all but one of the 50 highest-paying occupations—air-traffic controller—demand at least a bachelor’s degree.
For those with just a high school diploma(毕业证书).It’s going to get tougher to find a well-paying job. Since fewer factory and clerical jobs will be available .what’s left be the jobs that computations can’t kill, computers cant clean offices, or for Alzheimer’s patients(老年痴呆病人). But, since most people have the skills to fill those positions, the wages stay painfully low, meaning computation could drive an even deeper wedge (楔子) between the and poor, The best advice now, Never stop learning, and keep up with new technology.
For busy adults of course that can be tough, the good news is that very technology that’s reducing so many jobs is a making it easier to go back to school without having to sit in a classroom. So called internet distance learning is hot, with more than three million students currently enrolled , and it’s gaining credibility with employers.
Are you at risk of losing your job to a computer? Check the federal Bureau of Labor Statistics’ Occupational Outlook Handbook ,which is available online at bls.gov.
1.From the first paragraph we can infer that all of the following persons are easily thrown into unemployment EXCEPT（  ）.
2.In the second paragraph the anther mentions the tollbooth collector to
（  ）.
3.By saying “...computation could drive an even deeper wedge between the rich and poor” (line 5. Para 4 )the author means （  ）.
4.What is the author’s attitude towards computers？
5.Which of the following might serve as the best title of passage？

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】From the first paragraph we can infer that all of the following persons are easily thrown into unemployment EXCEPT ____.	从第一段我们可以推断出下列所有的人都很容易陷入失业，除了____。A. secretaries	A. 秘书B. stock clerks	B. 存货管理员；仓库管理员C. managers	C. 经理D. wholesalers	D. 批发商【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】第一段提到其工作很容易被电脑取代的人或职业为“word processors, typists, secretarial jobs, stock clerks, employees in manufacturing firms, wholesalers”。A、B、D选项位列其中，而C选项没有提及。C选项managers与首段第三句中的executives含义相似，该词的出现是为了说明主管利用计算机为自己工作，并非意指失业，故C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】根据以上分析可知，A、B、D选项均属于容易被电脑取代的人或职业，属于反向干扰。

options.A: mean he will get benefits from the telecomm fled
options.B: show he is too old to shift to a new position
options.C: console him on having been replaced by a machine
options.D: blame the PC for his unemployment

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】In the second paragraph the author mentions the tollbooth collector to ____. 在第二段中，作者提到收费站收费员____。A. mean he will get benefits from the telecom field A. 以说明他将从电信领域获得利益B. show he is too old to shift to a new position B. 以表明他太老了而无法换到新的位置C. console him on having been replaced by a machine C. 以安慰他已被机器取代D. blame the PC for his unemployment D. 是为了将他的失业归咎于个人电脑【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干的信息定位到第二段第五句。该段首先以Judy经过培训实现了再就业为例说明主旨句的观点：并不是所有失业的人都会下岗，许多人会转向其所在公司内部更有发展前途的职位。紧接着提到本题考查的举例，并且用“Of course”连接，由此可知作者试图用Judy的事例证明30多年从事收费员工作的人可能很难再接受培训，也很难转岗了。30年暗示了年龄是造成下岗的原因，故B选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“以说明他将从电信领域获得利益”和D选项“是为了将他的失业归咎于个人电脑”均不符合原文内容，属于曲解原文；C选项定位到第二段最后一句“……虽然电信领域正在蓬勃发展，但这并不能给人带来多少安慰”，属于反向干扰。

options.A: people are getting richer and richer
options.B: there will be a small gap between rich and poor
options.C: the gap between rich and poor is getting larger an larger
options.D: it’s time to close up be gap between the rich and poor

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】By saying “...computation could drive an even deeper wedge between the rich and poor” (line 5. Para 4) the author means ____. 作者说“……计算可能会在富人和穷人之间造成更深的鸿沟”（第4段第5行）的意思是____。A. people are getting richer and richer A. 人们越来越富有B. there will be a small gap between rich and poor B. 富人和穷人之间会有一个小差距C. the gap between rich and poor is getting larger and larger C. 富人和穷人之间的差距越来越大D. it’s time to close up be gap between the rich and poor D. 现在是缩小贫富差距的时候了【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】从第四段前四句可知，只有高中文凭的人很难找到工作，因为计算机自动化的发展使得工厂和办公室的职位越来越少，而剩下的那些计算机应付不了的工作，几乎人人都可以胜任，所以工资非常低。而且该段第四句指出这意味着计算机自动化会进一步拉大贫富差距，而不是缩小，故C选项“贫富差距将越来越大”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“人们越来越富有”、B选项“富人和穷人之间会有一个小差距”和D选项“现在是缩小贫富差距的时候了”均不符合原文内容，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】What is the author’s attitude towards computers? 作者对计算机的态度是什么？A. Positive. A. 肯定的。B. Negative. B. 否定的。C. Neutral. C. 中立的。D. Prejudiced. D. 有成见的。【考查点】观点态度题【解题思路】作者在前两段描述了计算机自动化的发展给人们带来的失业问题，而后四段作者对此提出了积极的建议和措施，比如在第四段最后一句，作者明确指出永远不要停止学习的脚步，要紧跟新技术发展的步伐；同时在第五段中，作者提到计算机自动化的发展促使网络远程教育的兴起。这都说明作者对计算机自动化是持积极态度的，故A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B选项“否定的”、C选项“中立的”和D选项“有成见的”均不符合文章内容，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】Which of the following might serve as the best title of passage? 以下哪项可以作为文章的最佳标题？A. Blaming the PC. A. 指责电脑。B. The booming telecom field. B. 蓬勃发展的电信领域。C. Internet distance learning. C. 互联网远程学习。D. Keeping up with computation. D. 跟上计算的步伐。【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】文章首段先介绍了计算机使很多人失业的事实；第二段用But转移了话题，将重点转向描述暂时失去工作的人经过再培训还可以重新就业的事实；接着在后四段给出建议和措施以应对计算机自动化的发展，尤其在第四段最后一句明确指出“永远不要停止学习，要跟上新技术”。由此可见本文的主旨是不断学习，跟上新技术发展的步伐就不会受到新技术的威胁，故D选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“指责电脑”、B选项“蓬勃发展的电信领域”和C选项“互联网远程学习”虽有提及，但都不是文章详细描述的内容，属于以偏概全。

questions.article:
Tens of thousands of 18 year olds will graduate this year and be handed meaningless diplomas .These diplomas won’t look any different from awarded their luckier classmates Their validity will be questioned only when their employers discover the these graduates are semiliterate(半文盲).
Eventually a fortunate few will find their way into educational – repair – adult – literacy Programs, such as the one where I teach grammar and writing .There, high school graduates and high school dropouts pursuing graduate equivalency certificates will learn the skills they should have learned in school. They will discover they have been cheated by our educational system.
I will never forget a teacher senior when be had her for English .“He site in the back of the room talking to his friends “.she told me,” Why don’t you move him to the front row?
I urged believing the embarrassment would get him to settle down, Mrs. Stifter said , “I don’t move seniors. I think (使……不及格) them.” Our son’s academic life flashed before my eyes. No teacher had ever threatened him. By the time I got home I was feeling pretty good this. It was a radical approach for these times, but well. Why not? "She’s going to flunk you", I told my son.
I did not discuss it any further. Suddenly English became a priority (头等重要) in his life. He finished out the semester with an A.
I know one example doesn’t make a case, but at night I see a parade of students who are angry for having been passed along until they could no longer even pretend to keep up. Of average intelligence or better, they eventually quit school, concluding they were too dumb to finish. ”I should have been held back,” is a comment I hear frequently. Even sadder are those students who are high-school graduates who say to me after a few weeks of class. ”I don’t know how I ever got a high-school diploma.”
Passing students who have not mastered the work cheats them and the employers who expect graduates to have basic skills. We excuse this dishonest behavior by saying kids can’t learn if they come from terrible environments. No one seems to stop to think that most kids don’t put school first on their list unless they perceive something is at risk. They’d rather be sailing.
Many students I see at night have decided to make education a priority. They are motivated by the desire for a better job or the need to hang on to the one they’ve got. They have a healthy fear of failure.
People of all ages can rise above their problems, but they need to have a reason to do so. Yong people generally don’t have the maturity to value education in the same way my adult students value it. But fear of failure can motivate both.
1.What is the subject of this essay？
2.How did Mrs. Sifter get the attention of one of the author’s children？
3.The author believes that most effective way for a teacher is to
（  ）.
4.From the passage we can draw the conclusion that the authors’ attitude toward flunking is（  ）.
5.Judging from the content, this passage is probably written for（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What is the subject of this essay?	这篇文章的主题是什么？A. View point on learning.	A. 关于学习的见解。B. A qualified teacher.	B. 一个合格的老师。C. The importance of examination.	C. 考试的重要性。D. The generation gap.	D. 代沟。【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】文章开篇描述了目前令人堪忧的高中教育状况。紧接着作者分别以自己的孩子和夜校补习班学生为例阐述了危机感可以激发学习的主动性。由此可见，本文的主题与学习有关，且阐释了危机感能激发孩子的学习主动性和积极性这一见解。由此可知，A选项“关于学习的见解”正确。【干扰项排除】B选项“一个合格的老师”文中虽然有提到Sifter老师对作者儿子的积极影响，但不是全文重点阐述的内容，属于以偏概全；C选项“考试的重要性”和D选项“代沟”原文均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】How did Mrs. Sifter get the attention of one of the author’s children? Sifter老师是如何引起作者的一个孩子的注意的？A. Flunking him. A. 让他不及格。B. Moving his seat. B. 移动他的座位。C. Blaming him. C. 责备他。D. Playing card with him. D. 和他玩牌。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据Mrs. Sifter可定位到第四段。该段第二句讲“Sifter老师说：‘我不移动高年级学生。我让他们考试不及格’”。由此可知，Sifter老师引起作者孩子注意力的方法就是让他考试不及格，故A选项符合原文。【干扰项排除】B选项“移动他的座位”与原文“我不移动高年级学生”不相符，属于反向干扰；C选项“责备他”和D选项“和他玩牌”原文均没有提及，属于无中生有。

options.B: set up cooperation between teachers and parents.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The author believes that most effective way for a teacher is to ____. 作者认为，对教师来说，最有效的方法是____。A. purify the teaching environments A. 净化教学环境B. set up cooperation between teachers and parents B. 建立教师与家长之间的合作关系C. hold back student C. 牵制学生D. motivate student D. 激励学生【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】文章开门见山地指出当前的教育状况——学生的毕业证与实际能力不匹配，他们毕业后有了危机感才进行学习；针对这一现状，接下来作者以自己的孩子和夜校学生为例说明危机感可激发学习动力；最后一段作者提出：无论是年轻人还是成年学生，危机感对他们都能够起到激励作用。由此可见，在作者看来，作为教师，最有效的教学方法应该是通过危机感来激励学生，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“净化教学环境”、B选项“建立教师与家长之间的合作关系”和C选项“牵制学生”均无法从文中推断出来，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】From the passage we can draw the conclusion that the authors’ attitude toward flunking is ____. 从文章中我们可以得出结论，作者对不及格的态度是____。A. negative A. 否定的B. positive B. 肯定的C. biased C. 有偏见的D. indifferent D. 冷漠的【考查点】观点态度题【解题思路】根据“flunking”定位到第四段第二句。紧接着作者结合自己儿子的经历谈及对此的感受，其中第五句提到作者认为让学生不及格的做法非常好（pretty good）；第六句则以反问句“虽然这种方法在这个年代有些激进，但为什么不试试呢？”的形式表明自己对于让学生不及格的态度是“positive（肯定的）”，因此B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“否定的”、C选项“有偏见的”和D选项“冷漠的”均不符合原文内容，属于曲解原文。

【选项释义】Judging from the content, this passage is probably written for ____. 从内容来看，这篇文章可能是为____写的。A. administrators A. 管理者，行政人员B. students B. 学生C. teachers C. 老师D. parents D. 父母【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】作者通过描述自己儿子和补习班学生之前对待学业的不同态度反映出老师在教学方法上的差异。前者的英语老师通过让学生不及格的方法调动了学生的学习积极性，而后者由于其之前的老师不负责而使其未能完成学业或学无所成。全文着重阐述了如何有效地调动学生的学习积极性，强调了教师的责任及其重要性。综上所述，“老师”作为本文的写作对象最为贴切，故C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“管理者，行政人员”、B选项“学生”和D选项“父母”均与文章内容不相符，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
Names have gained increasing importance in the competitive world of higher education. As colleges strive for market share, they are looking for names that project the image they want or reflect the changes they hope to make. Trenton.State College, for example ,became the College of New Jersey nine years ago when it began raising admissions standards and appealing to students from throughout the state.
“All I hear in higher education is, “Brand ,brand ,brand,” said Tim Westerbeck, who specializes in branding and is managing director of Lipman Hearne, a marketing firm based in Chicago that works with universities and other nonprofit organizations. “There has been a sea change over the last 10 years. Marketing used to be almost a dirty word in higher education.”
Not all efforts at name changes are successful, of course. In 1997, the New School for Social Research became New School University to reflect its growth into a collection of eight colleges, offering a list of majors that includes psychology, music, urban studies and management. But New Yorkers continued to call it the New School.
Now, after spending an undisclosed sum on an online survey and a marketing consultant’s creation of “naming structures.” “brand architecture” and “ identity systems,” the university has come up with a new name: the New School. Beginning Monday, it will adopt new logon (标 识), banners, business cards and even new names for the individual colleges, all to include the words “the New School.”
Changes in names generally reveal significant shifts in how a college wants to be perceived. In altering its name from Cal State. Hayward, to Cal State, East Bay, the university hoped to project its expanding role in two mostly suburban countries east of San Francisco.
The University of Southern Colorado, a state institution, became Colorado State University at Pueblo two years ago, hoping to highlight many internal changes, including offering more graduate programs and setting higher admissions standards.
Beaver College turned itself into Arcadia University in 2001 for several reasons: to break the connection with its past as a women’s college, to promote its growth into a full-fledged(完全成熟的) university and officials acknowledged, to eliminate some jokes about the college’s old name on late-night television and “morning zoo” radio shows.
Many college officials said changing a name and image could produce substantial results. At Arcadia, in addition to the rise in applications, the average student’s test score has increased by 60 points, Juli Roebeck, an Arcadia spokeswoman said.
1.Which of the following is NOT the reason for colleges to change their names？
2.It is implied that one of the most significant changes in higher education in the past decade is（  ）.
3.The phrase "come up with"(Line 3 Para 4) probably means
（  ）.
4.The case of name changing from Cal State Hayward to Cal State indicates that the university
（  ）.
5.According to the spokeswoman the name change of Beaver College（  ）.

options.B: They try to gain advantage in market share.
options.C: They want to project their image.
options.D: They hope to make some changes.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】Which of the following is NOT the reason for colleges to change their names?	下列哪一项不是大学改名的原因？A. They prefer higher education competition.	A. 他们更喜欢高等教育的竞争。B. They try to gain advantage in market share.	B. 他们试图在市场份额上取得优势。C. They want to project their image.	C. 他们想树立自己的形象。D. They hope to make some changes.	D. 他们希望做出一些改变。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】第一段第一句讲“在竞争激烈的高等教育领域（in the competitive world of higher education），名字越来越重要”，该句旨在说明学校更名是迫于竞争的压力，而非高校喜欢参与高等教育领域的竞争。因此A选项不是高校更名的原因，符合题干要求，为正确答案。【干扰项排除】第一段第二句讲“随着大学努力争取市场份额（strive for market share），他们正在寻找能够反映他们想要的形象（project the image they want）或反映他们希望做出的改变的名字（the changes they hope to make）”。B选项为该句中strive for market share的同义转述；C、D选项分别是原文project the image they want与the changes they hope to make的同义替换。三者均是高校更名的原因，属于反向干扰。

【选项释义】It is implied that one of the most significant changes in higher education in the past decade is ____. 这表明，在过去的十年中，高等教育最重要的变化之一是____。A. the brand A. 品牌B. the college names B. 大学的名字C. the concept of marketing C. 营销的概念D. list of majors D. 专业名单【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干的信息定位于第二段倒数第二句。该句指出：过去十年里，高等教育领域发生了巨大的变化。紧接着下一句就说到：在这个领域，营销曾经（used to）是一个近乎肮脏的字眼。“used to（过去常常）”的使用说明人们过去认为营销是肮脏的这一观念如今已发生了转变，而这一观念的转变正是过去十年里高等教育的一个最显著的变化，因此C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项定位到第二段第一句“我在高等教育中听到的都是‘品牌，品牌，品牌’”，与本题无关，属于出处错位；B选项根据文章内容可知，在竞争激烈的高等教育领域中，名字越来越重要，许多学校通过该名字来进行应对，但并没有说“大学的名字”是高等教育最重要的变化，属于曲解原文；D选项定位到第三段第二句“……提供一系列专业，包括心理学、音乐、城市研究和管理”，与本题无关，属于出处错位。

【选项释义】The phrase “come up with” (Line 3 Para 4) probably means ____. 短语“come up with”（第3行第4段）可能是指____。A. catch up with A. 赶上；逮捕；处罚B. deal with B. 处理；涉及C. put forward C. 提出（计划、建议、名称）D. come to the realization D. 开始意识到；实现【考查点】语义推断题【解题思路】根据题干定位到第四段第一句。分析句子结构可知，动词短语come up with的宾语是a new name，而在时间状语中naming structure、brand architecture以及identity systems与name的使用领域一致，它们都属于新名称的范畴，因此come up with应和上述名词短语所修饰的中心词creation（创建）的意思接近，且能和a new name搭配表示“命名”之意，故C选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】A选项“赶上；逮捕；处罚”、B选项“处理；涉及”和D选项“开始意识到；实现”均不符合原文内容，属于曲解原文。

options.C: prefers to reform its reaching programs

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】The case of name changing from Cal State Hayward to Cal State indicates that the university ____. 加州州立大学海沃德分校更名为加州州立大学的案例表明该大学____。A. is perceived by the society A. 被社会所认可B. hopes to expand its influence B. 希望扩大其影响力C. prefers to reform its teaching programs C. 更愿意改革其教学计划D. expects to enlarge its campus D. 希望扩大其校园【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干可定位在第五段第二句。该句是对首句提出的观点“改名一般都显示一所大学想让社会对其看法有一个巨大的改变”进行例证，而该句本身也提到：加州州立大学海沃德分校将校名更改为加州州立大学东海湾分校，该校希望以此显示其在旧金山以东两个重要郊县日益提升的地位（project its expanding role）。B选项是对原文中的hoped to project its expanding role的同义转述，符合题意，故B选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项根据第五段的内容可知，加州州立大学海沃德分校更名的目的是想让社会对其看法有一个巨大的改变，但不能推断出改名之后一定会被社会所认可，属于推理过度；C选项“更愿意改革其教学计划”和D选项“希望扩大其校园”原文均没有提及，属于无中生有。

【选项释义】According to the spokeswoman the name change of Beaver College ____. 根据女发言人的说法，海狸学院的名称变更____。A. turns out very successful A. 结果非常成功B. fails to attain its goal B. 没有达到它的目标C. has eliminated some jokes C. 消除了一些笑话D. has transformed its status D. 改变了它的地位【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据spokeswoman可定位至最后一段第二句。该句以朱莉•罗贝卡的发言为例，通过申请入学人数的增加以及学生成绩的大幅提高，说明海狸学院更名后（由上一段可知海狸学院更名为阿卡迪亚大学）对学校以及学生本身都产生了非常积极的影响。由此可知，海狸学院的更名是非常成功的，故A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B选项“没有达到它的目标”和D选项“改变了它的地位”原文没有提及，属于无中生有；C选项定位到倒数第二段“海狸学院于2001年更名为阿卡迪亚大学，原因有几个……消除深夜电视节目和‘早间动物园’广播节目中有关学院旧名的一些笑话”，由此可知该选项是海狸学院改名的原因之一，而与女发言人的说法无关，属于出处错位。

questions.article:
It looked just like another aircraft from the outside. The pilot told his young passengers that it was built in 1964.But appearances were deceptive and the 13 students from Europe and the USA who boarded the aircraft were in for the flight of their lives.
Inside the area that normally had seats had become a long white tunnel. Heavily padded(填塞) from floor to ceiling it looked a bit strange. There were almost no windows , but lights along the padded walls illuminated it. Most of the seats had been taken out apart from a few at the back where the young scientists quickly took their places with a look of fear.
For 12 months, science students from across the continents had competed to win a place on the flight at the invitation of the European Space Agency. The challenge had been to suggest imaginative experiments to be conducted in weightless conditions.
For the next two hours the flight resembled that of an enormous bird which had lose its reason, shooting upwards towards the heavens before rushing towards Earth. The invention was to Achieve weightlessness for a few seconds.
The aircraft took off smoothly enough. But any feelings that I and the young scientists had that we were on anything like a scheduled passenger service were quickly dismissed when the pilot put the plane into a 45 degree climb which lasted around 20 seconds. Then the engines cut out and we became weightless. Everything become confused and left or right. Up or down no longer had any meaning, after ten seconds of free fall descent the pilot pulled the aircraft out of its nosedive. The return of gravity was less immediate than its loss, but was still sudden enough to ensure that some students came down with a bump.
Each time the pilot cut the engines and we became weightless. A new team conduct it experience. First it was the Ducth who wanted to discover how it is that cats always land on their feet. Then the German team who conducted a successful experiment on a traditional building method to see if could be used for building a further space station, the Americans had an idea to create solar sails that could be used by satellites.
After two hours of going up and down in the lane doing their experiments, the predominate feeling was one of excitement rather than sickness. Most of the students thought it was an unforgettable experience and one they would be keen to repeat.
1.What did the writer say about the plane？
2.According to the writer ,how did the young scientists feel before the flight？
3.What did the pilot do with the plane after it took off？
4.According to the passage, the purpose of being weightless was to（  ）.
5.This passage was written to（  ）.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What did the writer say about the plane?	关于这架飞机，作者说了什么？A. It had no seats.	A. 它没有座位。B. It was painted white.	B. 它被漆成白色。C. It had no windows.	C. 它没有窗户。D. The outside was misleading.	D. 它的外部具有误导性。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干的信息可定位至第一、二段，这两段对飞机作了详细的描述。其中第一段第三句提到“appearances were deceptive（外表具有欺骗性）”。D选项中的“The outside”与原文中的“appearances”对应，而“misleading”则对应原文中的“deceptive”。显然，D选项是对原文的同义转述，故为正确答案。【干扰项排除】A选项定位到第二段最后一句“除了后面的几个座位外，大多数座位都被拿走了……”，属于曲解原文；B选项定位到第二段第一句“通常有座位的区域内变成了一条长长的白色隧道”，而不是说“飞机被漆成白色”，属于曲解原文；C选项定位到第二段第三句“几乎没有窗户（almost no windows），但沿着填充墙的灯光照亮了它”，原文用到了“几乎”一词，而该选项说法太绝对，属于推理过度。

【选项释义】According to the writer, how did the young scientists feel before the flight? 根据作者的说法，这些年轻的科学家在飞行前是什么感觉？A. Sick. A. 厌烦的。B. Keen. B. 渴望的。C. Nervous. C. 紧张的。D. Impatient. D. 不耐烦的；迫不及待的。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干的信息定位至第二段最后一句。该句提到“……年轻的科学家们带着恐惧的表情迅速就座”。“迅速就坐”即发生在飞行前；按常理推断，“a look of fear（害怕或恐惧的表情）”应该与紧张的心理或感受对应，也就是说，在飞行前他们感觉非常紧张，因此C选项正确。【干扰项排除】A选项“厌烦的”、B选项“渴望的”和D选项“不耐烦的；迫不及待的”均不符合原文内容，属于曲解原文。

options.A: He quickly climbed and then stopped the engines.
options.B: He climbed and them made the plane fall slowly.
options.C: He took off normally and then cut the engines for 20 seconds.
options.D: He climbed and then made the plane turn over.

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】What did the pilot do with the plane after it took off? 飞机起飞后，飞行员对飞机做了什么？A. He quickly climbed and then stopped the engines. A. 他先让飞机迅速爬升，然后关掉了引擎。B. He climbed and then made the plane fall slowly. B. 他先让飞机爬升，然后让飞机慢慢地下降。C. He took off normally and then cut the engines for 20 seconds. C. 他正常起飞，然后关掉引擎20秒。D. He climbed and then made the plane turn over. D. 他先让飞机爬升，然后把飞机翻了过来。【考查点】事实细节题【解题思路】根据题干的信息可定位至第五段。该段主要描述了飞行员驾驶飞机起飞以及操纵飞机飞行的整个过程。本段第二句提到飞行员驾驶飞机呈45度持续上升（a 45-degree climb）大约20秒；第三句又提到引擎被关闭（the engines cut out）。A选项中的climbed和stopped the engines分别对应原文的a 45-degree climb和the engines cut out，quickly与原文的around 20 seconds相对应，由此可知，A选项符合题意。【干扰项排除】B选项“他先让飞机爬升，然后让飞机慢慢地下降”的后半句与原文不相符，属于曲解原文；C选项“他正常起飞，然后关掉引擎20秒”与原文“飞行员让飞机爬升45度，持续了大约20秒”不相符，属于曲解原文；D选项“他先让飞机爬升，然后把飞机翻了过来”的后半句与原文不相符，属于曲解原文。

options.A: see what conditions bare like in space
options.B: prepare the young scientists for future work in space
options.C: show the judges of the competition what they could do
options.D: make the teams try out their ideas

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】According to the passage, the purpose of being weightless was to ____. 根据这篇文章，失重的目的是____。A. see what conditions bare like in space A. 看看太空中的环境是什么样的B. prepare the young scientists for future work in space B. 让年轻的科学家为未来的太空工作做好准备C. show the judges of the competition what they could do C. 向比赛的评委展示他们可以做什么D. make the teams try out their ideas D. 让团队实验他们的想法【考查点】推理判断题【解题思路】根据题干的信息可定位至倒数第二段。该段首句提到：每一次飞行员关闭引擎，我们就会处于失重状态，然后一个新的小组就开始进行实验。之后分别描述了荷兰、德国和美国的参赛学生的实验想法及目的。结合第三段第二句“他们（各大洲的理工科学生）的挑战是在失重情况下进行各种极具想象力的实验”可以推断，失重的目的是让各学生团队试验自己的想法，故D选项正确。【干扰项排除】A、B、C选项均无法从原文中推断出来，属于无中生有。

options.A: encourage young people to take up science
options.B: describe the process of a scientific competition
options.C: show scientists what young people can do
options.D: report on a new scientific technique

sub_questions.analysis: 【选项释义】This passage was written to ____. 写这篇文章是为了____。A. encourage young people to take up science A. 鼓励年轻人学习科学B. describe the process of a scientific competition B. 描述一次科学竞赛的过程C. show scientists what young people can do C. 告诉科学家年轻人可以做什么D. report on a new scientific technique D. 报道一项新的科学技术【考查点】主旨大意题【解题思路】文章开头两段首先对飞机的外观和内部情况作了细致的描述，并交待了参与此次飞行的乘客；第三段简要介绍了此次飞行的背景，即应欧洲航天局的邀请，来自各大洲的理工科学生参加竞争比赛，挑战在失重状态下进行各种富有想象力的实验；第四至六段详细叙述了整个飞行的过程以及参赛学生所进行的实验；末段总结了参赛学生对这次奇特的飞行之旅的感受。综上所述，本文主要是围绕科学实验竞赛活动展开描述的，故B选项“描述一次科学竞赛的过程”符合题意。【干扰项排除】A、C、D选项均不符合全文内容，属于曲解原文。

questions.article:
The smooth landing of shuttle（航天飞机）Discovery ended a flight that was successful in almost every respect but one: the dislodging of a big chunk of foam, like the one that doomed the Columbia. This flight was supposed to vault the shuttle fleet back into space after a prolonged grounding for repairs. But given the repeat of the very problem that two years of retooling was supposed to resolve ,the verdict is necessarily mixed. （1）
Once again, the space agency has been forced to put off the flight until it can find a solution to the problem, and no one seems willing to guess how that may take.
The Discovery astronauts performed superbly during their two-week mission, and the shuttle looked better than ever in some respects. （2）
Space officials were justifiably happy that so much had gone well, despite daily worries over possible risks. the flight clearly achieved its prime objectives.
The astronauts transferred tons of cargo to the international space station, which has been limping along overhead with a reduced crew and limited supplies carried up on smaller Russian spacecraft. （3）
They replaced a broken device .repaired another and carted away a load of rubbish that had been left on the station,  showing the shuttle can bring full loads back down from space.
This was the most scrutinized shuttle flight ever. with the vehicle undergoing close inspection while still in orbit. （4）
New sensing and photographic equipment to look for potentially dangerous damage to the sensitive external skin proved valuable.
A new back flip maneuver allowed station astronauts to photograph the shuttle‘s underbelly, and an extra-long robotic arm enabled astronauts see parts of the shuttle that were previously out of sight.
（5）
The flood of images and the openness in discussing its uncertainties about potential hazards sometimes made it appear that the shuttle was about to fall apart,In the end the damage was clearly tolerable.
A much-touted spacewalk to repair the shuttle's skin the first of its kind moved an astronaut close enough to pluck out some protruding material with his hand Preliminary evidence indicates that Discovery has far fewer nicks and gouges than shuttles on previous flights, perhaps showing that improvements to reduce the shedding of debris from the external fuel tank have had some success.

In this section, you are asked to write an essay based on the following diagram. Describe the diagram and analyze the possible causes. You should write at least 150 words on ANSWER SHEET 2.
工程硕士 Master of Engineering
